Ignurso.publicNotes:
Adventurers exploring active geothermal areas might encounter one of the extreme habitat's most unique creatures: ignursos. These ursine burrowers are partially armored with scales and a fearsome horn. They're sedentary by nature, but when disturbed, their rage combines the ferocity of a bear mauling with the heat of a pyroclastic flow.

Ignursos are specially adapted to survive in extremely hot climates but thrive anywhere warm. Those that inhabit less hospitable planets tend to stay underground and hibernate during the coldest parts of the year.

Scavengers by nature, ignursos can't take their next meal for granted. However, these clever creatures can manipulate their environment to trap prey. Ignursos have been observed creating large networks of burrows right before volcanic eruptions, seemingly because they understand lava flows will follow this path of least resistance. These tunnels turn to rivers of red-hot lava perfectly positioned to strand fleeing wildlife. Ignursos then return for their spoils after the worst of the eruption has passed.

Ignursos have a significant impact on volcanic ecosystems. Burrowing ignursos break up solid rock—often freshly deposited after an eruption—kick-starting the process of soil formation that's necessary for the return of plants. Some dig too close to underground magma chambers, turning their tunnels into volcanic vents that release lava and gases. In this way, they make explosive, damaging eruptions much less likely and provide a moderating force on a harsh environment.

Warpstitcher.name: Warpstitcher

Truespeech (constant).name: Truespeech (constant)
Truespeech (constant).description:
无论什么语言，目标都可以理解其所有的词语，并且向其他生物用该语言交流。当处于一群各异的生物中时，每次目标将要说话，他可以选择一个生物，并使用一种该生物理解的语言来讲述，即使该目标不知道这语言具体是什么。

升阶（7环级） 持续时间为8小时。

Resist Reality.name: Resist Reality
Resist Reality.description:
Trigger The warpstitcher would take damage

Effect The warpstitcher gains resistance 12 to the triggering damage type until the start of their next turn. The resistance applies to the triggering damage.

Fold Reality.name: Fold Reality
Fold Reality.description: The warpstitcher collapses the space between themself and another location. They choose a 10-foot square within 120 feet. The warpstitcher then Strides, treating the chosen area as adjacent to them.

Threads of Quintessence.name: Threads of Quintessence
Threads of Quintessence.description: The warpstitcher's claws are laced with strands of quintessence they force into victims' wounds. This functions as Grab, or Improved Grab on a critical hit.

Warp Suture.name: Warp Suture
Warp Suture.description:
Requirements The warpstitcher has a creature @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}

Effect The warpstitcher sews the creature into themself. The creature is pulled into the warpstitcher's space and moves with the warpstitcher. It becomes restrained and takes @Damage[3d12[force]] damage at the end of each of its turns until freed. Additionally, it takes @Damage[1d12[force]] damage whenever the warpstitcher uses Resist Reality (@Check[will|dc:34|basic] save). The Escape DC is 34 ([[/act escape dc=34]]), or the sutures can be severed (AC 25, Hardness 8, HP 30, weakness to slashing 5), freeing the creature. The warpstitcher can have up to 12 Bulk of creatures sewn into their body. The body of a creature that dies while sutured is absorbed into the warpstitcher's body and destroyed [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds} later.

Warpstitcher.publicNotes:
Warpstitchers are self-proclaimed guardians of existence, holding the belief that they were created by the gods to sew together the space between alternate realities. These horrifying aberrations relentlessly hunt anything they suspect abuses magic or technology to change the fabric of the multiverse. Once they find their quarry, they wait only long enough to confirm their suspicions before attacking. Potential threats—a neophyte spellcaster trying their first cantrips, an academic theorist exploring interstellar travel—instead earn a telepathic warning of what awaits should they pursue their efforts too far. Everything other than threats (potential or otherwise) is a possible tool, a target for warpstitcher lies and coercion.

Their mere appearance is fuel for nightmares, with insectile limbs supporting a massive, bulbous torso. From their back spring five multi-segmented limbs ending in wicked claws threaded with the very stuff of reality that they use to skewer their prey, who they then sew into their bodies. Such unfortunate creatures quickly dissolve, possibly sustaining the warpstitchers, though whether that's through their flesh, thoughts, or alternate reality potential is unknown. Those few who survive are plagued by disturbing, haunting visions; a smaller percentage instead see visions of happier possible lives, gaining peace and sometimes unprecedented witchwarper powers.

Deprimogon.name: Deprimogon

Diabolic Pact (DC 27).name: Diabolic Pact (DC 27)
Diabolic Pact (DC 27).description:
你恳请更强大的魔鬼，请求他派遣一些手下受你调遣。若你的仪式成功，魔鬼会根据自身的选择进行派遣，这可能是1个2级或更低等级的魔鬼、2个0级或更低等级的魔鬼、或者3个-1级或更低等级的魔鬼。

大成功 被派来的魔鬼会为你服务[[/gmr 1d4 #Weeks]]{1d4周}。

成功 被派来的魔鬼会为你服务[[/gmr 1d4 #Days]]{1d4天}。

失败 你的请求被拒绝了。

大失败 你的请求不仅被拒绝了，且强大的魔鬼会将自身的不满告知你的主人。

升阶（+1） 每个选项中为你派遣的魔鬼等级增加2级。

Masked Emotions.name: Masked Emotions
Masked Emotions.description: The deprimogon loses their immunity to emotion and mental effects if they aren't wearing a mask. In addition, an unmasked silencer devil uses the outcome one degree of success worse than they roll on saving throws against mental effects. A deprimogon's mask can be Disarmed as if it were a held item.

Evoke Paranoia.name: Evoke Paranoia
Evoke Paranoia.description:
The deprimogon infuses their weapons with paranoia and mistrust, instilling doubt in their victims with every attack. A creature damaged by a deprimogon's Strikes must attempt a @Check[will|dc:27] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune to the deprimogon's evoke paranoia for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune to the deprimogon's evoke paranoia for 1 round.

Failure Until the beginning of the deprimogon's next turn, the creature believes everyone it sees is a potential threat. It becomes unfriendly to all creatures to which it wasn't already hostile, even those who were previously allies. It treats no one as an ally.

Critical Failure As failure, except the target believes that everyone it sees is a mortal enemy. It uses its reactions and free actions against everyone, regardless of whether they were previously its allies, as determined by the GM. It otherwise acts as rationally as it normally does and likely prefers to attack creatures that are actively attacking or hindering it over those leaving it alone.

Infernal Disgrace.name: Infernal Disgrace
Infernal Disgrace.description: The deprimogon's Strikes count as magical and unholy.

Silent Prison.name: Silent Prison
Silent Prison.description: The deprimogon casts a 6th-rank @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.7Iela4GgVeO3LfAo]{Wall of Force} that dampens sound. Any creature that moves and begins its turn adjacent to the wall must attempt a @Check[will|dc:27] save. On a failure, the creature can't speak and can't use any actions and effects with the sonic or auditory traits while adjacent to the wall; this also prevents the creature from casting spells, with the exception of subtle spells.

Deprimogon.publicNotes:
Deprimogons stalk hidden tunnels, shadowed alleys, and looming towers of settlements whose governments have made infernal pacts as they silently monitor citizens for any signs of sedition. Agitators or freedom fighters are harassed or chased down and trapped by these devils before disappearing forever. Deprimogons hide their faces behind sleek metallic masks that suppress their own emotions, blocking out any pleas, threats, or bargaining that might dissuade them from their mission. Deprimogons delight in summoning diabolic creatures from eras long forgotten to torment their prisoners.

In the shadows of space stations, halls of courtrooms, and offices of corporations, devils tempt mortals with promises of power and lay the groundwork for their conquest. A devil's pacts can vary from elaborate contracts to quick promises, but they all end the same way: a mortal soul condemned, and Hell's forces bolstered. From the open rule of Malfane, the unseen control of Embroi, and hidden pacts with souls across the galaxy, devils continue their relentless work to spread tyranny and force the universe to resemble the hellish bureaucracy of their home.

Dredgelit.name: Dredgelit

Fan Club.name: Fan Club
Fan Club.description: A dredgelit maintains a bond with up to six other willing creatures. Creatures in the fan club always know the general distance and direction toward other bonded creatures, any conditions affecting them, and if they're @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yZRUzMqrMmfLu0V1]{Dying}, or dead. Creatures in the fan club can always communicate telepathically while on the same plane of existence.

Phantasmal Fans.name: Phantasmal Fans
Phantasmal Fans.description: 20 feet. A rowdy spectral crowd always surrounds the dredgelit. The dredgelit has concealment against all creatures, and the crowd counts as difficult terrain.

Alluring Debut.name: Alluring Debut
Alluring Debut.description:
Frequency once every 10 minutes

Effect The dredgelit attempts a Performance check against the Will DC of a single creature within 30 feet, recreating an iconic show.

Critical Success The creature becomes part of their fan club for 24 hours, even if it's unwilling.

Success As critical success, but only for 1 hour.

Failure The creature is unaffected.

Critical Failure The creature is unaffected, and the dredgelit is so embarrassed by their failure to perform that they take @Damage[2d8[mental]] damage.

Parasocial Leech.name: Parasocial Leech
Parasocial Leech.description:
Frequency once per hour

Effect The dredgelit leeches vitality from a creature in their fan club. The dredgelit attempts a Performance check against the Will DC of the creature. The creature takes @Damage[6d6[void]] damage, and the dredgelit regains @Damage[10[healing]] Hit Points.

Critical Success The target takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} for 1 round.

Success The target takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} and immobilized for 1 round.

Failure The target is unaffected.

Critical Failure The target is unaffected. The dredgelit's abysmal performance results in them taking @Damage[2d8[mental]] damage instead of regaining Hit Points.

Dredgelit.publicNotes:
Dredgelits linger as flickers on the silver screen or neon silhouettes glimpsed backstage. They seethe in shortwave static and stare out from fading gravure tableaux. Dredgelits are the undead specters of deceased media megastars who haunt the venues of their former glories and crave the attention of forgetful fans.

Most dredgelits live in a fantasy version of their former lives, but once their delusions are shattered, a burning desire to recapture their lost fame drives them. At this point, they become unpredictable, and even the smallest perceived slight can lead to a murderous fit of rage.

Dredgelits embody their former stage personas. These decaying debutantes ensnare admirers into their eternal fan clubs, blissfully unaware of the life-draining consequences they have upon new admirers. Dredgelit seek sacrificial fans, luring in the unwary and draining their vitality to stay famous and fabulous forever.

Emganat.name: Emganat

Motion Sense.name: Motion Sense
Motion Sense.description: An emganat can sense nearby creatures through vibrations in air or water caused by movement as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

Colony Mind.name: Colony Mind
Colony Mind.description: 30 feet. All allies with colony mind within the listed range of each other are in constant telepathic communication and are aware of all threats any individual knows about. While within range of at least one ally with colony mind, when a creature rolls a success on a save against a mental effect, it gets a critical success instead.

Burrowing Reveal.name: Burrowing Reveal
Burrowing Reveal.description:
Requirements Initiative hasn't yet been rolled, and the emganat is burrowed

Trigger A creature enters the range of the emganat's motion sense

Effect The emganat notices the creature and Burrows before it rolls initiative. This movement must cause the emganat to emerge from the ground.

Coordinated Digestion.name: Coordinated Digestion
Coordinated Digestion.description: If the emganat successfully Strikes a creature with its acidic mouth cannon and that creature was already taking persistent acid damage, increase the damage dice of the persistent damage by one step (to a maximum of d8s).

Emganat.publicNotes:
Emganats were first encountered during the Krethiskar subcolony's sudden emergence and the first attacks on Szandite Collective space. These wolf-sized components can burrow beneath the ground, and they move at great speeds to reach a location where they can set up an ambush. Then, once they detect a threat, the emganats spring forth from their burrowed positions and lay into their foes with an acidic cannon located in their mouths. The more emganats that unleash their bio-acid on foes, the faster the substance dissolves their targets in an agonizing display.

Developing far out in the Vast from an insectile species known as kucharns, the Swarm is considered the greatest threat in existence for several cultures. Composed of countless subcolonies, each consisting of myriad component subspecies, the Swarm is a vast host that acts in psychic union for the purpose of consuming all matter across the cosmos. Most frightening of all is the Swarm's ability to assimilate the knowledge and DNA of those they consume. Thanks to its pre-Gap development of star travel, the Swarm had already consumed innumerable systems before history short-circuited, and the Swarm's pace only accelerated once it received access to the Signal and the hyperspace dimension known as the Drift.

Worlds touched by the Swarm are drained of their natural resources and rendered barren places known as huskworlds. What outsiders consider to be "Swarm space" is little more than a swathe of lifeless worlds that the Swarm have consumed in its reckless advance. The Swarm so thoroughly strips these worlds that surveyors struggle to find even trace amounts of inorganic resources, such as rare minerals and water. Few of these huskworlds have been thoroughly explored, as oftentimes exploration awakens inert drones, theorized to have been left behind in case the devoured system was ever colonized and repopulated. Pockets of Swarm activity exist well beyond this region of space, as the Swarm sent out countless subcolonies in the ages before the Gap, and even now, only some of them have reached their intended destinations.

The Swarm is perhaps most known in the current era for two important facts. First, its assault on the Pact Worlds and the Veskarium led to the cessation of hostilities and tenuous alliance between the two powers. Second, the Swarm is the direct ancestor of the shirren species. Having broken away sometime during the Gap, shirrens found succor with the deity Hylax, who was believed to have evolved from a pre-Swarm kucharn and who turned her back on the ravenous entity the Swarm has evolved into.

In recent years, the Swarm had been seen as little more than a distant boogeyman with no activities save for classified actions in the Suskillon system in the Vast. However, the recent discovery of the missing Szandite world of Krethiskar inadvertently awoke a dormant subcolony that had been responsible for the world's consumption. Since then, the newly dubbed Krethiskar subcolony has become a scourge of Near Space, engaged in an active invasion of Szandite Collective worlds and poised to spill over into other powers like the Veskarium and Pact Worlds. This rediscovered subcolony has begun rapid evolution of its components and remains cut off from the wider Swarm, but it still represents a clear danger to Near Space.

Terror of Ukulam.name: Terror of Ukulam

Cataclysm.name: 大灾变 Cataclysm
Cataclysm.description:
你唤动难以想象的足以毁天灭地的大灾难的力量，从每种灾难中取一小块，并将它们组合在一起形成可怕的强力攻击。后续效果影响区域中的所有生物。在决定大灾变的伤害时，区域内的生物的抗力视为低10点。每个生物必须尝试一次基础反射豁免，对所有六种伤害类型生效。

  • 溶解肉体的酸雨造成3d10酸伤害。
  • 咆哮的地震击打地面上的生物，造成3d10钝击伤害。
  • 爆发的寒冰寒风暴造成3d10寒冷伤害。
  • 可怕的闪电撕裂区域，造成3d10闪电伤害。
  • 爆裂的狂风席卷天空，对在该区域飞行的生物造成3d10钝击伤害。
  • 突然的海啸横扫区域中的生物，造成3d10钝击伤害，并具有水特征（对于在该区域游泳的生物翻倍）。
  • 猛烈的野火如炼狱般烧过，造成3d10火焰伤害。

Indestructibility.name: 无敌咒 Indestructibility
Indestructibility.description: 你把自己与因果割裂。在法术持续时间内，你免疫任何对你有害的效果或者伤害，除了由神器，神力，以及类似的强力来源造成的效果。你仍然可以受到任何你愿意受到的效果的影响。

Atomic Blast.name: Atomic Blast
Atomic Blast.description:
You magically split an atom, creating an explosion with radioactive fallout. All creatures in the area take 5d12 bludgeoning damage and 5d12 fire damage, and they're exposed to extreme radiation that uses your spellcasting DC as the @Check[fortitude]{Fortitude DC} for radiation sickness incurred by exposure to this radiation. The onset and frequency of this magical radiation is 1 round, and the area remains radioactive for 10 minutes.

Critical Success The target is unaffected by the initial damage.

Success The target takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 minute.

Failure The target takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure As failure, and the target takes double damage.

Unfettered Movement (constant).name: Unfettered Movement (constant)
Unfettered Movement (constant).description:
你驱散可能影响一个生物的阻碍。当处于该法术效果中时，法术目标忽视会赋予其速度环境减值的效果。当法术目标尝试@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape}一个令其@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}、@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}或者@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}的效果时，它自动成功，除非该效果是魔法性的，并且其环级比行动无碍更高。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.CdAyAiMGESvgNQtz]{Spell Effect: Unfettered Movement}

Voice of Linked Minds.name: Voice of Linked Minds
Voice of Linked Minds.description: The Terror doesn't speak, but it can psychically communicate complex concepts with any creature within 200 feet that is part of a hive mind, husk mind, or similar shared mind. Other creatures that can communicate telepathically can somewhat understand the Terror, but their understanding is vague.

Ancient Bond.name: Ancient Bond
Ancient Bond.description: The Terror commands obedience from creatures on Castrovel, suggesting an ancient connection to the planet. Wild creatures native to Castrovel or its moon must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:45] saving throw when they first come within 200 feet of it on a given day; on a failure, they improve their attitude toward the Terror to friendly. Creatures that become friendly typically approach the Terror, resulting in a painful death by radiation sickness.

Radioactive Death.name: Radioactive Death
Radioactive Death.description: When the Terror of Ukulam dies, it explodes in a radioactive blast that instantly vaporizes all creatures and objects within 30 miles and deals @Damage[45d12[fire]] damage to all creatures, objects, and structures in a 150-mile-radius emanation with a @Check[fortitude|dc:45|basic] save. Creatures in the area are also exposed to terrible radiation sickness. The terrain and any bodies of water (or suitable portions of larger bodies, such as oceans, at the GM's discretion) become terribly radioactive for a minimum of 50 years, after which time they slowly become less radioactive, using less severe degrees of radiation from GM Core at the GM's discretion.

Terribly Radioactive.name: Terribly Radioactive
Terribly Radioactive.description: 120 feet. The Terror spreads radiation everywhere it goes. Non-creature plants near the Terror quickly wither and die. Any creature that begins its turn within the aura is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 3} and is exposed to terrible radiation sickness each round.

Induce Frenzy.name: Induce Frenzy
Induce Frenzy.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect The Terror stirs creatures in hive minds into a frenzy. Each creature within 200 feet that's part of a hive mind gains the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} condition for 1 minute, and it can use the extra action each round only to Stride or Strike.

Terrible Radiation Sickness.name: Terrible Radiation Sickness
Terrible Radiation Sickness.description:
This radiation causes unimaginably painful burns, unrelenting nausea, and death

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:45]

Onset 1 hour

Stage 1 @Damage[13d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (1 hour)

Stage 2 @Damage[14d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} (1 hour)

Stage 3 @Damage[15d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 3} (1 hour)

Stage 4 death

Toxic Touch.name: 剧毒之触 Toxic Touch
Toxic Touch.description: Creatures struck by the Terror's sludge tendril gain weakness 10 to poison and take a –2 status penalty to Fortitude saves for 1 round.

Undulating Wrath.name: Undulating Wrath
Undulating Wrath.description: The Terror Strikes with its sludge tendrils six times.

Vorpal Eye Beam.name: Vorpal Eye Beam
Vorpal Eye Beam.description: The Terror pulls back a lid of oozy "skin" to reveal its only anatomical feature—an enormous dark purple eye—firing an invisible particle beam along the path of its gaze. The beam is 10 feet wide and 120 feet long. Creatures in the area take @Damage[18d8[slashing]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:45|basic] save). Creatures that critically fail their save are instantly decapitated. This kills any creature except ones that don't require a head to live. For creatures with multiple heads, this usually kills the creature only if its last head is severed. The Terror can't use Vorpal Eye Beam again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Vorpal Eye Beam]]{1d4 rounds}. During this time, it loses immunity to critical hits and precision damage.

Terror of Ukulam.publicNotes:
When a massive ooze-like being emerged from the polluted caverns of Castrovel's industrial moon, Elindrae, the planet's leaders made the mistake of assuming the ooze posed no threat to Castrovel itself. When the ooze destroyed several of the moon's shipyards, then launched itself through space to the remote reaches of Ukulam, they made the mistake of assuming they had time to quarantine the threat in that continent's remote wilderness. When its radiation began to spread, and long-dead elindrian cultists rose from their nest-necropolises beneath the Singing Range to worship the ooze, the planet's leaders finally understood they couldn't ignore it. They called it the Terror of Ukulam. They still don't understand quite what it is, why it exists, or what it wants.

The Terror is an enormous ooze-like thing, somewhat resembling layered flaps of oily dark green flesh. It conceals its only anatomical feature—a large purple eye—beneath its undulating surface. The creature spends most of its time floating in deep subterranean caverns or ocean trenches, remaining immobile for days at a time, then suddenly traversing a great distance, almost always in a straight line, before stopping again. These stops are seemingly random, but they've displaced settlements with increasing regularity. The vengeful worshippers who follow the Terror's path are almost as dangerous as the radiation it spreads, though their reasons for following it are as inscrutable as the Terror itself.

Thanks to the nature of atomic energy and especially atomic weapons, Castrovel's scientists have deduced that the radioactive Terror is likely to cause as much destruction in death as it has caused in life—or perhaps more. Even if the combined military might of Castrovel and its allies was sufficient to defeat the Terror, these scientists have warned that they need more time to study the Terror and assess the risks of destroying it outright. Of course, every day that passes is another day that more elindrians flock to the Terror, and yet more victims cross its destructive path.

Cyanoscum Bloom.name: Cyanoscum Bloom

Water Sense.name: Water Sense
Water Sense.description: A cyanoscum can sense the presence of water within 60 feet as an imprecise sense.

Toxic Exposure.name: Toxic Exposure
Toxic Exposure.description: Creatures inside the cyanoscum bloom's space are exposed to cyanoscum toxin. A creature can't reduce the poison below stage 1 while it shares a space with the cyanoscum bloom.

Cyanoscum Toxin.name: Cyanoscum Toxin
Cyanoscum Toxin.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:18]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[1d8[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} (1 round)

Frenzied Scratching.name: Frenzied Scratching
Frenzied Scratching.description: Each enemy in the cyanoscum bloom's space takes @Damage[3d6[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save).

Squish.name: Squish
Squish.description: A cyanoscum bloom can Squeeze through spaces that aren't airtight at half its Speed per round.

Cyanoscum Bloom.publicNotes:
While these wood elementals bear the adorable appearance of a rodent, their main bodies are unfortunately composed of toxin-producing algae. Cyanoscums thrive in any warm, wet environment. Cyanoscum blooms are easy to spot; from a distance, they look like blue-green meadows floating atop still waters or clusters of fuzzy, teal moss clinging to plants and rocks. An explorer moved by this majestic sight might venture too close to the water's edge or reach out to touch the soft, resplendent moss—inviting contact with these beautiful but poisonous creatures.

Elementals are energetic intrusions from the primal planes of existence. They take many forms, from mindless eddies in the wind or water to complex entities capable of wielding primal magic. The advent of space travel and the progress of technology have allowed even more living embodiments of the elements to manifest in the Universe.

Natural events like wildfires, solar storms, and floods can unleash hordes of unruly elementals on a planet or into a region of space. Sometimes, a gathering of powerful elementals causes a natural disaster on an epic scale, like an off-the-charts solar flare that fries a planet's power grid or a ceaseless storm swirling in a gas giant's atmosphere.

Finitrium Fatebender.name: Finitrium Fatebender

Glimpse Fate.name: Glimpse Fate
Glimpse Fate.description: A finitrium fatebender gains a +5 circumstance bonus when rolling Perception for initiative, as it acts on flashes of insight harvested from potential futures.

A Million Fates.name: A Million Fates
A Million Fates.description: 20 feet. A finitrium fatebender is surrounded by an aura of countless shifting potential futures—fragmented milliseconds of possibility all overlapping in a tumultuous blur. A creature that ends its turn in the aura must attempt a @Check[will|dc:33] save. If it fails, it's @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} for 1 round (or [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds} on a critical failure). A creature that critically succeeds becomes temporarily immune to the aura for 1 day.

Fated Outcome.name: Fated Outcome
Fated Outcome.description:
Frequency once per day

Trigger The finitrium fatebender fails or critically fails an attack roll or saving throw

Effect The finitrium fatebender rerolls the triggering check and takes the better result.

Tactical Prescience.name: Tactical Prescience
Tactical Prescience.description:
Frequency once per round

Trigger The finitrium fatebender would be hit by a Strike

Effect Having foreseen the incoming attack, the finitrium fatebender shifts position, gaining a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

Fate Wave.name: Fate Wave
Fate Wave.description: The finitrium fatebender releases a wave of twisted futures and shattered possibilities, dealing @Damage[16d6[mental]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:60] (@Check[will|dc:36|basic] save). The finitrium fatebender can't use Fate Wave again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Fate Wave]]{1d4 rounds}.

Foretell Doom.name: Foretell Doom
Foretell Doom.description: Any creature hit by the finitrium fatebender's fist or ray of twisted fate Strikes receives a vision of their impending death at the hands of the finitrium fatebender. It must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:36] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2}; on a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}. If the creature is already affected by foretold doom, it increases the appropriate stunned and stupefied values by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 4}. When the finitrium fatebender is destroyed, the creature has avoided this tragic fate and immediately recovers from its stupefied condition.

Inexorable March.name: Inexorable March
Inexorable March.description: The finitrium fatebender Strides up to its Speed, pushing back each creature whose space it moves into and damaging creatures that try to stop its movement. A creature can attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:40] save to bar the way, stopping the finitrium fatebender's movement on a success. On a critical success, the resisting creature takes no damage; otherwise, it's damaged as if hit by the finitrium fatebender's fist Strike.

Finitrium Fatebender.publicNotes: Finitrium fatebenders are powerful magical constructs created from refined finitrium, a rare starmetal with divinatory properties. Thanks to their ability to see—and react to—the future, finitrium fatebenders make exceptional guardians and formidable foes.

Glass Serpent.name: Glass Serpent

Ambush.name: Ambush
Ambush.description:
Trigger A creature passes within 20 feet of the glass serpent and hasn't detected it

Effect The glass serpent lunges out of hiding. It Strides directly toward the triggering creature and makes a jaws Strike against it. The target creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to this attack.

Ravenous Invisibility.name: Ravenous Invisibility
Ravenous Invisibility.description: The glass serpent is naturally invisible. When a glass serpent takes a hostile action of any kind, it is hidden instead of undetected until the start of its next turn, as parts of its body become momentarily tangible. A glass serpent that has swallowed a creature becomes visible for the next day as its meal is being digested. If the swallowed creature escapes or is otherwise removed, the glass serpent immediately returns to being invisible.

Concealing Coil.name: Concealing Coil
Concealing Coil.description:
Requirements The glass serpent's last action was a successful tail whip Strike

Effect The creature the glass serpent hit is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} from all creatures except the glass serpent until that creature moves out of the glass serpent's reach or until the start of the glass serpent's next turn.

Glass Serpent.publicNotes:
The great glass serpents of the barren planet of Eox are one of the Pact Worlds' most notorious apex predators. With an average length of almost 60 feet and a weight of about 5,000 pounds, the size and grace of glass serpents are only part of what makes them such efficient killers. Thanks to an innate invisibility provided by their unique scales, glass serpents remain outside of the visual spectrum of most creatures, allowing them to silently approach unsuspecting prey. Having evolved through Eox's numerous cataclysms, these serpents find sustenance from the rare bands of living creatures who visit the world, and in an odd turn, while they're consuming the living is when glass serpents are at their most vulnerable. The combination of acids and digestive fluids used in consuming a living creature also deprives the serpents of their invisibility, and canny glass serpent hunters actively tempt their quarry with animals to consume before attacking in force.

Glass serpents have made their way well beyond the confines of Eox, and some have even found an accepting ecosystem in the Diaspora's strange waterway, the River Between. The local sarcesians blame Eox for intentionally polluting their waterways with predators, though Eox denies all accusations of intentional meddling. Such debates have only intensified since the Diaspora was granted provisional Pact World status under the sarcesians' stewardship.

Cloud Ray.name: Cloud Ray

Explosive Death.name: Explosive Death
Explosive Death.description: If a cloud ray dies due to fire or electricity damage, its internal reservoirs of explosive gases and combustible fluids detonate in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] that deals @Damage[11d8[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save).

Lash Out.name: Lash Out
Lash Out.description: The cloud ray makes two consecutive tail lash Strikes at a –2 penalty against any targets within reach. These attacks count toward the cloud ray's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it makes all its attacks.

Natural Thruster.name: Natural Thruster
Natural Thruster.description:
Frequency once per hour

Effect Until the beginning of its next turn, the cloud ray gains a +80-foot status bonus to its fly Speed and deals an additional 1d10 slashing damage with its tail Strikes.

Stunning Lash.name: Stunning Lash
Stunning Lash.description: A creature that takes electricity damage from a cloud ray's tail Strike is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} on a critical hit), then becomes temporarily immune to stunning lash for 1 minute. Creatures with a weakness to electricity don't become temporarily immune.

Cloud Ray.publicNotes: Enormous cloud rays are apex predators on gas giants such as Bretheda and Liavara. Cloud rays are incredibly maneuverable and can fly through the atmosphere at speeds few can match, rendering them nearly inescapable when on the hunt.

Akashic Dragon (Young).name: Akashic Dragon (Young)

Interplanar Teleport (at will; self only.name: Interplanar Teleport (at will; self only; to and from Akashic Record and the Universe only)
Interplanar Teleport (at will; self only.description:
需求 你具有目标位面的位面密钥用于给法术定位

你和你的盟友跨越位面之间的屏障进行旅行。目标移动至其他位面，比如火元素位面、幽界、或者外层裂隙。你必须知晓目标位面的存在，而且需要使用由该位面的物质制作的魔法位面密钥来给法术定位。就如同跨界传送这个法术本身一样，大多数著名位面的位面密钥具有罕见稀有度，而鲜为人知的位面及半位面通常具有稀有、甚至有可能时独特稀有度的位面密钥。

该法术极其不精准，你会出现在目标之一（由你选择）上一次造访该位面时的位置周边，误差为1d20 x 25英里。若所有目标都是初次造访某个特定位面，你们就会出现在该位面的随机位置。跨界传送不会提供返回的手段，不过除非发生了什么另外情况，否则再次施放跨界传送能让你返回自身之前身处的位面。

Clairvoyance.name: 鹰眼术 Clairvoyance
Clairvoyance.description: 你在射程内创造出一个@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{隐形 Invisible}的悬浮眼睛（它甚至可以在你的视觉线或者效果线之外）。这只眼睛不能移动，但是你可以从该处看到所有方向，如同使用你通常的视觉感官。

Confusion.name: 困惑术 Confusion
Confusion.description:
你使用奇怪的能量波动扰乱了目标的思维，令其随机行动。法术效果由目标的意志豁免决定。你可以解消该法术。

大成功 目标不受影响。

成功 目标胡言乱语，并且获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{震慑 Stunned 1}。

失败 目标获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{困惑 Confused}1分钟。在其每个回合结束，它能尝试一次新豁免来结束困惑术。

大失败 目标困惑1分钟，并且没有提前结束的豁免。

升阶（8环级） 你能以至多10个生物为目标。

Akashic Download (at will).name: Akashic Download (at will)
Akashic Download (at will).description:
You execute a psychic search of the Akashic Record for information about a specific topic and magically transfer this data to your comm unit or computer. When you Cast this Spell, choose any skill in which you're trained that has the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} action.

Any time during the duration, you can consult your comm unit while attempting a Recall Knowledge check using your chosen skill. This check gains a +1 status bonus. Checking your comm unit is part of the action to Recall Knowledge. You must be holding the comm unit to do so. The comm unit instantly compiles and displays snippets and selections from various media relevant to the topic, which grants you the bonus. If you roll a critical failure on this check, you get a failure instead. If the check is successful and the subject is a creature, you gain additional information or context about the creature. Once you reference the comm unit, the spell ends. When the spell ends, your access to the Akashic Record ends, and all the information you downloaded returns to the Akashic Record.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.h1i8c47U0IOrMrHJ]{Spell Effect: Akashic Download}

Heightened (3rd) You can reference your comm unit three times before the spell ends.

Akashic Eye.name: Akashic Eye
Akashic Eye.description: Instead of using their own memories, an Akashic dragon can ascertain information from the Akashic Records. This gives it the Akashic Record Lore skill, which it can use to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} of any kind.

+2 Status to All Saves vs. Occult.name: +2 Status to All Saves vs. Occult

Akashic Interference.name: Akashic Interference
Akashic Interference.description:
90 feet. The swirl of constant uploads and downloads near the Akashic dragon make attempts to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} unpredictable. A creature other than an akashic dragon that enters or begins its turn in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:23|options:area-effect] save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to this dragon's Akashic interference for 1 minute.

Critical Success For 1 round, when the creature attempts to Recall Knowledge, it gains a +3 status bonus to its check.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure For 1 round, when the creature attempts to Recall Knowledge, it takes a –3 status penalty to its check.

Critical Failure As failure, but the effect lasts 1 minute.

Deflecting Lasers.name: Deflecting Lasers
Deflecting Lasers.description:
Trigger The dragon is critically hit by an attack

Effect The young Akashic dragon raises a web of deflecting lasers using the eyes embedded all over their body, gaining resistance 8 to all damage against the triggering attack and absorbing some of the blow's energy, recharging their Akashic Barrage. If the Akashic dragon has succeeded at a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} check against the creature in the last minute, they gain resistance 11 instead.

Akashic Adaptability.name: Akashic Adaptability
Akashic Adaptability.description: The Akashic dragon can activate magic and tech items with the Cast a Spell activation of any tradition as if the spells they contain were occult spells.

Akashic Barrage.name: Akashic Barrage
Akashic Barrage.description: The dragon condenses cutting truths and fiery revelations from the Akashic Record into sizzling laser fire that they project as a cone from their primary eye to assail their foes. This deals @Damage[10d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30] (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:stupefied] save). If a creature critically fails its Reflex save against Akashic Barrage and the Akashic dragon has succeeded at a check to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about the creature in the last minute, the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round. The dragon can't use Akashic Barrage again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Akashic Barrage]]{1d4 rounds}.

Draconic Frenzy.name: 龙之狂怒 Draconic Frenzy
Draconic Frenzy.description: The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.

Draconic Momentum.name: Draconic Momentum
Draconic Momentum.description: The dragon recharges their Akashic Barrage whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.

Records of the Enemy.name: Records of the Enemy
Records of the Enemy.description: The first time each encounter the Akashic dragon hits and deals damage to a creature with their jaws Strike, they receive information about the creature as if they'd succeeded at a check to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about that creature.

Akashic Dragon (Young).publicNotes: Though rarely encountered outside their crumbling, relic-filled lairs, Akashic dragons occasionally live in hubs of knowledge, such as bustling space stations or cities. These dragons, bestowed with a direct connection to the Akashic Record, are arrogant curators of knowledge, assuming (often correctly) that no other being could possibly have a deeper understanding of the universe's secrets. Akashic dragons spend extensive time in the Akashic Record, often emerging after what they perceive as many days while mere hours have passed for the rest of the galaxy.

Elimination-Class Assassin Robot.name: Elimination-Class Assassin Robot

Integrated Advanced Nano-edge Rapier.name: Integrated Advanced Nano-edge Rapier
Integrated Advanced Nano-edge Rapier.description:
Upgrades: 1

The edge of this precision-crafted foil is sharpened to the thickness of a single atom, using regenerating nanites to maintain itself after countless strikes.

Integrated Advanced Semi-Auto Pistol.name: Integrated Advanced Semi-Auto Pistol
Integrated Advanced Semi-Auto Pistol.description:
Upgrades: 1

This pistol automatically ejects used cartridges and loads new ammunition after every shot fired.

Accelerated Response.name: Accelerated Response
Accelerated Response.description:
Trigger The target of the elimination-class assassin robot's Target Tracking attempts a Strike against the robot

Effect The robot reads its target's movements, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering Strike.

Burn Protocol.name: Burn Protocol
Burn Protocol.description: The circuitry of an assassin robot self-destructs upon the robot's destruction, making tracing its owner extremely difficult. Add 10 to the DC of a Computers check to recover data from a destroyed assassin robot.

Holographic Camouflage.name: Holographic Camouflage
Holographic Camouflage.description:
Frequency once per hour

Effect The elimination-class assassin robot becomes invisible for 10 minutes. If the robot uses a hostile action or moves more than half its Speed in a single action, the robot becomes visible again after it completes that action.

Nanite Surge.name: Nanite Surge
Nanite Surge.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect The elimination-class assassin robot restores @Damage[45[healing]] Hit Points to one technological creature or item.

Retractable Weapons.name: Retractable Weapons
Retractable Weapons.description: The elimination-class assassin robot can Interact to draw or retract any of its integrated weapons as a free action. An observer must succeed at a @Check[perception|dc:40] check to notice a retracted weapon.

Sneak Attack.name: 偷袭 Sneak Attack
Sneak Attack.description: The elimination-class assassin robot's Strikes deal an additional 3d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

Target Tracking.name: Target Tracking
Target Tracking.description: The elimination-class assassin robot establishes a target lock on a creature it can see within 120 feet. The creature can't become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} against the robot or [[/act create-a-diversion]] against the robot. This ability lasts until the robot loses line of sight with the target or the target otherwise becomes hidden.

Elimination-Class Assassin Robot.publicNotes:
Elegant and deadly, elimination models rely upon their stealth fields to keep foes guessing and strike where least expected. Their advanced intelligence gives them the capacity to set up more complex ambushes as well as react to changes in the situation as needed and handle requests from the client. This could mean making the killing as subtle or as gruesome as possible, depending on the intent and message intended.

In some societies, machines automate many processes. It only makes sense, then, that some would attempt to automate the act of murder. Most manufacturers would never admit to creating such designs, forcing wealthy figures to go through hidden channels to acquire units from secret facilities or unscrupulous manufacturers. Regardless of their origins, these robots can be programmed with a target and set loose, the owner assured that either their target will be dead or will be unable to prove who tried to kill them thanks to the unit's fried circuitry.

Dust Manta.name: Dust Manta

Sandsense.name: Sandsense
Sandsense.description: The dust manta can feel the slightest disturbance in nearby sand, granting it the ability to sense other creatures moving through or on top of the same body of sand that it's in. In addition, the dust manta ignores @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealment} from sandstorms, and they're immune to being @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} by sand or other grit.

Burrowing Charge.name: Burrowing Charge
Burrowing Charge.description: The dust manta surges through sand to attack unwary prey. It Burrows up to twice its burrow Speed, then Leaps vertically up to 25 feet high, making a stinger Strike against a creature at any point during the jump (this lets it attack a creature within 35 feet of the surface). After the [[/act leap]], the dust manta falls to the ground but takes no damage from the fall if it lands on sand.

Disappear in Dust.name: Disappear in Dust
Disappear in Dust.description: The dust manta's sand-colored hide allows it to [[/act hide]] in sand or any other fine, similarly colored substance even without cover or concealment.

Dust Manta Toxin.name: Dust Manta Toxin
Dust Manta Toxin.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:24]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[2d4[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[3d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[3d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} (1 round)

Stage 4 @Damage[4d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 3} (1 round)

Stage 5 death, and the victim's body disintegrates into dust and can only be brought back via a wish ritual or similarly powerful magic

Spray Sand.name: Spray Sand
Spray Sand.description:
The dust manta sprays a cloud of sand from its gills in a @Template[type:burst|distance:30]. All creatures in the cloud become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}, and all creatures outside the cloud become concealed to creatures within it. The cloud lasts until the end of the dust manta's next turn. The dust manta can't use Spray Sand again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Spray Sand]]{1d4 rounds}. A non-dust manta creature that enters or starts its turn in the cloud must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:24] save.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 minute or until it spends an Interact action to wipe the dirt and grit from its eyes. Critical

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round, after which it's dazzled for 1 minute or until it spends an Interact action to wipe its eyes.

Dust Manta.publicNotes: Deadly stealth predators, dust mantas lurk just below the surface on desert worlds across the Universe. Despite a wingspan averaging 10 feet across and weighing 1 ton, dust mantas are nearly undetectable in their native habitat thanks to their pebbly, sand-colored hide. Like a ray through water, they can sense nearby vibrations and glide through sand flats and dunes to ambush unfortunate prey. Their wicked stinger injects a lethal toxin that breaks down flesh and bone, dissolving it into sand so filter-feeding dust mantas can strain particles of food from the resulting suspension.

Enraged Noquasar.name: Enraged Noquasar

Gravity Crash.name: Gravity Crash
Gravity Crash.description: A creature critically hit by an enraged noquasar's graviton flare is knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If the target is flying, it falls up to 30 feet.

Ionized Plasma Blas.name: Ionized Plasma Blas
Ionized Plasma Blas.description: The noquasar spark unleashes plasma in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] or @Template[type:line|distance:30]. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[3d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save). A creature that critically fails its save also takes @Damage[1d8[persistent,electricity]|options:area-damage] damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. The noquasar spark can't use Ionized Plasma Blast again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Ionized Plasma Blast]]{1d4 rounds}.

Enraged Noquasar.publicNotes:
A noquasar's rage increases as its torture extends over many years.

When a solarian perishes while out of sync with the cycle of stars, their soul might become entangled with their solar mote. Extreme cosmic forces act on the soul as it attempts to reach the River of Souls, stretching it infinitely thin for all of eternity.

Assembly Ooze.name: Assembly Ooze

Techsense.name: Techsense
Techsense.description: An assembly ooze can detect creatures and objects with the tech trait as a precise sense within the listed range.

Assemble.name: Assemble
Assemble.description: Over the course of 1 minute, the assembly ooze crafts a random piece of gear (rolling a [[/r 1d6]]: 1–armor, 2–shield, 3–weapon, 4–armor upgrade, 5–weapon upgrade, or 6–tech item) using the UPBs stored inside of it. An assembly ooze can craft items with a cost up to the UPBs stored, but with an item level and Bulk of no greater than 4 each.

Disassemble.name: Disassemble
Disassemble.description: The assembly ooze engulfs an adjacent, unattended piece of gear of no more than 4 Bulk, deconstructing it into its component parts. The ooze gains a number of UPBs equal to the item's price in credits. An assembly ooze can hold up to 1,000 UPBs at a time.

Sticky Disarm.name: Sticky Disarm
Sticky Disarm.description:
Requirements The assembly ooze's last action was a successful pseudopod Strike

Effect The ooze attempts to [[/act disarm options=sticky-disarm]] the creature it hit. It gains a +4 status bonus to the Athletics check. This attempt neither applies nor counts toward the ooze's multiple attack penalty.

Assembly Ooze.publicNotes:
The assembly ooze is believed to have been created by accident when a manufacturing company attempted to automate an object printer as a cost cutting measure. The original creature has long since left containment in the Pact Worlds, replicating itself across the cosmos. While often considered a nuisance akin to pests of yore, an assembly ooze left unchecked on a starship or space station can eventually turn into an existential threat.

An assembly ooze typically takes the form of an amorphous metallic cube. It has an outer shell composed of a liquid polycarbonate slurry that encases a core of replicating nanobots. The ooze's color can change based on the materials it breaks down, but it's most often a metallic amalgamation of brown or gray. To survive, the ooze attempts to constantly disassemble technological items in following its programmed subroutines then manufacture something new. The nanites within an assembly ooze do have a drive for reproduction, splitting in a process similar to cellular mitosis when the ooze absorbs enough material in a short period of time. Assembly oozes generally don't assault those not carrying the technological items they require as fuel but do lash out to protect themselves.

Oozes of many types lurk in dark and abandoned places across the galaxy, emerging only to satiate their appetites and threaten adventurers, explorers, and hapless workers alike.

Observer-Class Security Robot.name: Observer-Class Security Robot

Intergrated Commercial Pulsecaster Pisto.name: Intergrated Commercial Pulsecaster Pistol
Intergrated Commercial Pulsecaster Pisto.description:
Upgrades: 1

This imposing pistol fires a jolt of paralyzing electricity from its barrel. Its remaining charges are visible as glowing lines of energy radiating down its body. Pulsecaster pistols are often used by bounty hunters and law enforcement who need to keep their targets alive.

Exigency.name: Exigency
Exigency.description:
Frequency once every 10 minutes

Trigger A creature targets the security robot with an attack

Effect The robot overclocks its processing power to attempt to avoid an attack, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to its AC against the triggering attack.

Observer-Class Security Robot.publicNotes:
Programmed to patrol small areas, an observer-class security robot is akin to a watch dog, but with a built-in gun. Some manufacturers design these robots with canine forms, and their capability to fly can surprise would-be burglars who were expecting a more terrestrial defense.

Security robots are ubiquitous throughout industrialized space and have major presences in both the Pact Worlds and Veskarium, employed and customized by corporations and private citizens alike.

While most soldier robot models are built to be as destructive as possible, and elimination robots are designed to kill, security robots usually have fail-safes programmed into their behavior to prevent them from causing any more violence than is necessary to perform their designated security function. This makes them especially useful in cities, where unintended destruction might harm bystanders or cause legal issues, and even on starships, where a stray laser blast might cause serious damage to vital starship systems.

Gwereiad.name: Gwereiad

Gravity Field.name: Gravity Field
Gravity Field.description:
Bending the laws of the Universe to your will, you increase the forces of gravity in an area. All flying creatures in the area descend 40 feet. The entire area is greater difficult terrain for Flying and Climbing creatures, and difficult terrain for creatures on the ground. Any creature pulled to the ground by a gravity field takes bludgeoning damage as though it had fallen.

Gravity in the area increases by one step (for example, zero-g becomes low gravity, low gravity becomes standard, standard gravity becomes high gravity, and high gravity becomes extreme gravity).

Electrotether.name: Electrotether
Electrotether.description:
You supercharge the targets' electromagnetism and pull them with a tether of lightning. Make a ranged spell attack against each target. If you attack two targets, both attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but it doesn't increase until after both attacks. The targets each take 4d8 electricity damage and are pulled 5 feet closer to you, or 10 feet closer on a critical hit. If both attacks hit, you can choose to move the creatures closer to one another instead of closer to you. Moving a target into a barrier, creature, or obstacle causes the target to stop before entering that space.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

Personal Gravity (Constant).name: Personal Gravity (Constant)
Personal Gravity (Constant).description: You alter gravity's effects on you. You treat gravity as if it were your choice of one step higher or lower (for example, if you were in an area of low gravity, you could choose either standard gravity or zero-g). While the spell is active, you reduce the distance of any forced movement that would affect you by 5 feet (minimum 0 feet), and you ignore being clumsy and untethered because of gravity effects on you. You can Dismiss the spell.

Fear.name: 恐惧术 Fear
Fear.description:
你将恐惧植入目标；它必须尝试一次意志豁免。

大成功 目标不受影响。

成功 目标获得 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{惊惧 Frightened 1}.

失败 目标获得 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{惊惧 Frightened 2}.

大失败 目标获得 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{惊惧 Frightened 3} 并且 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{逃跑 Fleeing} 1轮.

升阶（3环级） 你能以至多五个生物为目标。

Glow Up.name: Glow Up
Glow Up.description:
You create an illusion that causes the target to appear more charming and genteel to observers. The spell accentuates the target's appearance, clothing, voice, and mannerisms according to what an observer finds appealing. Held items are unaffected, and any worn item removed from the creature returns to its true appearance.

The target gains a +1 status bonus to Diplomacy checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.OX4fy22hQgUHDr0q]{Make an Impression} and lets the target add its level to Diplomacy checks if untrained. In addition, the target can attempt a Diplomacy check to Gather Information in only 10 minutes. You can Dismiss this spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.hOp5ybOzyJlECU6B]{Spell Effect: Glow Up}

Heightened (+2) The bonus increases by 1, to a maximum of +4 at 7th rank.

Stumble.name: Stumble
Stumble.description:
A burst of microgravity causes a tremor around the target, pulling it toward the ground. The target must attempt a Reflex save.

Failure The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} for 1 round. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 10 feet downward.

Critical Failure The target falls @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 20 feet downward.

Heightened (+1) If the target falls, it falls an additional 5 feet on a failure and 10 feet on a critical failure.

Sense Transponder.name: Sense Transponder
Sense Transponder.description: While on their bonded celestial body, a nymph can sense approaching starships within a day's travel from the celestial body. This doesn't give them any information about the ship, its occupants, or its exact position. The nymph can send a message to the ship as if using a personal comm unit.

Black Hole-Dependent.name: Black Hole-Dependent
Black Hole-Dependent.description: A gwereiad is mystically bonded to a single black hole. If they're away from their bonded black hole for more than 24 hours, they become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} and must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:29] save each additional 24 hours or increase their drained value by 1. A gwereiad can perform a 24-hour ritual to bond themself to a new black hole.

Singularity Field.name: Singularity Field
Singularity Field.description: 30 feet. The area around a gwereiad inexorably draws things toward its center. Creatures entering or starting their turns inside the aura must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:29] save or be pulled 5 feet toward the gwereiad (10 feet on a critical failure).

Gravitic Crush.name: Gravitic Crush
Gravitic Crush.description:
The gwereiad summons a gravitational collapse around a creature within 30 feet, dealing @Damage[6d6[bludgeoning]] damage. The affected creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:29] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature's body crumples under the gravity, and it takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} by the gravitational collapse. It takes @Damage[2d6[persistent,bludgeoning]] damage until it [[/act escape dc=29]]{Escapes}.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature takes double damage and the persistent damage is 4d6.

Gwereiad.publicNotes:
Gwereiads are aloof and enigmatic nymphs who keep to themselves, nestling deep inside the phenomena known as black holes. Whether their solitary nature is an active preference or simply a consequence of their homes is up for debate, as few creatures can brave the gravitational forces of a black hole long enough to make contact with gwereiads. They're rarely hostile unless provoked and occasionally even rescue starships at risk of being devoured by their homes.

Gwereiads adore art and music, though their tastes tend toward the strange and avant-garde. While most nymphs aren't overtly religious, gwereiads devote themselves to esoteric spirituality focused on space-time.

Nymphs are beautiful fey who appear as humanoids with elven features. They generally take female forms, but nymphs of all genders and presentations exist. While many nymphs live on planets and act as protectors of natural ponds, forests, caves, and cliffsides, other nymphs—often called space nymphs—are known for their association with celestial bodies. Like their planet-bound kin, space nymphs guard their bonded territories from exploitation or harm.

As admirers of the arts, nymphs are especially friendly toward creatives, and offering to depict a nymph in story, picture, or song is a sure way to win their favor—unless the nymph doesn't like the final result and lashes out at the artist for insulting them with a mediocre product. Vidcasting and livestreaming are popular pastimes among more sociable nymphs, whether to show off their remote environments or find audiences for performances. Some nymphs entice movie crews to film in their territories, with two provisos: first, that the production not damage the environment, and second, that the nymph receive a prominent role in the film.

Unless their territories are threatened, most space nymphs are friendly or indifferent to mortals, but they have long memories. Anyone visiting a space nymph's territory would be wise to research that nymph's grudges, past encounters with mortals, or any recent events in the area that might color the nymph's perspective of those traveling near their home.

A mortal who demonstrates the right attitude and abilities, such as by protecting a stellar body from destruction or creating an especially beautiful work of art, might draw a space nymph's attention. From time to time, space nymphs will offer their services as mentors to solarians or mystics, though they seldom seek pupils on their own. Space nymphs rarely enter into more than one such arrangement at a time and typically only make commitments that promise to last decades or centuries, so they're extremely selective in their choice of mentee.

Zalyop.name: Zalyop

Explosive Burst.name: Explosive Burst
Explosive Burst.description:
Trigger The zalyop is reduced to 0 Hit Points by physical or fire damage

Effect The zalyop explodes, dealing @Damage[4d6[sonic]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in a @Template[type:burst|distance:20] centered on itself (@Check[fortitude|dc:18|basic] save). This also triggers a Befuddling Cloud, even if it's been used in the last hour.

Befuddling Cloud.name: Befuddling Cloud
Befuddling Cloud.description:
Frequency once per hour

Effect The zalyop expels a gaseous cloud in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30]. Creatures within the cloud must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:18] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round (or 1 minute on a critical failure). Confused creatures that are also engulfed by the zalyop don't receive flat checks to end the confused condition.

Zalyop.publicNotes:
The jet streams of the gas giant Bretheda are filled with large, bulbous silhouettes called zalyops, sometimes referred to as balloon plants. Gas-filled and hardy, they're buffeted around by the planet's eternal storms. It's common for a cascade of these plants to be found trailing in the wake of an oma or plastered against one of the many spacecrafts and aircrafts frequenting the gas giant.

The sound of zalyops bouncing against a metal hull at high velocity can be a humorous one, but travelers should be careful not to assume these plants are harmless. Zalyops are known to expel high-speed organic shrapnel by reflex. Additionally, their confusion-inducing gaseous waste easily spreads across the atmosphere and can be caught in a ship's air-intake valve.

Zalyops can also cause chaos at ground level. When they float into small settlements, flocks of them will descend on small creatures, which they engulf for nutrients. The widespread befuddling clouds caused by such incursions are a dangerous nuisance. Pedestrians and pets alike must take care during such events to stay inside and simply wait the invasive balloons out.

Pact Worlds chemists have developed (and patented) Balyoon, an anesthetic drug from zalyop gases. In addition to being difficult to obtain in the first place, the drug is highly unstable until refined in the laboratory, and more than one starship has been lost when its crew failed to properly store the gases they just harvested from a zalyop.

Balyoon also plays a role in Eoxian soap operas and other dramatic vidshows across the Pact Worlds. It's used as a plot device in a myriad of ways, such as a villain contriving to discredit a hero or for a family member to suddenly lose their mind or memory in a soap opera. How these characters obtained the processed gas of a predatory Brethedan plant is rarely as important as the exciting drama, however.

Hellknight Armiger.name: 地狱骑士扈从 Hellknight Armiger
Hellknight Armiger.tokenName: 地狱骑士扈从 Hellknight Armiger

Frag Grenade (Tactical).name: Frag Grenade (Tactical)
Frag Grenade (Tactical).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @Damage[1d4[bleed]]

These fragmentary grenades explode in a cloud of shrapnel and deal 2d8 piercing damage in a @Template[burst|distance:10] with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save.

Incendiary Grenade (Tactical).name: Incendiary Grenade (Tactical)
Incendiary Grenade (Tactical).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical 5 feet

These grenades use an ignited chemical to explode in a blast of intense heat and deal 2d8 fire damage with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save (@Template[burst|distance:10]). If you have access to the critical specialization effect of grenades, creatures who critically fail their saving throw against incendiary grenades are knocked away from the grenade.

Hardlight Series.name: Hardlight Series
Hardlight Series.description:
Upgrades: 1

A projector fastened to a belt or lapel encases the wearer's body in this shimmering hardlight shell. Military-issue hardlight armor follows a predictable standard, but private users enjoy a range of customizable fashion options.

Forged in Hellfire.name: Forged in Hellfire
Forged in Hellfire.description:
Trigger The armiger takes fire damage

Effect The armiger absorbs a fraction of the fire to empower their attacks. Until the end of their next turn, their Strikes deal an additional @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage.

Four-Armed.name: Four-Armed
Four-Armed.description: You have four arms, which allows you to wield and hold up to four hands' worth of weapons and equipment. At any time, one pair of hands is designated as your active hands. You can change this designation from one pair of hands to another by taking the Switch Hands action, which is a single action. You can wield items only with your active hands.

Rapid Bombardment.name: Rapid Bombardment
Rapid Bombardment.description: The armiger draws two grenades, then Area Fires both grenades. Creatures in the area of both grenades take a –1 circumstance penalty to their saving throws against both grenades.

Hellknight Armiger.publicNotes:
Hellknight armigers are the lowest-ranked Hellknights, though attaining this rank requires them to spend six years as an initiate and then pass the Hellknight Test, during which they must summon, combat, and defeat a fiend of Hell.

Hellknights are infernal knights, and while they once ruled entire nations on Lost Golarion, the Hellknights of the Pact Worlds enforce law and order more holistically, traveling in massive citadel starships to spread their influence rather than remaining in any one place. In battle, Hellknights are orderly, unrelenting, and deadly.

Crest-Eater Necrograft Zombie.name: Crest-Eater Necrograft Zombie

Necrograft.name: Necrograft
Necrograft.description: A necrograft zombie's necrografts can be disabled for 1 hour by a creature that succeeds at a @Check[religion|dc:22] check. Attempting to do so can typically be done only while within reach of the zombie, and is a 2-action activity that has the manipulate trait. A necrograft zombie with disabled necrografts is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and can't use any reactions.

Rapid Decomposition.name: Rapid Decomposition
Rapid Decomposition.description:
A necrograft zombie must use this reaction unless its necrograft is disabled

Trigger The zombie is reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The zombie rapidly decomposes into a heap of rotting biomass, dealing @Damage[2d8[poison],2d8[void]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:15] (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|options:area-effect] save) and making that area hazardous terrain for 1 minute (@Damage[1d4[void]|options:area-damage] damage). This ability destroys any organic items on the zombie (including necrografts).

Scree Strider.name: Scree Strider
Scree Strider.description: The crest-eater necrograft zombie ignores difficult terrain caused by rubble, sand, and uneven ground made of earth and stone.

Crest-Eater Necrograft Zombie.publicNotes:
Necrograft zombies are animated by necrografts created from the body parts and internal organs of undead creatures. On Eox, it's common to augment animals and beasts with necrografts to ensure their survival on the dead planet. After death, many of these creatures return as true undead to continue serving their creators.

Technological zombies are created when a dead creature is reanimated (or otherwise kept moving) by an internal or integrated technological item—usually an augmentation. Despite the vast differences in form and hardware between different tech zombies, they do have similarities—they're mindless undead driven to harm the living, with technological components and weaknesses that can be leveraged.

Line-Class Soldier Robot.name: Line-Class Soldier Robot

Integrated Commercial Hammer.name: Integrated Commercial Hammer
Integrated Commercial Hammer.description:
Upgrades: 1

Valued as both a crafting tool and an effective weapon, this hammer uses specially weighted heads and handles to deliver maximum force with each strike.

Field Repairs.name: Field Repairs
Field Repairs.description:
Frequency once per 10 minutes

Effect The line-class soldier robot salvages an adjacent corpse of a tech creature that was destroyed no more than 1 month ago to regain @Damage[18[healing]] Hit Points.

Formation.name: Formation
Formation.description: When a line-class soldier robot is adjacent to at least two other allies, the robot gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws. This bonus increases to +2 for Reflex saves against area effects.

Line-Class Soldier Robot.publicNotes:
Most line-class soldier robots are lanky, headless humanoid machines. They have four-fingered hands and armored torsos painted in their owner's livery, with a single red sensor in the upper torso. By default, they're armed with bargain-brand laser rifles and breaching hammers. Each is 5 feet tall and weighs 300 pounds.

Line soldiers are the final refinement of the soldier robot concept, made dangerous through their simplicity. Line-class robots can be adapted to any battlefield, download skill packages via infosphere, cannibalize damaged allies for repairs, and are simple to make, maintain, and modify. As such, they are relatively cheap to purchase in large numbers and can be deployed with little regard for casualties.

Living soldiers are a liability. They have families who don't want them to die, need salaries, have morale, require discipline, commit atrocities, and have opinions on the war. They're a necessary, but unfortunate, part of warfare for any society without an abiding love for necromancy.

Cutting-edge soldier robots promise to solve this problem. Soldier robots have no loved ones, opinions, self-preservation instinct, or tendency toward random atrocities. Many companies market their soldier robots as a humane solution to modern combat.

In practice, they're anything but.

Without lives on the line, soldier robots encourage their owners to engage more often and more violently. Standoffs turn into skirmishes, skirmishes into battles, and inevitably the innocent are caught up in the mix. They're particularly popular with corporations and non-governmental organizations that appreciate being able to throw a dozen soldier robots at any naysayer, public critic, or explorer they dislike while being able to deny true responsibility for what happens.

Though there's constant public fear of soldier robots going rogue, it has yet to prove a serious concern. If a soldier robot wants you dead, it's because someone doesn't mind if it kills you.

Barachius Angel.name: Barachius Angel

Interplanar Teleport (Self Only).name: Interplanar Teleport (Self Only)
Interplanar Teleport (Self Only).description:
需求 你具有目标位面的位面密钥用于给法术定位

你和你的盟友跨越位面之间的屏障进行旅行。目标移动至其他位面，比如火元素位面、幽界、或者外层裂隙。你必须知晓目标位面的存在，而且需要使用由该位面的物质制作的魔法位面密钥来给法术定位。就如同跨界传送这个法术本身一样，大多数著名位面的位面密钥具有罕见稀有度，而鲜为人知的位面及半位面通常具有稀有、甚至有可能时独特稀有度的位面密钥。

该法术极其不精准，你会出现在目标之一（由你选择）上一次造访该位面时的位置周边，误差为1d20 x 25英里。若所有目标都是初次造访某个特定位面，你们就会出现在该位面的随机位置。跨界传送不会提供返回的手段，不过除非发生了什么另外情况，否则再次施放跨界传送能让你返回自身之前身处的位面。

Truespeech (Constant).name: Truespeech (Constant)
Truespeech (Constant).description:
无论什么语言，目标都可以理解其所有的词语，并且向其他生物用该语言交流。当处于一群各异的生物中时，每次目标将要说话，他可以选择一个生物，并使用一种该生物理解的语言来讲述，即使该目标不知道这语言具体是什么。

升阶（7环级） 持续时间为8小时。

Eldritch Wrath.name: Eldritch Wrath
Eldritch Wrath.description:
You channel the bizarre powers that exist beyond the outer reaches of the universe, dealing 3d12 mental damage to each creature in the area as phantasmal claws, jaws, and tentacles lash out at each target. Each creature in the area must attempt a Will save. The area is difficult terrain for any creature sickened by the effects of this spell.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d12.

Holographic Memory.name: Holographic Memory
Holographic Memory.description:
You reach into the target's mind, extract a specific memory you have some awareness of, and project it into a hologram. Alternatively, when casting this spell, you can choose to pull a random memory left to the GM's discretion. The holographic memory can be no longer than 5 minutes; if the extracted memory is longer than this, the hologram simply ends at that point. The hologram includes audio as well as visual components, but it doesn't contain haptic, olfactory, or other components. The hologram typically plays a memory that takes place in a single scene, though it can cut to multiple scenes if they're accurate to the memory and its length allows. The hologram plays the memory exactly as the target experienced it and from the target's perspective, so it often lacks context.

This spell can target any living, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}, or deceased sentient creature with an Intelligence modifier of –3 or higher. If the target is deceased, it can't have been dead for longer than 1 week, or this spell fails; once a corpse has been subjected to holographic memory by any caster, any subsequent castings of holographic memory fail.

If the target is unwilling to share this memory, it can attempt a Will save to resist this spell.

Share Pain.name: Share Pain
Share Pain.description:
You telepathically share the sensation of agonizing pain with a creature. The target takes 3d12 mental damage and attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} for 1 minute.

Heightened (6th) The spell targets up to four creatures and deals 6d12 mental damage.

Doom Scroll.name: Doom Scroll
Doom Scroll.description:
You magically broadcast grim news onto nearby devices, holo projectors, screens, and other displays (including comm units on armor). Creatures in the area who can see one or more displays must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by the display.

Failure The creature is fascinated by the display and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} (even if the fascination ends).

Critical Failure The creature is fascinated by the display, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} (even if the fascination ends).

Soul Surge.name: Soul Surge
Soul Surge.description:
You channel your life force into a spear of energy. You lose 6 Hit Points or, if you're in a bond with a vitality network that has at least 12 Hit Points, you can reduce the vitality bond's Hit Points by 12 instead. Make a ranged spell attack against the target's AC. On a hit, you deal 6d6 spirit damage. On a critical hit, the target takes double damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}.

Heightened (+1) Increase the damage dealt by 2d6 and the Hit Points lost by 2 (or by 4 if you spend Hit Points from a vitality network).

Analyze Target.name: Analyze Target
Analyze Target.description:
You focus on the target, gathering data as magical holograms display information. When the casting is complete, you learn biometric information about the target, including its fingerprints, medical conditions, and other basic physiological information. You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.AJstokjdG6iDjVjE]{Impersonate} the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds} on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.7r6mw8H87SUaj9Tq]{Spell Effect: Analyze Target}

Heightened (3rd) The status bonus increases to +2, and you can target up to 10 creatures.
