  • 整理 为一件轻负载或者更低的物体染色，清洁，或者弄脏它。你可以通过10轮专注来影响一件1负载的物体，对于更大的物体为每负载1分钟。

魔法伎俩无法造成伤害或者负面效果。对一件物体的任何改变（在上述内容之外的）只会在你仍维持该法术时持续。

Reorient.name: Reorient
Reorient.description: Applying a combination of vital energy and acupressure, you help the target regain its footing. The target loses the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} condition if it has it. The target becomes immune to the off-guard condition until the start of your next turn, even if the condition would be circumstantially applied, such as by being flanked or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. The target is then immune to reorient for 10 minutes.

Stabilize.name: 稳定伤势 Stabilize
Stabilize.description: 生命能量将目标从鬼门关拉回。目标失去 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yZRUzMqrMmfLu0V1]{濒死 Dying} 状态，但它仍然 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{失去意识 Unconscious}，并为0生命值。

Vitality Lash.name: 命能袭 Vitality Lash
Vitality Lash.description:
你用创生熔炉的能量破坏目标的腐败本源。你造成2d6点命能伤害，这需进行一次基础强韧豁免。如果该生物在豁免中大失败，直到你的下个回合开始前，他还会陷入@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{力竭 Enfeebled 1}。

升阶（+1） 该伤害增加1d6。

Wisp Ally.name: Wisp Ally
Wisp Ally.description:
You summon a Tiny bouncing wisp of light that easily distracts even the most determined of combatants. The wisp floats near your target. A wisp doesn't take up space, grant flanking, or have any other attributes a creature would. The wisp generates bright light in a 10-foot radius. When you Cast the Spell and each time you Sustain the spell, your wisp attempts to distract your target, which must succeed at a Will save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the start of your next turn. A creature who critically succeeds becomes immune to wisp ally for 1 hour.

Heightened (+1) You create one additional wisp, allowing you to choose a different target. Each wisp you create must have a different target, and all of them attempt to distract their targets at the same time when you cast or Sustain this spell.

商业 Hammer.name: 商业 Hammer
商业 Hammer.description:
Upgrades: 1

Valued as both a crafting tool and an effective weapon, this hammer uses specially weighted heads and handles to deliver maximum force with each strike.

Feyhammer.name: Feyhammer
Feyhammer.description: In the hands of an obrhinnshee, all hammers gain a reach of 10 feet and are treated as magical weapons.

Soul Warden.name: 渡魂典卫 Soul Warden
Soul Warden.description:
Trigger A willing fey or creature sworn to serve an Eldest dies within 100 feet of an obrhinnshee

Effect The creature's soul is drawn into the obrhinnshee's hammer to be carried home.

Stardust.name: Stardust
Stardust.description: When an obrhinnshee dies, they burst into a 10-foot aura of blinding stardust. All non-fey in the area must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:16] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 minute and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round (dazzled for 1 hour and blinded for 1 minute on a critical failure).

Deathly Nimbus.name: Deathly Nimbus
Deathly Nimbus.description:
Frequency once per hour

Requirements A soul is contained in the obrhinnshee's hammer

Effect The obrhinnshee wreathes themself in flame. All of the obrhinnshee's Strikes deal an additional @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage for 1 minute.

Minor Wish.name: Minor Wish
Minor Wish.description: The obrhinnshee grants a willing creature within 100 feet a +2 circumstance bonus on its next attack roll, check, or saving throw before the end of its next turn. The target becomes immune to Minor Wish for 1 minute.

Starlight Charge.name: Starlight Charge
Starlight Charge.description: The obrhinnshee Strides twice and makes a melee Strike. On a critical hit using this action with a feyhammer, the target is automatically shoved 10 feet away from the obrhinnshee.

Obrhinnshee.publicNotes:
Obrhinnshees travel the Universe to expedite the return of fey souls to the First World after they die, preventing psychopomps from bringing them to the Boneyard. They believe the Eldest shouldn't allow the gods to take those they turned their backs on. In addition to this duty, the small fey seem drawn to people in need and feel compelled to render aid if they can. They're most commonly encountered in areas influenced by the First World but can be found traveling any region of space.

An obrhinnshee resembles a gnome with a shock of green, purple, or deep blue hair and glowing marks on their bodies that resemble star charts. These marks glow brighter when an obrhinnshee uses magic or whenever their hammer contains a soul. Obrhinnshees are small but strong creatures who prefer to wreathe themselves in flame and charge into battle, using their spells only if necessary.

Ksarik.name: Ksarik

Adapted Languages.name: Adapted Languages
Adapted Languages.description: A ksarik understands up to 3 languages from among the prevalent languages spoken by those it has most recently attacked.

Carrion Spores.name: Carrion Spores
Carrion Spores.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:18]

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effect (1d6 hours),

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} (1 day),

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} (1 day),

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} (1 day),

Stage 5 1d10+10 ksarik seedlings erupt from the victim's body, dealing @Damage[1[piercing]] damage per seedling and ending the disease

Ingested Adaptation.name: Ingested Adaptation
Ingested Adaptation.description:
A ksarik's tentacle Strike siphons the target's genetic code and psychic resonance. On a successful Strike, the ksarik gains one of the target's abilities, chosen from the following.

  • The amphibious or aquatic trait
  • A new precise or imprecise sense
  • A resistance to one damage type (up to resistance 7)
  • A burrow, fly, or swim Speed (up to 30 feet)
  • The ability to wield a category of melee weapons (simple, martial, or advanced) in its tentacles that the target is trained in (using its tentacle Strike bonus)
  • Altering the type of damage dealt by its acid spit to a type (cold, electricity, fire, poison, or sonic) dealt by one of the target's Strikes or other abilities

The ksarik can't duplicate abilities from equipment. It can maintain one adaptation at a time, which it retains for 24 hours or until it uses ingested adaptation again.

Ksarik.publicNotes:
Originating as mindless plants from Castrovel, ksariks developed rudimentary intelligence and uncanny adaptive abilities after millennia of exposure to psychic energy expended in the conflicts between formians and lashuntas. These 12-foot-long quadrupeds have up to a dozen face tentacles and see through two eyestalks that protrude from either side of the tentacular mass. Ksariks' mutable forms might temporarily develop webbing, wings, and other adaptations after they siphon off genetic code from their prey.

Originally scavengers, ksariks have evolved into deadly hunters capable of weaponizing their digestive and reproductive systems. The internal acid ksariks use to digest prey can be projected as lethal spittle. The dozens of thorns along their sides carry their genetic code and can be launched as projectiles, allowing ksarik saplings to feed off a victim as they grow. This easy transmittal of their young and their environmental adaptability have allowed ksariks to spread far beyond Castrovel.

Corpse Fleet Infantry.name: Corpse Fleet Infantry

Shotalashu Armor.name: Shotalashu Armor
Shotalashu Armor.description:
Upgrades: 1

Flexible but sturdy plates layered together form this traditional armor worn by lashunta cavalry on Castrovel. In times past, a shotalashu cavalry member forged their armor from their mount's shed skin, or more rarely, the skin of a fallen mount, to honor them in future battles. Synthetic imitations are fashionable on some parts of Castrovel.

Coordinated Shuffle.name: Coordinated Shuffle
Coordinated Shuffle.description:
Trigger An adjacent ally moves away from the Corpse Fleet infantry

Effect The Corpse Fleet infantry Strides up to half their Speed. They must move in the same direction as the triggering ally.

Fusillade.name: Fusillade
Fusillade.description: The Corpse Fleet infantry's ranged Strikes deal an additional 1d4 precision damage when the Corpse Fleet infantry is within the first range increment of at least one other member of the Corpse Fleet wielding a ranged weapon.

Corpse Fleet Infantry.publicNotes:
The Corpse Fleet's rank and file infantry are trained to work as a unit.

The Corpse Fleet is an army made up of undead supremacists who refuse to bow to the will of the living. Founded by defectors from the Eoxian navy, the Corpse Fleet aims to shatter Pact Worlds unity and liberate Eox from the influence of the living and those bone sages who sided with them. Unyielding and utterly ruthless, the Corpse Fleet deals in death and destruction, feeding on the living and exploiting the dead.

Digital Ghost Vidgamer.name: Digital Ghost Vidgamer

Techsense (Imprecise) 30 feet.name: Techsense (Imprecise) 30 feet
Techsense (Imprecise) 30 feet.description: A digital ghost senses creatures and items with the tech trait at the listed range.

Backup.name: Backup
Backup.description:
An offline copy of the digital ghost is stored in a device. When an active digital ghost is destroyed, it reforms after [[/gmr 2d4 #days]]{2d4 days} within the device that the copy is stored in, fully healed, but with no memory of what destroyed them. A digital ghost can be permanently removed from that device by attempting a @Check[computers|dc:24] check against the digital ghost's Will DC.

On a success, the copy of the digital ghost is purged from the device, and no new copies will be generated. On a critical success, any active copies are also purged.

Device Bound.name: Device Bound
Device Bound.description: A digital ghost can stray only a short distance from the device where they're stored. A typical limit is 120 feet.

Malware Infection.name: Malware Infection
Malware Infection.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect The digital ghost attempts to infect an item with the tech trait being held or used by a creature within 30 feet. The bearer can attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save.

Critical Success The item is unaffected, and the digital ghost can't use Malware Infection for 1 week.

Success The item is unaffected.

Failure The item becomes glitching 2.

Critical Failure The item becomes glitching 2, and an offline digital ghost based on the bearer is created on the device in [[/gmr 2d4 #days]]{2d4 days}.

Virtual Arms.name: Virtual Arms
Virtual Arms.description: A digital ghost's weapons deal mental damage, have the ghost killer upgrade, and disappear if dropped.

Digital Ghost Vidgamer.publicNotes:
Some vidgamers are so terminally online that they can manifest as a digital ghost.

Digital ghosts are data constructs crafted from forgotten user accounts and metadata that are gathered and formatted into a simulacrum of the source user.

Paradox 17.name: Paradox 17

Event Horizon.name: Event Horizon
Event Horizon.description:
You unleash the power of a supermassive black hole. All creatures in the area take 6d10 bludgeoning and 6d10 void damage. Event horizon ignores up to 10 resistance creatures in the area have to bludgeoning, and up to 10 resistance they have to void. Each creature attempts a Reflex save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} until the end of its next turn.

Failure The creature takes full damage, is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 3} until the end of its next turn, and slowed 1 for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is slowed 3 until the end of its next turn, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for 1 minute.

Indestructibility.name: 无敌咒 Indestructibility
Indestructibility.description: 你把自己与因果割裂。在法术持续时间内，你免疫任何对你有害的效果或者伤害，除了由神器，神力，以及类似的强力来源造成的效果。你仍然可以受到任何你愿意受到的效果的影响。

Implosion.name: 内爆术 Implosion
Implosion.description:
你通过令目标自身崩坏来摧毁目标，造成75伤害。每当你维持该法术时，你必须选择一个新的目标承受相同的效果；相同的生物不能被同一次施放的该法术重复作为目标。你也不能在一回合中通过内爆术影响超过一个生物。你不能以虚体，气体，液体或者其他缺少固体形态的生物作为目标。

升阶（+1） 该伤害增加10。

Slice Reality.name: Slice Reality
Slice Reality.description:
You slice through reality, forming a chasm of life-draining entropy in a straight line 20 feet high, 120 feet long, and 2 inches thick. The chasm provides cover. The chasm deals 7d8 void damage to each creature that's in the chasm's space when it's created, that attempts to pass through the chasm, or that ends its turn inside the chasm, with a basic Reflex save. A creature that succeeds at this save when the chasm is created is pushed to the nearest space on the side of its choice. Creatures trying to move through the chasm fail to do so if they critically fail the save, ending their movement next to the chasm.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

Truespeech (constant).name: Truespeech (constant)
Truespeech (constant).description:
无论什么语言，目标都可以理解其所有的词语，并且向其他生物用该语言交流。当处于一群各异的生物中时，每次目标将要说话，他可以选择一个生物，并使用一种该生物理解的语言来讲述，即使该目标不知道这语言具体是什么。

升阶（7环级） 持续时间为8小时。

Wave of Warning.name: Wave of Warning
Wave of Warning.description:
You deter enemies with a wave of hostile intent. The target takes 7d8 nonlethal mental damage, and the target must attempt a Will save. The psychic energy then arcs to another creature within 30 feet of the first target, jumps to another creature within 30 feet of that target, and so on. You can end the wave at any point. You can't target the same creature more than once, and you must have line of effect to all targets. Roll the damage only once, and apply it to each target (halving or doubling as appropriate for its saving throw outcome).

Critical Success The target creature is unaffected, and the wave ends with that target.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d8.

Causational Moments.name: Causational Moments
Causational Moments.description:
Frequency once per round

Trigger A creature would trigger Paradox 17's Reactive Strike, but it has no reactions remaining

Effect Paradox 17 Steps or Strikes prior to resolving the triggering action.

Reject Reality.name: Reject Reality
Reject Reality.description: 100 feet. When a creature in the aura observes Paradox 17 for the first time each day, it must attempt a @Check[will|dc:42] save or Performance check. On a failure, reality rejects the creature for as long as it can observe Paradox 17. The affected creature becomes hidden from all other creatures (except Paradox 17) as though invisible itself.

Bridge of Perception.name: Bridge of Perception
Bridge of Perception.description:
Requirements Another creature is observing Paradox 17 or has triggered interdict definition in the last hour

Effect Paradox 17 teleports to any location that both Paradox 17 and the required creature can observe across any distance, even to another galaxy or plane.

Disregard Reality.name: Disregard Reality
Disregard Reality.description: Paradox 17 can move through all creatures, objects, and structures as though they were incorporeal.

Interdict Definition.name: Interdict Definition
Interdict Definition.description: Paradox 17 automatically and instantly learns and can observe the exact location of any creature on any plane that succeeds at a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} check to learn any information about Paradox 17 or any creature who communicates information about Paradox 17 in any way. The ability to observe the creature and its surroundings, wherever the creature goes, lasts 1 hour.

Natural Invisibility.name: Natural Invisibility
Natural Invisibility.description: Paradox 17 is naturally invisible. It becomes visible only when using a hostile action.

Waking Light.name: Waking Light
Waking Light.description:
Paradox 17 burns away the fabric of reality with intense sunlight. If no creatures are observing Paradox 17, the light fills a @Template[type:cone|distance:60]. If any creature is observing Paradox 17, the area is a @Template[type:line|distance:60]. Creatures and unattended objects in the area turn temporarily translucent. Creatures in the area take @Damage[24d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:45] save. Paradox 17 can't use Waking Light again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Waking Light]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Success The target takes no damage.

Success The target takes half damage becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1}.

Failure The target takes full damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 2}.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 3}.

Paradox 17.publicNotes:
As Paradox 17 is normally invisible, most recorded observations of it start with hearing a roar "like static from a dead signal." All reports mention a profound yet hard-to-place sense of meaningfulness. One survivor saw it illuminated by a blast of light she described as "a spiral galaxy made up of shining eyes and glass feathers." Another described it as "dozens of interleaved wings scattered with embers." The only thing consistent about Paradox 17, however, is how deadly it can be if confronted directly.

Can you ever really know that the Universe you perceive exists? That you'll never awaken one day to find it was all a dream? A planet full of philosophers sought the answers to these questions once upon a time… shortly before they all vanished en masse. They left behind confusing records stating that the light of a "whirling star" revealed that physical matter had no substance and the Universe was secretly a mental prison. These records are all that remain from the first known encounter with the being known as Paradox 17.

Excuba.name: Excuba

Truespeech (constant).name: Truespeech (constant)
Truespeech (constant).description:
无论什么语言，目标都可以理解其所有的词语，并且向其他生物用该语言交流。当处于一群各异的生物中时，每次目标将要说话，他可以选择一个生物，并使用一种该生物理解的语言来讲述，即使该目标不知道这语言具体是什么。

升阶（7环级） 持续时间为8小时。

Caustic Conversion.name: Caustic Conversion
Caustic Conversion.description:
You launch a torrent of magical nanites that begin dissolving your target. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, you deal 3d8 acid damage plus 1d6 persistent acid damage. On a critical hit, double the initial damage, but not the persistent damage.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 2d8, and the persistent acid damage increases by 1d6.

Inject Nanobots.name: Inject Nanobots
Inject Nanobots.description:
You inject magical nanobots into a creature. Make a melee spell attack roll. On a hit, the target takes 4d6 acid damage. On a critical hit, the target takes double damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round as nanobots swarm through the target's biological or mechanical systems.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

Skim Data (at will).name: Skim Data (at will)
Skim Data (at will).description:
You touch one object and concentrate on a dataset contained within it, such as a story, message, file, infosphere site, audio recording, or video. You understand the gist of the dataset and can quickly locate the most relevant pages or sections to your search. If the dataset is written or otherwise communicated in a language you don't know, or you attempt to gain information from a computer you don't have access to (such as one with a password you don't know), the spell fails.

If you Cast this Spell as part of a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.t5nBkpjroaq7QBGK]{Research} activity, you treat a critical failure as a failure instead.

Analyze Target.name: Analyze Target
Analyze Target.description:
You focus on the target, gathering data as magical holograms display information. When the casting is complete, you learn biometric information about the target, including its fingerprints, medical conditions, and other basic physiological information. You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.AJstokjdG6iDjVjE]{Impersonate} the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds} on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.7r6mw8H87SUaj9Tq]{Spell Effect: Analyze Target}

Heightened (3rd) The status bonus increases to +2, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +3, and you can target any number of creatures.

Delete.name: Delete
Delete.description:
You delete data from a device or object with a flamboyant magical gesture. This data might be handwritten, printed, or digital. You delete up to 1,500 words' worth of text, one page of content, or one display screen worth of text or other visual information. Non-magical writing in or on an unattended or held object is automatically deleted. Attempt a counteract check to delete digital or magical writing. If you fail to delete writing that's part of a hazard, you trigger the hazard. This can't be used to delete the contents of a spellbook or similar means of storing magical formulas or spells.

If you target an attended tech item with the tracking trait, attempt a counteract check. On a success, the item becomes glitching 1. A creature holding the item can Interact to restart the software, reducing the glitching value by 1.

Heightened (7th) You can delete all the data stored locally on a single device or book, or all the data on a device or book related to a specific name, topic, or category.

Dream of Home.name: Dream of Home
Dream of Home.description: You draw on a creature's thoughts of home and the comfort it finds in that concept, overwhelming the creature with wistful memories of whatever home means to it. The target must succeed at a Will save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by the memories.

Implant Data.name: Implant Data
Implant Data.description:
You reprogram a computer or device to display a chosen piece of data when accessed. You can implant up to one piece of data per spell rank. A piece of data can be a simple fact, such as a creature or object's location or physical description; an image of a creature or object; or a simple statement of no more than 300 characters. When the target computer or device is accessed, this implanted data is the first data the accessing creature gains, regardless of what data the creature is looking for. Implanted data doesn't prevent a creature from accessing other information stored on a device, change how the device functions, or otherwise inhibit the device from functioning. If this spell's duration ends before a creature accesses the implanted data, the information vanishes.

Heightened (3rd) The duration is one year.

Heightened (6th) The duration is unlimited.

Seek the Stars.name: Seek the Stars
Seek the Stars.description:
In your mind's eye, you chart a path through the cosmos. If you're in Pact Worlds space, you immediately know which direction to travel in order to reach the Starstone on Absalom Station. If you're outside the Pact System, you know the direction to the nearest Drift beacon or the Starstone.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.

Digital Telepathy.name: Digital Telepathy
Digital Telepathy.description: 100 feet. An excuba can communicate telepathically with any item or creature with the tech trait within their aura as well as with other technologically telepathic creatures (such as those with shortwave and similar abilities). This ability also allows the spectra to remotely attempt Computers checks within their aura.

Arc Network.name: Arc Network
Arc Network.description: The excuba can communicate through items and creatures with the tech trait, projecting sound from and speaking through the item or creature's voice, speakers, or other audio output. In addition, the excuba can use any item or creature with the tech trait within their digital telepathy aura as the point of origin for their spells and abilities when calculating range, distance, line of effect, and area of effect.

Slip Drive.name: Slip Drive
Slip Drive.description: An excuba has an internal biomechanical Drift engine, allowing them to function as a Drift engine for a starship or vehicle they pilot as a 1-minute activity. An excuba can enter or exit the Drift themself as a 1-minute activity. When an excuba gets a critical failure on a Piloting check to Navigate or Plot a Course through the Drift, they get a failure instead.

Techsense.name: Techsense
Techsense.description: The excuba senses creatures and objects with the tech trait as a precise sense within the listed range.

Autoencode.name: Autoencode
Autoencode.description: An excuba's visual appearance is altered when the excuba is perceived by creatures, items, and objects with the tech trait (such as robotic creatures, cameras, and sensors) unless they succeed at a Perception check against the excuba's Deception DC. Excubas in their swarm form appear to be formless shadows and are additionally @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}, while excubas in their humanoid forms register as familiar- or unremarkable-looking humanoids.

Data Vault.name: Data Vault
Data Vault.description: An excuba can store data within their body as if they were a computer. Hacking an excuba requires three successes against their Will DC to access their data.

Change Shape.name: 变身 Change Shape
Change Shape.description:
The excuba can take on the appearance of any Small or Medium humanoid.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ChangeShape]

Data Search.name: Data Search
Data Search.description:
Frequency once per minute

Effect The excuba selects one creature with the tech trait in their space, searching its mind and memories. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and immune to Data Search for 24 hours.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The excuba accesses the creature's memories of the last 24 hours, downloading a holographic copy into their data vault and gaining insights into the creature's tactics. The creature must roll twice and take the lower result on its next attack roll, skill check, or saving throw against the excuba or one of the excuba's spells or abilities. This effect has the misfortune trait.

Critical Failure As failure, but the excuba also obtains a specific type of memory (such as what happened during the last time the creature encountered a Drift beacon) regardless of how long ago it was. The target creature loses this memory until it gains access to the excuba's data vault.

Swarming Synchronization.name: Swarming Synchronization
Swarming Synchronization.description: Each enemy creature in the swarm's space takes @Damage[2d8[sonic]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save). On a failure, creatures and objects with the tech trait become glitching 1 (or glitching 2 on a critical failure).

Excuba.publicNotes:
Unlike all other known types of spectra, excuba spectra are fragmented creatures made of countless tiny pieces that act together as a single networked whole. No accounts of an excuba encounter exist before the Drift crashed, and prevailing theories hold that either the trauma of the crisis created them, or they were created by the Drift as a response to the crisis itself. Whatever the case, excuba encounters have become all too common. These spectra swarms are often dispatched to infiltrate mortal worlds as spies or observers. They collect information for more powerful spectra like wyspirias to analyze and preserve. Some speculate that excubas have always lurked in the galaxy as invisible spies for other spectra, and that the Drift Crisis merely caused them to take a new, less cohesive form.

In the Great Beyond, time is measured not in hundreds, thousands, or millions of years, but rather in eons—in iterations of reality itself. Only rising to prominence within the last three centuries alongside the advent and proliferation of Drift technology, spectra have thrived in a burgeoning niche of the otherwise stagnant zeitgeist of the Great Beyond.

Though their origins and objectives remain shrouded in mystery, encounters with spectra revolve around new technological discoveries, sharing the knowledge of faster-than-light travel, and protecting the Drift. No one knows if spectra existed before the Drift, but their connection to the hyperspace plane is indisputable. Prominent metaphysicians believe spectra were created as amalgams of raw quintessence pulled into the Drift, just as the Drift itself grows from pieces of the Multiverse. If this is true, spectra's interest in the proliferation of Drift technology takes a subtle sinister turn, as each new Drift engine serves to harvest the Great Beyond and all its denizens for the growth of the Drift and spectra themselves.

Skarak.name: Skarak

Destructive.name: Destructive
Destructive.description: A skarak's horn Strike bypasses the Hardness of structures made primarily of wood, stone, and metal.

Etheric Tether.name: Etheric Tether
Etheric Tether.description: A creature hit by a skarak's etheric web is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} by the skarak. The creature isn't @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, but it can't move beyond the maximum range of the skarak's etheric web. A creature can sever the etheric web with a Strike against AC 30 that deals at least 18 fire or force damage. This deals no damage to the skarak.

Rampage.name: 暴走 Rampage
Rampage.description: The skarak makes a horn Strike against each creature within reach. These attacks count toward the skarak's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the skarak makes all these attacks.

Tether Pull.name: Tether Pull
Tether Pull.description:
Requirements A creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} by the skarak's etheric tether

Effect The creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:33] save or be teleported to a space adjacent to the skarak of the skarak's choosing. This ends the grabbed condition from etheric tether unless the creature critically fails the save.

Skarak.publicNotes:
The skarak is a ravenous mass of spiked chitin and teeth propelled with frightening speed by a collection of spindly, arachnid legs. It rampages across its territories, consuming everything smaller than itself and taking delight in the destruction it leaves in its wake. It's difficult to hide from a skarak, as it'll rip down fortifications and smash through concrete walls to get at prey cowering inside buildings. It's even more difficult to run from a skarak since it can spit a corrosive webbing that magically binds its prey to itself, allowing it to pull its prey across any distance to within reach of its waiting maw.

A skarak possesses a terrible cunning to complement its destructive might. It often watches a targeted settlement for days, analyzing defenses before launching a devastating offensive. It's typically a solitary creature except when training a newly hatched brood to hunt. During this period, it ranges ahead of its young, rampaging through densely populated areas and leaving wounded, weakened targets behind it. Survivors of the initial rampage soon discover they aren't the lucky ones as the skarak young come after, devouring all in their path.

Irradiated Undead Flyer.name: Irradiated Undead Flyer

Meltdown.name: Meltdown
Meltdown.description:
Trigger The irradiated undead plant is reduced to 0 Hit Points by energy damage of any kind

Effect The energy acts as a catalyst for a nuclear reaction, causing the plant to explode and dealing @Damage[5d6[poison]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] with a @Check[reflex|dc:19|basic] save.

Radioactive Remains.name: Radioactive Remains
Radioactive Remains.description: 20 feet. A creature entering or starting its turn inside the aura is exposed to severe radiation.

Strafing Snap.name: Strafing Snap
Strafing Snap.description: The irradiated undead flyer Strides twice up to its Speed in a straight line, making a single wing slash Strike against all creatures whose spaces it flies adjacent to. All creatures damaged by the Strafing Snap are considered to be within the flyer's aura at the start of their next turn. The flyer can't use Strafing Snap again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Strafing Snap]]{1d4 rounds}.

Wing Spasm.name: Wing Spasm
Wing Spasm.description:
Frequency once per minute

Effect The irradiated undead flyer channels energy into its wings, forcing all creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] to attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:19] save.

Critical Success No effect.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d8[void]|options:area-damage] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[2d8[void]|options:area-damage] damage and is pushed back 5 feet.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[4d8[void]|options:area-damage] damage and is pushed back 10 feet.

Irradiated Undead Flyer.publicNotes:
Flyer is a general term referring to winged creatures such as drakes, gigantic dragonflies, raptors, and other flying beasts that have been reanimated by severe radiation.

To survive in the unforgiving expanse of space, creatures must be naturally adapted or take protective measures. While the dead rarely need such precautions against these threats, sometimes radiation can animate a corpse (or several) to a state of undeath!

Scientists and religious scholars alike debate whether a specific type of radiation is to blame for this phenomenon that spontaneously triggers unlife, but all agree on the dangers such irradiated creatures pose. Many types of radiation could be the cause: intense bursts of cosmic radiation emitted by the stars, magically enhanced radioactive isotopes found in a planet's or moon's crust, or even the advent of new energy sources in a society (or its radioactive waste) contaminating the land. Regardless of their specific origin, irradiated undead all pose the same threat—they emit ionizing radiation that ravages everything around them.

Cybernetic Zombie.name: Cybernetic Zombie

Integrated Commercial Laser Pistol.name: Integrated Commercial Laser Pistol
Integrated Commercial Laser Pistol.description:
Upgrades: 1

This compact pistol is the most popular sidearm in the Pact Worlds thanks to its reliability, durability, and affordability. Most common laser pistols have a lightweight polycarbon body ending in a long, slim barrel.

Cybernetics.name: Cybernetics
Cybernetics.description: A cybernetic zombie's cybernetics can be disabled for 1 hour by a creature that succeeds at a DC 15 check to [[/act disable-device dc=15]] or Disable a System. Attempting to do so can typically be done only while within reach of the zombie and is a 2-action activity that has the manipulate trait. The cybernetics are also disabled for 1 hour if the zombie takes any electricity damage. A cybernetic zombie with disabled cybernetics is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and can't use any reactions.

Self-Destruct.name: Self-Destruct
Self-Destruct.description:
A cybernetic zombie must use this reaction unless its cybernetics are disabled

Trigger The cybernetic zombie is reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The zombie's damaged cybernetics explode, dealing @Damage[1d8[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] (@Check[reflex|dc:15|basic] save). This ability destroys the zombie's integrated items and any tech items on the zombie of 1st level or lower.

Cybernetic Zombie.publicNotes:
The most common form of tech zombie is the cybernetic zombie, but tech zombies can also arise through other types of technologies, including nanites.

Technological zombies are created when a dead creature is reanimated (or otherwise kept moving) by an internal or integrated technological item—usually an augmentation. Despite the vast differences in form and hardware between different tech zombies, they do have similarities—they're mindless undead driven to harm the living, with technological components and weaknesses that can be leveraged.

Tashtari.name: Tashtari

Bristle Flash.name: Bristle Flash
Bristle Flash.description:
The tashtari causes their filaments to glow with intense light. Non-tashtari creatures within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:40] must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:19] save. A creature that attempts this save is immune to all Bristle Flashes for 1 minute. The tashtari's fur then dims, and they can't use this ability until they bask in sunlight for at least 10 minutes.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is dazzled for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round and dazzled for 1 minute.

Tashtari.publicNotes: Native to the green world of Castrovel, the tashtari has become known throughout the wider galaxy as the laser wolf. Wolflike pack hunters, tashtaris have a layer of flexible filaments that emit phosphorescent lights and allow them to silently communicate through a bespoke visual language. Each tashtari possesses a node within their throat that allows them to channel their stored solar energy and unleash a beam of focused light, giving them their moniker of laser wolves. A mating pair, consisting of one or two alphas, leads packs of tashtaris, who generally become the apex predators of large areas with one or more forested regions. The presence of trees is important for the nocturnal tashtaris, who sleep on the treetops during the day to bask in sunlight and recharge their solar energies.

Jinsul Pontiff.name: Jinsul Pontiff

Selective Invisibility.name: Selective Invisibility
Selective Invisibility.description:
A willing creature you touch becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} to everyone except a second target within the spell's range. If the invisible creature uses a hostile action against anyone who isn't the target that can see them, the spell ends after that hostile action is completed.

Heightened (5th) You can make up to 6 willing creatures within 30 feet invisible, but they all must have the same target.

Share Pain.name: Share Pain
Share Pain.description:
You telepathically share the sensation of agonizing pain with a creature. The target takes 3d12 mental damage and attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} for 1 minute.

Heightened (6th) The spell targets up to four creatures and deals 6d12 mental damage.

Void Whispers.name: Void Whispers
Void Whispers.description:
The target hears ominous sounds containing unspeakable mysteries from beyond the stars that disorient its mind. The target attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The target hears the strange whispers, becoming @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The target begins to wander aimlessly, using each action to Step in a random direction. When it moves, the target takes the safest route and doesn't enter hazardous terrain. The spell has a duration of 1 minute or until a creature uses a hostile action against the target.

Critical Failure As failure, except the target babbles incoherently. Any creature that begins its turn within 20 feet of the target must attempt a Will saving throw. On a failure, it must use its first action on its next turn to Step in a random direction and becomes immune to this spell for 24 hours. This is an incapacitation effect.

Blur.name: 朦胧术 Blur
Blur.description: 目标的形态变得模糊不清。它获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}。由于该效果的原理，目标的位置仍然是显然的，目标不能使用该隐蔽来躲藏或潜行。

Bless.name: 祝福术 Bless
Bless.description:
来自众界的祝福加护你同伴的攻击得以命中。你和你在弥漫中的盟友在攻击骰上获得+1状态加值。每轮一次，你可以维持该法术将弥漫的半径增加10尺。

祝福术可以反制@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.7ZinJNzxq0XF0oMx]{绝望术 Bane}。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.RfCEHpMoEAZvB9IZ]{灵光：祝福术}

Mind Skewer.name: Mind Skewer
Mind Skewer.description:
You overload a creature's mind with a glut of psychic information or divine judgments. The mental onslaught deals 2d6 mental damage. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

Overheat.name: Overheat
Overheat.description:
You vent heat toward a nearby target. You deal 2d6 fire damage to the target. On a failed save, the target's held equipment becomes hot to the touch for the next round. Unless the target drops its currently held equipment at the start of its turn, it takes @Damage[(3*@item.rank)[fire]] damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6. Increase the damage of touching affected objects by 3.

Shifting Surge.name: Shifting Surge
Shifting Surge.description:
You release a surge of magical energy that temporarily changes the type of damage dealt by a weapon. The next Strike the weapon makes deals your choice of acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic damage and deals an additional 1d6 extra damage of the same type as the weapon's normal damage.

Heightened (+2) The extra damage increases by 1d6.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.pWm0NCsxjxTUCJ7y]{Spell Effect: Shifting Surge}

Soothe.name: 安抚术 Soothe
Soothe.description:
你安抚目标的精神，增强其精神防御并治愈其伤口。目标在你施放该法术时，回复1d10+4生命值，并且在持续时间中，目标对抗心灵效果的豁免上获得+2状态加值。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.nkk4O5fyzrC0057i]{法术效果：安抚术}

升阶（+1） 该治疗量增加1d10+4。

Vitality Network.name: Vitality Network
Vitality Network.description: A jinsul pontiff can channel vitality to up to four bonded allies as though they were a mystic. This network stores up to @Damage[20[healing]] Hit Points and begins combat full.

Fill Vitality.name: Fill Vitality
Fill Vitality.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The jinsul pontiff restores @Damage[6[healing]] Hit Points to their vitality network, up to its maximum.

Strider Leap.name: Strider Leap
Strider Leap.description: The jinsul Strides and then performs a [[/act long-jump]]. They can Stride less than 10 feet and not automatically fail their check to Long Jump.

Transfer Vitality.name: Transfer Vitality
Transfer Vitality.description: The jinsul pontiff transfers any number of Hit Points from their vitality network to a visible bonded target.

Jinsul Pontiff.publicNotes:
Pontiffs rally jinsuls to a near suicidal frenzy in the throes of combat. Part magic wielder and part orator, they act as inspirational battlefield leaders. Each serves one of the major jinsul hierarchs, who in turn act as the leaders of the overall Jinsul Hierocracy.

Jinsuls are a warlike species scattered across the Vast. Six-legged creatures with a bulbous body covered in eyes, jinsuls have notable segments of chitin along with a set of upper forelimbs for using equipment. They long ago lived in the region of space known as the Scoured Stars but eventually departed and settled on the harsh world of Rax. In recent years, the divine herald Dhurus unified the jinsuls into a cohesive force known as the Jinsul Hierocracy, which briefly retook the Scoured Stars. A joint alliance between several species and members of the Starfinder Society defeated Dhurus and scattered the jinsuls back into the Vast.

With countless bases and fleets operating in deep space, jinsuls mostly act as raiders, scavenging for equipment and testing their mettle against other species. Indoctrinated to view themselves as a superior species, with only other inhabitants of the Scoured Stars having any value, jinsuls prefer to engage in short-ranged combat with their foes.

Jinsuls aren't numerous enough to fill every need on the many ships and starbases they control across the galaxy. Instead, they rely on robots and automated computer systems (always commanded by at least one jinsul officer) to keep their space stations running efficiently. These systems are programmed to be just as deadly and ruthless as jinsuls themselves; even the most innocuous automated system or robot, like a garbage collector, might wield deadly weapons or contain vicious technomagical countermeasures.

Elindrian Acolyte.name: Elindrian Acolyte

Daze.name: 晕眩术 Daze
Daze.description:
你侵入目标的精神，利用精神冲击令目标晕眩。该冲击造成1d6心灵伤害，具有基础意志豁免。如果目标在豁免中大失败，它获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{震慑 Stunned 1}。

升阶（+2） 该伤害增加1d6。

Enfeeble.name: 衰弱术 Enfeeble
Enfeeble.description:
你抽走目标的力量，基于其强韧豁免。

大成功 目标不受影响。

成功 目标获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{力竭 Enfeebled 1}，直到你的下个回合开始。

失败 目标获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{力竭 Enfeebled 2}，持续1分钟。

大失败 目标获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{力竭 Enfeebled 3}，持续1分钟

Force Barrage.name: 力场飞弹 Force Barrage
Force Barrage.description:
你发射出一道固态的魔力碎片，射向一个你能看见的生物。它自动命中，并造成1d4+1力场伤害。你施放该法术时每额外使用一个动作，你射出的碎片增加一个，最大为3个动作发射三个碎片。你为每个碎片分别选择目标。如果你对同一个目标射击超过1个碎片，将伤害合计后再计算伤害加值，减值，抗力，弱点等。

升阶（+2） 你花费的每个动作额外射出一枚碎片。

Injury Echo.name: Injury Echo
Injury Echo.description:
You manifest an injury from the future or past to harm a creature in the present. Choose bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage. The target takes 1d8 of the selected damage type with a basic Will save. If the target fails its save and then takes the same type of damage before the end of your next turn, it takes an additional @Damage[ceil((@item.level)/2)d4[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 1d8, and the persistent damage on a failure increases by 1d4.

Mind Skewer.name: Mind Skewer
Mind Skewer.description:
You overload a creature's mind with a glut of psychic information or divine judgments. The mental onslaught deals 2d6 mental damage. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

Read Aura.name: 阅读灵光 Read Aura
Read Aura.description:
你专注于目标物体，拓展神智，以感受魔法灵光。当你完成施法后，你得知该物品是否具有魔法。你或者你共有了灵光情报的人在对该物品@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.eReSHVEPCsdkSL4G]{Identify Magic}时获得+2环境加值。如果该物体是幻象，仅当该效果的环级低于你的阅读灵光环级时你才能发现此事实。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.J6OhRNSN23KzOPRL]{Spell Effect: Read Aura}

升阶（3环级） 你能以至多10个物体为目标。

升阶（8环级） 你能以任意数量物体为目标。

Seek the Stars.name: Seek the Stars
Seek the Stars.description:
In your mind's eye, you chart a path through the cosmos. If you're in Pact Worlds space, you immediately know which direction to travel in order to reach the Starstone on Absalom Station. If you're outside the Pact System, you know the direction to the nearest Drift beacon or the Starstone.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.

Void Warp.name: 虚能噬 Void Warp
Void Warp.description:
你唤来虚能伤害生命力量。该目标承受2d4虚能伤害，这有一次基础强韧豁免. 如果该生物在豁免中大失败，他也会@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{力竭 Enfeebled 1}直到你得下个回合开始。

升阶（+1） 该伤害增加1d4。

Husk Mind.name: Husk Mind
Husk Mind.description:
Elindrians can communicate nearly instantaneously via a primal bond similar to a formian hive mind. While within telepathic range of at least one other elindrian with husk mind, an elindrian acolyte gains a +2 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls, Perception checks, and saving throws against mental effects. If one elindrian is aware of a combatant, all members of the husk mind within range are aware of it.

The elindrian's husk mind is integrated with their scent; for purposes of detecting other elindrians, formians, undead, or corpses, their scent is a precise sense, rather than imprecise.

An elindrian always knows the precise distance and direction to the Terror of Ukulam, so long as they're on the same plane of existence.

Unfetter Allies.name: Unfetter Allies
Unfetter Allies.description: While the elindrian acolyte is within 30 feet of another elindrian who's unable to move or act normally (such as if they're buried, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}, stunned, or otherwise impeded), the elindrian acolyte becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} 1 and can use the extra action to Burrow, Fly, [[/act shove]], Stride, or Strike.

Elindrian Acolyte.publicNotes:
These elindrians are the rank-and-file worshippers of the Terror of Ukulam. Recently risen from the dead by some primal force or occult upheaval—no one is sure—they single-mindedly pursue their grisly task of unearthing fellow elindrians across Castrovel; many others have risen from the dead, but most still remain buried. They stop only to attack other living creatures that intervene.

The people known as elindrians vanished eons ago into desolate husk-hives beneath the surface of the planet Castrovel. Until recently, they were creatures of legend, existing only in the most obscure formian histories. Now, scholars on Castrovel desperately search for additional records about elindrians, their origins, and—most importantly—the truth about their connection to the terrifying ooze-like creature that recently descended from their moon, Elindrae. One theory is that elindrians share some primal bond with the ooze-thing called the Terror of Ukulam, and perhaps both originate from Elindrae—other theories claim that elindrians created the ooze in the first place or that they're distant cousins to formians, corrupted by the Terror long ago.

Apari.name: Apari

Carapace.name: Carapace
Carapace.description: The apari's carapace has Hardness 10, reducing any damage the apari takes by that amount. Once the apari is reduced to fewer than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon taking damage from a critical hit, the carapace breaks, removing the Hardness and reducing its AC to 20. An apari with a broken carapace gains resistance 5 to precision damage and weakness 5 to both area damage and splash damage. It also gains the Swarming Bites aura.

Swarming Bites.name: Swarming Bites
Swarming Bites.description:
10 feet. The constituents of an apari with a broken carapace angrily swarm around its body. A creature that begins its turn in this aura takes @Damage[4d6[piercing]] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:21|basic] save. A creature that fails this save is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure).

Can alternatively switch to @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] or @Damage[4d6[slashing]] with the Fungible ability.

Constituent Sortie.name: Constituent Sortie
Constituent Sortie.description:
Requirements The apari's carapace isn't broken

Effect The apari releases a bevy of tiny, flying constituents in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] that sting all within. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[4d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save.

Foraging Constituents.name: Foraging Constituents
Foraging Constituents.description: The apari Seeks by dispersing constituents to scan a wide area, attempting to find hidden or undetected creatures within 90 feet.

Fungible.name: Fungible
Fungible.description: The apari's constituents can change their physiology, taking advantage of an opponent's weakness. The apari alters the type of damage it deals with its claw attack (which also affects the type of persistent damage), its Constituent Sortie ability, or its swarming bites aura, choosing from the following: bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing.

Apari.publicNotes:
An apari is a living hive beholden to its beetle-like queen, whose massive carapace houses generations of its insect brood. Millions of constituents across various developmental stages animate the exoskeleton of a single apari. At the direction of their queen's chemical signals, individual constituents, most no larger than a grain of rice, adapt to the hive's current needs, assuming diverse forms to fulfill specialized roles and supporting its gestalt biological functions.

Once introduced within an ecosystem, an apari asserts itself rapidly. Winged constituents forage for food as the hive itself hunts living prey, tearing its victims open with sharp claws while grub-like constituents writhe into fresh wounds and consume the prey from within. When an apari is threatened, combat-ready drones selflessly rush to defend the hive or to cover its escape.

Deh-Nolo.name: Deh-Nolo

Corrosive Haze.name: Corrosive Haze
Corrosive Haze.description:
You unleash a cloud of corrosive nanites. Creatures that start their turn in the area take 6d8 acid damage and 1d6 persistent acid damage with a basic Fortitude save. The nanites also corrode any unattended objects in the area, dealing 3d8 acid damage. Each round you Sustain the spell, you can move the cloud up to 10 feet in any direction. You can Dismiss the spell.

Heightened (+1) The initial damage increases by 1d8, and the persistent acid damage increases by 1d6.

Promession.name: Promession
Promession.description:
You flash freeze a target then send subsonic waves through it, potentially reducing it into fine powder. Make a spell attack against the target. If you hit a target, the target takes 6d8 cold damage and must attempt a Fortitude save. If you critically hit, the target gets a result one degree of success worse than it rolled on its Fortitude save. A creature reduced to 0 Hit Points is blasted to fine powder; its gear remains.

Critical Success The target is unaffected by further damage.

Success The target takes an additional @Damage[((@item.level -3)d8)[sonic]] damage.

Failure The target takes an additional @Damage[((@item.level)d8)[sonic]] damage.

Critical Failure The target takes an additional @Damage[((@item.level +6)d8)[sonic]] damage.

Heightened (+1) The cold and sonic damage each increase by 1d8.

Overload Systems.name: Overload Systems
Overload Systems.description:
You flood the area with a wave of electrical interference. You deal 7d8 electricity damage to creatures in the area. Each creature in the area must attempt a Reflex save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage, and any of its items with the tech trait become glitching 1 for 1 minute or until the condition is removed.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage, and any of its items with the tech trait become glitching 2 for 1 minute or until the condition is removed.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

Synaptic Pulse.name: 神经脉冲 Synaptic Pulse
Synaptic Pulse.description:
你释放出爆发的精神脉冲，穿透区域中每个敌人的精神。每个该区域中的敌人必须尝试一次意志豁免。

大成功 该生物不受影响。

成功 该生物获得 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{震慑 Stunned 1}。

失败 该生物获得 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{震慑 Stunned 2}。

大失败 该生物震慑，持续1轮。

Translocate (at will).name: Translocate (at will)
Translocate (at will).description:
你立即将自身以及自己正在穿戴和持有的物品从当前空间转移走，移动至法术射程内你能看到的未被占据的空间。如果这将会带着其他生物与你一同移动——即便你把该生物放置在异次元容器中——你就会失去该法术。

升阶（5环级） 法术射程增加至1英里。你不需要看到目的地，只要你曾经去过目的地，并且知晓该地点与你相对的位置以及距离即可。之后你会在1小时内临时免疫该法术。

Discharge (at will).name: Discharge (at will)
Discharge (at will).description:
You ignite a sudden spark of electricity that surges into a technological creature or object. You de-energize an item with the tech trait or disrupt a creature with the tech trait, with effects based on the target being a creature or object.

  • Creature If your target is a creature with the tech trait, it attempts a Will save. On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}; on a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}.
  • Object If you target an attended object, the creature with the object attempts a Will save. On a success, the object loses half of its maximum charges. On a failure, or if you target an unattended object, the object loses all its remaining charges.

Instant Virus (at will).name: Instant Virus (at will)
Instant Virus (at will).description:
Your touch installs a dangerous self-replicating virus that disrupts a technological creature's programming. The target must attempt a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}.

Failure The target is glitching 1 and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2}.

Thoughtsense.name: 思维感知 Thoughtsense
Thoughtsense.description:
The deh-nolo senses a creature's mental essence as a precise sense with the listed range; they can't sense mindless creatures with thoughtsense.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Thoughtsense]

+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: 在所有对魔法的豁免上获得+1状态加值

Brain Loss.name: Brain Loss
Brain Loss.description: If a deh-nolo takes 60 damage from a critical hit or takes 50 mental damage, they must succeed at a DC 34 save (@Check[fortitude|dc:34] for critical damage or @Check[will|dc:34] for mental damage) or one of their brain sacs is destroyed.

Brain Sacs.name: Brain Sacs
Brain Sacs.description: A deh-nolo snatcher has four brain sacs that they use to store harvested brains. A deh-nolo knows one additional language for each brain they have stored in a brain sac, chosen from the languages that the creature knew when its brain was harvested. A deh-nolo without all four sacs full is stupefied with a value equal to the number of empty sacs.

Open Pustules.name: Open Pustules
Open Pustules.description: When the deh-nolo takes damage, pustules on their body burst, spraying foul poison. Each adjacent creature is exposed to dominion bile.

Dominion Bile.name: Dominion Bile
Dominion Bile.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:34]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage, enfeebled 1, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} (1 round)

Stage 4 death

Harvest Brain.name: Harvest Brain
Harvest Brain.description: The deh-nolo harvests the brain of a creature that's been dead for no more than 1 minute. They can then use an Interact action to secure the brain in one of their empty brain sacs.

Deh-Nolo.publicNotes:
Deh-nolos are the Dominion's foremost engineers, seamlessly and deviously merging technology and biological systems to create any device imaginable, from sinister weapons to massive starships to shipmind oozes. These creations are grown, not built, but they're neither cybernetics nor biotech—rather, something in between. Deh-nolos seek to understand all technology they encounter, and they believe it's their right to consume the brain of anyone they think might help them better understand an unfamiliar device. Then again, deh-nolos have no qualms about consuming brains for nearly any reason, seeing all forms of life outside the Dominion as lesser beings.

While mostly organic, deh-nolos can concentrate their shimmering liquid metal secretions into foul crystals that they can use as projectiles or mechanical components for their twisted inventions. A casual onlooker might assume these creations are biotechnological in nature, as they often look like atrophied organs and mutated body parts held together by seemingly crude or incomplete circuitry. Those who attempt to incorporate deh-nolo technology into their bodies as augmentations typically experience terrible pain and grisly deaths, even if they somehow manage to isolate or drain the toxins naturally produced by a deh-nolo's alien flesh. Even less intrusive experiments with deh-nolo technology are unpredictable and dangerous, and the results of a "successful" outcome are disturbing at best.

Athraxer.name: Athraxer

Motion Sense.name: Motion Sense
Motion Sense.description: An athraxer can sense nearby creatures through vibrations in air or water caused by movement as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

Acid Blood.name: Acid Blood
Acid Blood.description: An adjacent creature that deals slashing or piercing damage to the athraxer is sprayed with acidic blood and takes 2 persistent acid damage (@Check[reflex|dc:14|basic] save).

Colony Mind.name: Colony Mind
Colony Mind.description: 30 feet. All allies with colony mind within the listed range of each other are in constant telepathic communication and are aware of all threats any individual knows about. While within range of at least one ally with colony mind, when a creature rolls a success on a save against a mental effect, it gets a critical success instead.

Death Throe.name: Death Throe
Death Throe.description:
Trigger The athraxer would die

Effect The athraxer first makes a claw Strike against a creature in reach, then dies.

Athraxer.publicNotes:
Swarm invasions deploy athraxers as infantry. Fearless and violent, they're crucial for providing reconnaissance of a world's environment and defenses. A single Swarm ship can carry thousands of eggs in stasis.

Developing far out in the Vast from an insectile species known as kucharns, the Swarm is considered the greatest threat in existence for several cultures. Composed of countless subcolonies, each consisting of myriad component subspecies, the Swarm is a vast host that acts in psychic union for the purpose of consuming all matter across the cosmos. Most frightening of all is the Swarm's ability to assimilate the knowledge and DNA of those they consume. Thanks to its pre-Gap development of star travel, the Swarm had already consumed innumerable systems before history short-circuited, and the Swarm's pace only accelerated once it received access to the Signal and the hyperspace dimension known as the Drift.

Worlds touched by the Swarm are drained of their natural resources and rendered barren places known as huskworlds. What outsiders consider to be "Swarm space" is little more than a swathe of lifeless worlds that the Swarm have consumed in its reckless advance. The Swarm so thoroughly strips these worlds that surveyors struggle to find even trace amounts of inorganic resources, such as rare minerals and water. Few of these huskworlds have been thoroughly explored, as oftentimes exploration awakens inert drones, theorized to have been left behind in case the devoured system was ever colonized and repopulated. Pockets of Swarm activity exist well beyond this region of space, as the Swarm sent out countless subcolonies in the ages before the Gap, and even now, only some of them have reached their intended destinations.

The Swarm is perhaps most known in the current era for two important facts. First, its assault on the Pact Worlds and the Veskarium led to the cessation of hostilities and tenuous alliance between the two powers. Second, the Swarm is the direct ancestor of the shirren species. Having broken away sometime during the Gap, shirrens found succor with the deity Hylax, who was believed to have evolved from a pre-Swarm kucharn and who turned her back on the ravenous entity the Swarm has evolved into.

In recent years, the Swarm had been seen as little more than a distant boogeyman with no activities save for classified actions in the Suskillon system in the Vast. However, the recent discovery of the missing Szandite world of Krethiskar inadvertently awoke a dormant subcolony that had been responsible for the world's consumption. Since then, the newly dubbed Krethiskar subcolony has become a scourge of Near Space, engaged in an active invasion of Szandite Collective worlds and poised to spill over into other powers like the Veskarium and Pact Worlds. This rediscovered subcolony has begun rapid evolution of its components and remains cut off from the wider Swarm, but it still represents a clear danger to Near Space.

Void Palm.name: Void Palm

Antigrav Interference.name: Antigrav Interference
Antigrav Interference.description: 120 feet. The void palm causes antigravitational technology to malfunction. Upon entering this aura, any items or objects with the tech trait that employ some form of antigrav tech, including antigrav harnesses as well as most hover vehicles and starships, become glitching 1.

Disorienting Patterns.name: Disorienting Patterns
Disorienting Patterns.description:
Trigger A creature with darkvision within 60 feet of the void palm begins its turn

Requirements The void palm is in darkness

Effect The patterns on a void palm's mottled red fronds resemble a distant star field when viewed in the darkness. The void palm sways its fronds in a disorienting manner. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and becomes immune to Disorienting Patterns for 24 hours.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} until the end of the void palm's next turn.

Failure As success, and the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to the void palm's melee Strikes while it's dazzled.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 minute

Double Lash.name: Double Lash
Double Lash.description: The void palm makes two sweeping frond Strikes against the same target, each using its current multiple attack penalty. If both attacks hit, combine their damage. This counts as two attacks when calculating the void palm's multiple attack penalty.

Gravity Void.name: Gravity Void
Gravity Void.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect The void palm creates a zero-gravity area of darkness in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20], negating the effects of gravity and non-magical light sources in the area. Magical light sources in the area work normally. The void palm is unaffected by this lack of gravity, but other creatures that enter or start their turn in the area become untethered unless they can move normally in zero-gravity environments; they're also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} while untethered in the area. An untethered creature attempting to Push Off in the area must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] or the action is disrupted. The Gravity Void lasts for 10 minutes.

Void Palm.publicNotes:
A void palm poses a danger to sentient creatures and their antigravity technology alike. The plant propagates slowly yet steadily by catapulting an annual yield of brilliant blue seeds spaceward. The imparted momentum provides sufficient velocity for most seeds, aided by their supernatural ability to ignore gravity, to escape into the depths of space. Each seed drifts unremittingly until it encounters an object suitable for sustaining it, whether a wandering asteroid, planetary body, or sizable starship.

Once embedded in a fitting surface, a void palm seed germinates rapidly. Upon sprouting, the plant begins drawing nutrients from its surroundings, which periodically become enriched by the decomposing corpses or the oxidizing scraps of the plant's victims. The plant's fronds routinely sweep its area clear of remains or other debris that could serve as handholds or counterbalance or that could alert potential prey to their peril.

Oma.name: Oma

Chain Lightning.name: 闪电链 Chain Lightning
Chain Lightning.description:
你向目标释放一束强力的闪电，造成8d12电击伤害。目标必须尝试一次基础反射豁免。电流会跳跃到第一个目标30尺的内另一个生物上，接着跳到这个生物30尺内的下一个生物上，以此类推。你可以在任何时候终止连锁。你无法将同一个生物作为目标多于一次，并且你必须与所有的目标间有效果线。只骰一次伤害，并将其应用于每个目标（对于其豁免结果，取半或者加倍）。该连锁反应直到任何一个目标在豁免上大成功为止。

升阶（+1） 该伤害增加1d12。

Overload Systems.name: Overload Systems
Overload Systems.description:
You flood the area with a wave of electrical interference. You deal 7d8 electricity damage to creatures in the area. Each creature in the area must attempt a Reflex save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage, and any of its items with the tech trait become glitching 1 for 1 minute or until the condition is removed.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage, and any of its items with the tech trait become glitching 2 for 1 minute or until the condition is removed.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

Discharge.name: Discharge
Discharge.description:
You ignite a sudden spark of electricity that surges into a technological creature or object. You de-energize an item with the tech trait or disrupt a creature with the tech trait, with effects based on the target being a creature or object.

  • Creature If your target is a creature with the tech trait, it attempts a Will save. On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}; on a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}.
  • Object If you target an attended object, the creature with the object attempts a Will save. On a success, the object loses half of its maximum charges. On a failure, or if you target an unattended object, the object loses all its remaining charges.

Starsong.name: Starsong
Starsong.description: While not a language, starsong allows omas to communicate telepathically with one another across space; omas can communicate with other omas in the same star system. Any other creature with telepathy can receive but not necessarily understand starsong at a range of 10,000 miles.

Cavatina.name: Cavatina
Cavatina.description:
Frequency once per hour

Effect The oma sings a telepathic song that encourages its friends and dispirits its foes within 120 feet. Each ally who can hear the song gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks for 1 minute. Each enemy must attempt a @Check[will|dc:35] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 3} on a critical failure), as the song affects their inner ear.

Overloading Shock.name: Overloading Shock
Overloading Shock.description: When an oma scores a critical hit with its electric discharge Strike, the target must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:35|basic] save or all tech items held by the target become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}.

Oma.publicNotes:
Also known as space whales, omas project powerful magical electromagnetic fields that protect them from both vacuum and toxic atmospheres while allowing them to feed. Omas gain sustenance from the particulate rings and dense atmospheres of gas giants, although exactly which substances are nutritious to an oma is still unknown. Studying a feeding oma isn't without danger. Omas sometimes accidentally swallow drones and small starships, such as fighters and interceptors, and they've been known to do so deliberately when provoked.

Most omas have been observed traveling alone. On occasion, omas form pods of three to five individuals and migrate across systems, on currents and schedules only they know. In very rare cases, omas gather among the rings of a planet in massive numbers. Their energy fields intermingle and react with local gases to light up large areas of space in a multicolored show.

Their most enigmatic yet well known feature is their starsong. Traveling through space for billions of miles, the starsong is a haunting telepathic melody. Most people perceive starsong as crooning, slow and mournful. No one can agree on the details, suggesting that each listener hears something different. Attempts to translate starsong have failed thus far, stumping even the most brilliant magic and linguistics experts. Since the Drift Crisis, for unknown reasons pods of omas have begun bombarding Drift beacons with a seemingly new starsong frequency, in some cases causing catastrophic malfunctions.

Wayward Warp.name: Wayward Warp

Void Whispers.name: Void Whispers
Void Whispers.description:
The target hears ominous sounds containing unspeakable mysteries from beyond the stars that disorient its mind. The target attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The target hears the strange whispers, becoming @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The target begins to wander aimlessly, using each action to Step in a random direction. When it moves, the target takes the safest route and doesn't enter hazardous terrain. The spell has a duration of 1 minute or until a creature uses a hostile action against the target.

Critical Failure As failure, except the target babbles incoherently. Any creature that begins its turn within 20 feet of the target must attempt a Will saving throw. On a failure, it must use its first action on its next turn to Step in a random direction and becomes immune to this spell for 24 hours. This is an incapacitation effect.

Illusory Creature.name: 幻象生物 Illusory Creature
Illusory Creature.description:
你创造一个大型或者更小的生物的幻象。它发出适当的声音，气味，触摸起来也非常可信。如果你和幻象相距超过500尺，法术结束。

幻象不能说话，但是你可以使用你的动作来通过该生物说话，法术会适当地掩饰你的声音。你可能要尝试欺骗或者表演检定来模仿该生物，由GM决定。如果你想表现一个特定的人，并与一个知晓该人的人交互时，很可能会如此。

在战斗中，该幻象每回合能使用2动作，它在你维持该法术时使用这些动作。它使用你的法术攻击调整值为其攻击骰，使用你的法术DC为其AC。其豁免骰调整值等于你的法术DC-10。它足够结实，以至于可以夹击其他生物。如果幻象被攻击命中，或者在豁免中失败，法术结束。

该幻术可以通过令目标相信来造成伤害，但它不能直接影响现实世界。如果幻象生物的打击命中，目标承受@Damage[3d4[mental]]伤害。这是一个心灵效果。幻象的打击是非致命的。如果伤害与怪物的幻象不对应——举例而言，如果幻象巨型龙只造成了5点伤害——GM可以允许目标通过一个自由动作尝试一次察觉检定来不相信法术。只要目标认为能，任何有关的抗力和弱点都会生效，这由GM判定。举例而言，如果幻影持用一把战锤，攻击一个具有钝击抗力的生物，该生物承受的心灵伤害会变少。但是，幻觉伤害不会抑制再生，或者触发其他需要特定伤害类型的能力。GM应当记录幻像造成的幻觉伤害。

当生物触摸幻像，或者使用@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{搜索 Seek}动作来检查它时，该生物可以尝试不相信你的幻术。当生物不相信幻术时，它回复从该幻术承受的伤害的一半（如果有），并且不会再因该幻术而承受伤害。

升阶（+1） 幻像的打击伤害增加1d4，你可以创造的生物最大体型增大一级（最大为巨型）。

Stupefy.name: 愚者之思 Stupefy
Stupefy.description:
你令目标精神迟钝，基于其意志豁免。

大成功 目标不受影响。

成功 目标获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{呆滞 Stupefied 1}，持续1分钟。

失败 目标获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{呆滞 Stupefied 2}，持续1分钟。

大失败 目标获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{呆滞 Stupefied 3}，持续1分钟。

Dizzying Colors.name: 眩彩喷射 Dizzying Colors
Dizzying Colors.description:
你释放出大量旋转变化着的色彩，根据生物的意志豁免受到影响。

大成功 该生物不受影响。

成功 该生物@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{目眩 Dazzled}1轮。

失败 该生物@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{震慑 Stunned 1}，@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{目盲 Blinded}1轮，并目眩1分钟。

大失败 该生物震慑1轮，并目盲1分钟。

Dream of Home.name: Dream of Home
Dream of Home.description: You draw on a creature's thoughts of home and the comfort it finds in that concept, overwhelming the creature with wistful memories of whatever home means to it. The target must succeed at a Will save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by the memories.

Eldritch Lance.name: Eldritch Lance
Eldritch Lance.description:
You condense a beam of mind-assaulting eldritch energy from the dark spaces between the stars and fire it at a target. Make a ranged spell attack roll against the target's AC. If you hit, you deal 2d6 mental damage. On a critical success, you deal double damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d6.

Figment.name: 虚构幻术 Figment
Figment.description:
你创造出简单的声音或图像的幻术。声音会为法术添加听觉特征，且不能包含能被理解的单词或复杂的音乐。图像会为法术添加视觉特征，但是它不能大于5尺边长的立方体，且在15尺的距离内观察会发现它的构造明显很粗糙且缺乏细节。当你施放或维持该法术时，你能尝试用幻术让人@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.GkmbTGfg8KcgynOA]{分神 Create a Diversion}，这会为你的欺骗检定提供+2环境加值。若对某个生物进行的此尝试失败了，该生物就不会相信虚构幻术。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.2Hg3a06gZCSnUgSA]{法术效果：虚构幻术}

Illusory Object.name: 幻象造物 Illusory Object
Illusory Object.description:
你创造一个固定物体的视觉幻象。该幻象必须能完全处于法术区域中。该物体会自然活动，但是不会发出声音或者产生气味。举例而言，幻象瀑布中会出现水并流下，但是它会是安静的。

当生物触摸幻像，或者使用@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{搜索 Seek}动作来检查它时，该生物可以尝试不相信你的幻术。

升阶（2环级） 你的幻象也会发出合适的声音，产生正常气味，并且摸起来也很正常。该法术获得听觉和嗅觉特征。持续时间增加到1小时。

升阶（5环级） 如2环级，但是持续时间为无限。

Seek the Stars.name: Seek the Stars
Seek the Stars.description:
In your mind's eye, you chart a path through the cosmos. If you're in Pact Worlds space, you immediately know which direction to travel in order to reach the Starstone on Absalom Station. If you're outside the Pact System, you know the direction to the nearest Drift beacon or the Starstone.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.

Telekinetic Hand.name: 念动之手 Telekinetic Hand
Telekinetic Hand.description:
你创造出一只漂浮着的魔法手掌，它可以隐形或者宛如幽灵，它能抓住目标物体并向着任意方向缓慢漂浮至多20尺。当你维持法术时，你能够将物体移动额外20尺。若物体位于空中之时法术结束，那么该物体便会坠落。

升阶（3环级） 你能以一个1负载或更轻的无主物体为目标。

升阶（5环级） 法术射程增加至60尺，而且你能以一个1负载或更轻的无主物体为目标。

升阶（7环级） 法术射程增加至60尺，而且你能以一个2负载或更轻的无主物体为目标。

Void Warp.name: 虚能噬 Void Warp
Void Warp.description:
你唤来虚能伤害生命力量。该目标承受2d4虚能伤害，这有一次基础强韧豁免. 如果该生物在豁免中大失败，他也会@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{力竭 Enfeebled 1}直到你得下个回合开始。

升阶（+1） 该伤害增加1d4。

Warp Sense.name: Warp Sense
Warp Sense.description: A wayward warp can sense creatures within their warp field as a precise sense at the listed range.

Reality Shimmer.name: Reality Shimmer
Reality Shimmer.description: The wayward warp has concealment against attacks originating outside of their warp field.

Warp Field.name: Warp Field
Warp Field.description:
15 feet. Reality within the area becomes a different type of terrain. Structures and creatures in the area aren't transformed, and the change immediately ends when the aura moves or the wayward warp dies. The nature of the aura is chosen randomly using the table below each time the wayward warp moves. A wayward warp is immune to their own warp field's effects and to the aligned damage type, but they gain weakness 5 to the opposing damage type.

[[/r 1d6]]{D6}FIELDALIGNED DAMAGEOPPOSING DAMAGEFIELD EFFECT
1CosmicVoidVitalityZero gravity
2EarthPiercingAcidDifficult terrain (sand)
3InfernoFireColdHazardous terrain (@Damage[1d6[fire]])
4StormElectricityBludgeoningHazardous terrain (@Damage[1d6[electricity]])
5FrozenColdFireUneven ground (ice)
