When the target computer or device is accessed, this implanted data is the first data the accessing creature gains, regardless of what data the creature is looking for. Implanted data doesn't prevent a creature from accessing other information stored on a device, change how the device functions, or otherwise inhibit the device from functioning. If this spell's duration ends before a creature accesses the implanted data, the information vanishes.

Heightened (3rd) The duration is one year.

Heightened (6th) The duration is unlimited.

Measure.name: Measure
Measure.description:
You learn the approximate measurements (length, width, depth, height, and weight) of creatures and objects you can observe in the area. If an object or creature extends out of the area, you learn only the measurements within the area, but nothing about what you can't observe. For example, you can't determine the thickness or weight of a wall you can observe only one side of, or the depth of water that extends beyond the spell's range. Each time you Sustain the spell, you learn the exact measurements of one creature or object in the area, including imprecisions or anomalies that might indicate structural weakness. Magically altered targets give you information that reflects what you observe rather than accurate measurements.

Heightened (+1) Extend the range of measure by 100 feet.

Scan Environment.name: Scan Environment
Scan Environment.description:
You analyze the natural environment in the area within 1 mile, learning general information about the atmosphere, biomes, and gravity. You forecast the weather for the next hour, including the direction it will come from; learn which atmospheres, biomes, and gravity are in the area, as well as the direction and distance to those elements; and learn if anything in the area presents an environmental hazard, including cold or heat dangers (at what temperature range), radiation (at what severity), smoke effects, and underwater conditions. This is enough to detect large effects that span spaces over 30 feet in diameter only. You gain a general sense of where and how far away these dangers are.

Heightened (+1) The scanned area increases in radius by 1 mile.

Input Overload Aura.name: Input Overload Aura
Input Overload Aura.description: 20 feet. Enemies in the aura take a –1 status penalty to AC and all saves against sonic effects or effects that alter written or stored data. A creature within this aura attempting to read a document must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] or cannot read the document for the next [[/gmr 1d4+1]]{1d4+1 rounds}.

Pedant Annoyance.name: Pedant Annoyance
Pedant Annoyance.description: Typodaemons paradoxically despise having their errors pointed out. If a creature Interacts to correct the grammar or syntax of something the typodaemon has written or is writing, including with a spell such as implant data, the typodaemon becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}. While sickened this way, the daemon must spend an action to justify its actions instead of retching, attempting a @Check[will] save instead to remove the condition.

Error Injection.name: Error Injection
Error Injection.description: The typodaemon remotely accesses the code of a visible item with the tech trait within 60 feet and injects erroneous data. The item becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}; if the item is in possession of a creature, the creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18|options:inflicts:glitching] save, negating the effect on a success. The item is then immune to Error Injection for 1 hour.

Typodaemon.publicNotes:
These child-sized daemons represent the horrors of death through dictation errors, transcription woes, or incorrect copying and pasting of text. Typodaemons make discreet adjustments to text, changing a word, adding a taunting emote, or adjusting punctuation to create entirely new meanings for written words. These changes lead to conflicts, and the typodaemon thrives on the strife and potential for death because of these simple errors.

Hailing from the plane of Abaddon, daemons are utterly devoted to the destruction of all life. Their existence is based around specific means of death, and they seek the death of all things in service to their lords: the Apocalypse Riders. Each daemon represents a different way to die, and they use their specific powers to further that means of death.

Advents in technology have only created new means of death and torment, so new types of daemons enter existence with regularity, each created once a sufficient mass of new deaths occurs to spawn them. These new advent daemons often prefer to spend their time in the galaxy rather than Abaddon, as they can better spread their torments to the mortals of creation.

Emvermod.name: Emvermod

Truespeech (Constant).name: Truespeech (Constant)
Truespeech (Constant).description:
无论什么语言，目标都可以理解其所有的词语，并且向其他生物用该语言交流。当处于一群各异的生物中时，每次目标将要说话，他可以选择一个生物，并使用一种该生物理解的语言来讲述，即使该目标不知道这语言具体是什么。

升阶（7环级） 持续时间为8小时。

Analyze Target.name: Analyze Target
Analyze Target.description:
You focus on the target, gathering data as magical holograms display information. When the casting is complete, you learn biometric information about the target, including its fingerprints, medical conditions, and other basic physiological information. You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.AJstokjdG6iDjVjE]{Impersonate} the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds} on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.7r6mw8H87SUaj9Tq]{Spell Effect: Analyze Target}

Heightened (3rd) The status bonus increases to +2, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +3, and you can target any number of creatures.

Daze.name: 晕眩术 Daze
Daze.description:
你侵入目标的精神，利用精神冲击令目标晕眩。该冲击造成1d6心灵伤害，具有基础意志豁免。如果目标在豁免中大失败，它获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{震慑 Stunned 1}。

升阶（+2） 该伤害增加1d6。

Delete (At Will).name: Delete (At Will)
Delete (At Will).description:
You delete data from a device or object with a flamboyant magical gesture. This data might be handwritten, printed, or digital. You delete up to 1,500 words' worth of text, one page of content, or one display screen worth of text or other visual information. Non-magical writing in or on an unattended or held object is automatically deleted. Attempt a counteract check to delete digital or magical writing. If you fail to delete writing that's part of a hazard, you trigger the hazard. This can't be used to delete the contents of a spellbook or similar means of storing magical formulas or spells.

If you target an attended tech item with the tracking trait, attempt a counteract check. On a success, the item becomes glitching 1. A creature holding the item can Interact to restart the software, reducing the glitching value by 1.

Heightened (7th) You can delete all the data stored locally on a single device or book, or all the data on a device or book related to a specific name, topic, or category.

Implant Data.name: Implant Data
Implant Data.description:
You reprogram a computer or device to display a chosen piece of data when accessed. You can implant up to one piece of data per spell rank. A piece of data can be a simple fact, such as a creature or object's location or physical description; an image of a creature or object; or a simple statement of no more than 300 characters. When the target computer or device is accessed, this implanted data is the first data the accessing creature gains, regardless of what data the creature is looking for. Implanted data doesn't prevent a creature from accessing other information stored on a device, change how the device functions, or otherwise inhibit the device from functioning. If this spell's duration ends before a creature accesses the implanted data, the information vanishes.

Heightened (3rd) The duration is one year.

Heightened (6th) The duration is unlimited.

Motivating Ringtone.name: Motivating Ringtone
Motivating Ringtone.description:
You cause the target comm unit to play a cosmically composed personal theme song specific to its owner. The creature who owns the target comm unit regains 1d8+4 Hit Points when you Cast the Spell, a +5-foot status bonus to all Speeds, and a +1 status bonus to saves against fear effects for the duration as long as the comm unit continues to play the ringtone.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.NmhqKZyMgQSHbyHj]{Spell Effect: Motivating Ringtone}

Heightened (+1) The amount of healing increases by 1d8+4.

Comm Unit.name: Comm Unit
Comm Unit.description:
A personal comm unit is a pocket-sized device that combines a minor portable computer and a cellular communications device, allowing instantaneous wireless communication with other comm units in both audio- and text-based formats at planetary range. A personal comm unit also includes a calculator, a flashlight (emits bright light in a 5-foot radius around you and dim light in the next 5 feet), a browser that accesses any local infospheres, and several entertainment options (including games).

Personal comm units integrated into armor have some voice-activated capabilities; you can communicate with other comm units in audio-based formats and receive responses without needing a free hand, but typing a message, browsing the infosphere, or using an app requires the use of at least one hand.

Comm units that function at longer ranges aren't as portable; they include built-in generators that provide the necessary amount of power and rely on active Drift beacons to communicate. Short-term use of these units can be purchased in most major settlements.

Dutiful Moderator.name: Dutiful Moderator
Dutiful Moderator.description: An online community moderated by an emvermod never experiences random bugs or downtime. Any checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Yn4jLPVWVE1vtAaF]{Access Infosphere}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.rmwa3OyhTZ2i2AHl]{Craft} virtual items, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.QyzlsLrqM0EEwd7j]{Earn Income}, or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.RF8xNJ8QsMwogerB]{Hack} using the community as a tool receive a +2 circumstance bonus. If the emvermod is unfriendly, such checks take a –2 circumstance penalty instead. An emvermod must spend a week in an online space to become a moderator.

Modvision.name: Modvision
Modvision.description: An emvermod can sense items and creatures with active accounts on any community they moderate as an imprecise sense at the listed range. They recognize community members, even if they use an infosphere alias.

Shy.name: Shy
Shy.description: An emvermod is naturally invisible while holding a comm unit or in a virtual space. They don't appear to have an account in any infosphere space they moderate. They can become visible, or even selectively visible, allowing only specific people to see them.

Shadow Ban.name: Shadow Ban
Shadow Ban.description:
Frequency once per 10 minutes

Effect A creature within 60 feet with a comm unit or other computer connected to an infosphere must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save as the emvermod tries to ban it from communicating.

Critical Success No effect, and the creature is temporarily immune to Shadow Ban for 1 day.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature can't use auditory actions or communicate, even by gesturing, generating text, or using telepathy. This also prevents the target from casting spells due to the magical words involved in casting, with the exception of subtle spells. This effect lasts for 1 minute, or until the moderator fey retracts the Shadow Ban or dies, whichever comes first.

Critical Failure As failure, except this effect lasts 24 hours.

Emvermod.publicNotes:
A form of house spirit, the emvermod, or "moderator fey," is a shy resident of one or more online communities who helps keep the online space vibrant, engaged, and orderly. They often take on the appearance of their avatar, choosing one that fits with the other members of their infosphere niche.

When given proper respect, an emvermod is a diligent administrator who helps an online community thrive while working alongside other moderators in the online space. When disrespected or accused of causing harm to the community, they cause all sorts of mischief, such as deleting posts, shadow banning members, and even imitating other users.

Repair-Class Security Robot.name: Repair-Class Security Robot

Exigency.name: Exigency
Exigency.description:
Frequency once every 10 minutes

Trigger A creature targets the security robot with an attack

Effect The robot overclocks its processing power to attempt to avoid an attack, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to its AC against the triggering attack.

Fire Suppressor.name: Fire Suppressor
Fire Suppressor.description: The robot sprays a torrent of water that functions as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.jfVCuOpzC6mUrf6f]{Hydraulic Push} (1st-rank, DC 16). The robot must refill its tanks after using this ability.

Repair-Class Security Robot.publicNotes:
On many scrappy starships and remote space stations, it's hard to afford dedicated security robots, especially if you can't manage to secure a license to own them in the first place. Most such operations rely on cheap maintenance bots to step up and repel intruders—they might not get the job done against serious threats, but they might be able to take out common vermin before they can cause too much damage to the starship's circuitry. After all, sometimes the best maintenance is preventive maintenance.

Security robots are ubiquitous throughout industrialized space and have major presences in both the Pact Worlds and Veskarium, employed and customized by corporations and private citizens alike.

While most soldier robot models are built to be as destructive as possible, and elimination robots are designed to kill, security robots usually have fail-safes programmed into their behavior to prevent them from causing any more violence than is necessary to perform their designated security function. This makes them especially useful in cities, where unintended destruction might harm bystanders or cause legal issues, and even on starships, where a stray laser blast might cause serious damage to vital starship systems.

Garaggakal Polymath.name: Garaggakal Polymath

Emotion Sense.name: Emotion Sense
Emotion Sense.description: The garaggakal polymath senses all creatures that are under emotion effects as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

Sense Through.name: Sense Through
Sense Through.description: The garaggakal's imprecise senses function through most materials and walls. The senses are blocked by energy fields (such as force fields and force effects) and sufficiently dense material: 10 feet of wood or plastic, 5 feet of stone, 1 foot of common metal, or 6 inches of lead or any starmetal.

Analyzed Strike.name: Analyzed Strike
Analyzed Strike.description:
Requirements The garaggakal's last action damaged a living creature with a jaws Strike

Effect The garaggakal uses their extensive knowledge of anatomy to dig into the wound, causing agonizing pain to the target. The garaggakal attempts a jaws Strike against the required creature, using the same multiple attack penalty as the previous Strike. On a hit, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical hit). This counts as two Strikes for the garaggakal's multiple attack penalty.

Draining Saliva.name: Draining Saliva
Draining Saliva.description: A creature that takes damage from a garaggakal's spit Strike must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:36] save. On a failure, it's @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} on a critical failure).

Leech Life.name: Leech Life
Leech Life.description:
Requirements A living creature within 30 feet of the garaggakal has the drained condition

Effect The garaggakal amplifies the drain on the required creature's essential life force, dealing @Damage[10d6[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:38|basic] save). The garaggakal gains temporary Hit Points equal to half the damage dealt that last 1 minute. Regardless of the result of the save, the garaggakal then Flies up to 15 feet closer to the required creature, and the required creature becomes temporarily immune to Leech Life for 24 hours.

Phase Through.name: Phase Through
Phase Through.description: A garaggakal can shift out of phase with reality and thus can't be held by walls or cages. The garaggakal moves through up to 15 feet of solid matter. They must end their turn outside of the solid matter or take @Damage[5d6[bludgeoning]] damage. A garaggakal can't phase through force effects.

Garaggakal Polymath.publicNotes:
These garaggakals have lived for centuries and reached sizes greater than 15 feet tall and 1,000 pounds. Garaggakal polymaths have spent their many years tormenting and tearing apart all the creatures they've encountered, and they have extensive recollection of anatomy of a wide variety of living things. They're even crueler than younger garaggakals, taking great delight in causing excruciating pain to their victims before stealing their life force. They travel much further through the Drift than their younger counterparts, driven by their insatiable desire to find and dissect new creatures.

The Drift is a strange place, and the creatures native to it are stranger still. Garaggakals prove no exception. Shaped vaguely like humanoids, they have arms and legs but also sallow, rubbery skin with long, scaly tails and gauzelike wings. Most horrifyingly, their faces are almost entirely taken up by their enormous, funnel-like mouth, lined with sharp teeth. While they use these mouths for the obvious purpose of biting, they can also use its powerful suction to draw actual life force from their prey.

Also known as "Drift wraiths," garaggakals hunt down any living creatures that linger too long in the Drift. For garaggakals, anything that isn't a fellow garaggakal is prey. However, they're no mere mindless hunters. Garaggakals are surprisingly intelligent and prefer to dissect victims, learning all about them before eating them. Often, garaggakals take their victims apart while they're still alive. Xenobiologists have determined that garaggakals don't even technically need to eat to survive, so their relentless hunger is seemingly psychological. They do require the energy of the Drift to survive in the absence of food, however, and further need the energy of the Drift to reproduce, seeking out areas of intense radiation to absorb and then build a new offspring from their own cells. Their children are fully grown at creation.

Garaggakals surprise their prey. They are quite stealthy and can even phase through solid matter, suddenly appearing on the inside of a starship—bad news for whatever living creatures happen to be inside. Con artists sell garaggakal repelling devices for travelers to use while in the Drift to prevent such visits.

Terrible Roar.name: Terrible Roar

Thundering Roar.name: 雷鸣咆哮 Thundering Roar
Thundering Roar.description:
Trigger A creature takes a hostile action against the kyokor, produces a visible area effect, or flies within 120 feet of the kyokor

Effect The kyokor roars, producing a @Template[type:cone|distance:120] of sound in the direction of the triggering creature. Unattended objects of 5 or less Hardness immediately gain the broken condition, and concrete and metal surfaces warp into difficult terrain. Creatures in the area take @Damage[20d6[sonic]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:44|basic] save). On a failure, a creature also takes @Damage[3d6[persistent,mental]|options:area-damage] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

Terrible Roar.hazarddescription:
The kyokor unleashes a terrible roar.

When a kyokor passes by or through a settlement—or even attacks!—it leaves a trail of destruction that can be represented by the following hazards.

Terrifying, skyscraper-sized beasts roam the wastelands of Daimalko. Called colossi by the population, these monsters erupted from deep within the earth to stomp cities into dust, decimating the population and destroying every surface civilization on the planet in a terrifying Awakening. Kyokors are among the most dreaded colossi. There's no hope of defeating one without a valko—a war machine created by Daimalko's stubborn survivors to fight the colossi.
Terrible Roar.reset: The kyokor must wait [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds} before roaring again.

Crest-Eater.name: Crest-Eater

Bone Tracker.name: Bone Tracker
Bone Tracker.description: A crest-eater can use its scent as a precise sense at the listed range against creatures that have taken damage from crest-eater venom in the past hour.

Crest-Eater Venom.name: Crest-Eater Venom
Crest-Eater Venom.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:21]

Max Duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d6[acid]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[1d8[acid]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[1d10[acid]] damage, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}, and enfeebled 2 (1 round)

Scree Strider.name: Scree Strider
Scree Strider.description: Crest-eaters ignore difficult terrain caused by rubble, sand, and uneven ground made of earth and stone.

Crest-Eater.publicNotes:
Crest-eaters are large four-legged beasts with traits of both reptiles and mammals. Originating from the distant world of Kasath, they've spread throughout the cosmos, accompanying numerous kasathas and even having a small enclave aboard the Idari worldship in the Pact Worlds. A crest-eater is a predator known for consuming the bones of their prey. They use a highly corrosive acidic venom that eats through minerals with ease. Their name comes from their history of tracking down kasathas and cracking open their prey's elongated skulls to feed on the "bony crests" within. As crest-eaters consume bones and other substances, they produce large mineral protrusions across their bodies. Crest-eaters are social creatures and often form prides to hunt together or join with other nearby species if they're accepted—it's not uncommon to find a starship crew or local raider gang with one or more crest-eaters they've adopted. Tamers who have hunted with crest-eaters for generations craft a ceremonial cestus from their comrade's body after a crest-eater passes, a tradition still practiced to this day despite the high value and demand for crest-eater bones.

Most crest-eaters can grow as long as 14 feet and about 6 feet tall (not including their noticeable mineral protrusions). Thanks to their mineral-laden composition, they often weigh in excess of 1,000 pounds, somewhat hampering their mobility in environments that can't support their girth. Young and mature crest-eaters tend to stay together in small prides, though some act as solitary hunters in environments where food sources are scarce. Especially large males frequently become increasingly aggressive as they age, and they're often driven away by the rest of the pride for both their hostility and gluttonous appetites. If these solitary beasts survive long enough, they can become unpredictable and dangerous threats that attempt to establish territory and find food by attacking desert settlements. Many kasathan epics feature one or more brave warriors defending their village against an ancient male crest-eater. Whether dispatched by local legends or eventually dying off in secret, these oversized crest-eaters can leave behind a valuable cache of minerals for enterprising adventurers.

Free Captain.name: Free Captain

进阶 Phase Cutlass.name: 进阶 Phase Cutlass
进阶 Phase Cutlass.description:
Upgrades: 1

A phase cutlass resembles a traditional curved sword until it's powered. Then its blade morphs into a shimmering void, with a tiny star for each life it ended.

Starfall Pistol.name: Starfall Pistol
Starfall Pistol.description:
Upgrades: 1

While originally a kit-bashed plasma caster, the commercial models fire streams of superheated plasma. Akitonian revelers enjoy discharging these pistols into the air during outdoor celebrations.

Freebooter Armor.name: Freebooter Armor
Freebooter Armor.description:
Upgrades: 1

Freebooter armor is named for the pirates and outlaws who often wear these outfits. Freebooter armor typically consists of a breastplate worn under a jacket or flight suit, heavy boots and gloves, a helmet, and hidden straps and holsters for concealing weapons.

Seashell Religious Symbol of Besmara.name: Seashell Religious Symbol of Besmara
Seashell Religious Symbol of Besmara.description: This piece of wood or silver is emblazoned with an image representing a deity. Some divine spellcasters, such as clerics, can use a religious symbol of their deity to use certain abilities and cast some spells. A religious symbol can be worn on the body on a chain or pin, or can be held.

Give 'Em No Quarter!.name: Give 'Em No Quarter!
Give 'Em No Quarter!.description: The Free Captain telepathically orders their crew to mercilessly slaughter one target. All allied creatures of equal or lower level within 60 feet of the Free Captain gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls against one creature until the end of the Free Captain's next turn.

Soul Squall.name: Soul Squall
Soul Squall.description: The stars of the Free Captain's cutlass seem to empower their starfall pistol, which deals an additional @Damage[2d6[void]] damage as long as the Free Captain is holding both weapons.

Stardust Storm.name: Stardust Storm
Stardust Storm.description: The Free Captain uses Area Fire with their starfall pistol split by their phase cutlass, causing the starry darkness to spread. The pistol's Area Fire uses a @Template[type:cone|distance:10] that deals @Damage[2d6[void]|options:area-damage] damage. Creatures that fail their save against the Area Fire become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure).

Free Captain.publicNotes:
Captain is the highest rank on board any vessel. They answer only to admirals of the Free Captains and only if their ship isn't engaged in active combat. This captain uses the void mode of her cutlass by default, the hundreds of shimmering stars in the blade representing the lives taken in her countless engagements.

The Free Captains have been a scourge of interstellar shipping across the Pact Worlds for generations, but since the adoption of Drift Lanes, the outlaws have expanded their reach and scale by targeting ships across the galaxy using the new standardized interstellar shipping routes.

Blight Drake.name: Blight Drake

Wing Deflection.name: Wing Deflection
Wing Deflection.description:
Trigger The blight drake is targeted with an attack from a creature they can see

Effect The blight drake raises their wing, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the blight drake is flying, they descend 10 feet after the attack is complete.

Draconic Frenzy.name: 龙之狂怒 Draconic Frenzy
Draconic Frenzy.description: The drake makes two jaws Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.

Smokestack.name: Smokestack
Smokestack.description:
The blight drake blasts a thick, fetid smog from slits around their neck, creating a cloud of smoke in a @Template[type:burst|distance:20] within 120 feet and that remains for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}. All creatures within the cloud become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}, and all creatures outside the cloud become concealed to creatures in it.

Each creature that begins its turn inside the cloud takes @Damage[5d6[poison]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:sickened] save). A creature that fails the save is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure).

Toxic Breath.name: Toxic Breath
Toxic Breath.description: The blight drake spews a fetid ball of polluted putrescence up to a range of 60 feet that explodes in a @Template[type:burst|distance:15]. Creatures within the burst take @Damage[4d8[acid]|options:area-damage] damage and @Damage[4d8[poison]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] save). The blight drake can't use Toxic Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Toxic Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

Blight Drake.publicNotes:
Blight drakes have a well-earned reputation among draconic creatures for stench and pollution. They corrupt the air around them with the sort of filth and decay commonly found in the worst kinds of industrialization. These solitary monstrosities resemble their swamp-dwelling draconic cousins but are often surrounded with a miasma of thick, choking smog. Blight drakes revel in befouling clean and verdant environments, leaving a permanent trail of filth in their wake.

Drakes resemble their larger draconic cousins in more than just scaled, monstrous acclaim. Ranging in size from the diminutive fey drake to the massive, ship-sized star drake, drakes share a dragons' winged reptilian form, long necks, and breath weapons. But where dragons often have forelimbs and wings, a drake typically only has one or the other. Their long, winding tails and sharp jaws more than make up for this lack, however.

Despite being more animalistic than dragons, drakes still possess a formidable cunning, and most share a dragon's ability to generate some form of breath weapon. However, their lack of intelligence makes drakes substantially easier to domesticate than other draconic creatures, and their familial nature makes them excellent pack hunters.

Appendage Cluster.name: Appendage Cluster

Cluster Onslaught.name: Cluster Onslaught
Cluster Onslaught.description: An appendage cluster attempts an Acrobatics check to [[/act tumble-through]] a creature's space. On a success, the mouths of the cluster gnash and gnaw at the creature, dealing @Damage[3d8[piercing]] damage. If the Acrobatics check was a critical success, the target also takes @Damage[4[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Appendage Cluster.publicNotes: Caypins are brawny predators with the agility of wolves and the scaly skin of crocodiles. Their most striking feature is a mass of wriggling, semi-sentient appendages that cover their heads. Each of these tentacle-like protuberances contains a bloodshot eyeball and a gaping mouth full of teeth, and the caypin—which has no eyes or mouth of its own—relies on these orifices to see and feed. Nutrients absorbed by the appendages are passed to the caypin via tiny receptive sockets in the beast's jaw, and caypins can detach these appendages into writhing clusters that independently move up to 100 feet from their bodies. In the wild, caypins use these clusters to ambush prey, and the creatures are known across the galaxy for their rapacious ability to consume half of their body weight in raw meat.

Tregalxi.name: Tregalxi

Wire Sense.name: Wire Sense
Wire Sense.description: A tregalxi can sense the presence of electrical wires within 30 feet as an imprecise sense.

Insulated Shell.name: Insulated Shell
Insulated Shell.description: When a tregalxi is reduced to less than half their Hit Points, their insulated wire shell breaks, freeing their contained radiation. All electricity damage dealt by the tregalxi instead becomes poison damage. When a creature takes poison damage from a tregalxi, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} for 1 minute with no way to reduce the condition.

Circuit Splice.name: Circuit Splice
Circuit Splice.description: The tregalxi extends their wire frame to all enemies within 30 feet. Each creature takes @Damage[2d6[piercing]] and @Damage[2d6[electricity]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save). The tregalxi can't use Circuit Splice again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Circuit Splice]]{1d4 rounds}.

Short Circuit.name: Short Circuit
Short Circuit.description: When a creature makes direct contact with a tregalxi's wires, the creature takes @Damage[1d6[electricity]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save.

Tregalxi.publicNotes:
Tregalxis generally look like a knotted bundle of electrical wiring. Their true form is a shifting ball of radioactive energy safely nestled within their wired shell. Tregalxis need a near-constant source of power to safely maintain their radiation. They're most often found parasitically integrated into a ship's wiring, secretly siphoning power or wedged into a reactor. They leech power from the chosen source until they become either satiated or bored enough to seek out a new power source.

Theoretically, a wayward tregalxi could find their way into a large ship's power core, a city power grid, or the control center for a satellite, where they would most certainly cause power outages and malfunctions, all while becoming incrementally more powerful and destructive.

Elementals are energetic intrusions from the primal planes of existence. They take many forms, from mindless eddies in the wind or water to complex entities capable of wielding primal magic. The advent of space travel and the progress of technology have allowed even more living embodiments of the elements to manifest in the Universe.

Natural events like wildfires, solar storms, and floods can unleash hordes of unruly elementals on a planet or into a region of space. Sometimes, a gathering of powerful elementals causes a natural disaster on an epic scale, like an off-the-charts solar flare that fries a planet's power grid or a ceaseless storm swirling in a gas giant's atmosphere.

Szynegator Swarm.name: Szynegator Swarm

Motion Sense.name: Motion Sense
Motion Sense.description: An szynegator swarm can sense nearby creatures through vibrations in air or water caused by movement as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

Colony Mind.name: Colony Mind
Colony Mind.description: 30 feet. All allies with colony mind within the listed range of each other are in constant telepathic communication and are aware of all threats any individual knows about. While within range of at least one ally with colony mind, when a creature rolls a success on a save against a mental effect, it gets a critical success instead.

Comm Occlusion.name: Comm Occlusion
Comm Occlusion.description: 40 feet. All electronic and telepathic communication, except for that with the colony mind ability, ceases to function, and affected creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to Perception checks. When three or more szynegator swarms are aware of each other using colony mind, the range of comm occlusion increases to 100 feet. This range increases to planetary when 10 or more szynegator swarms are aware of each other using colony mind.

Sonic Distortion.name: Sonic Distortion
Sonic Distortion.description: Each enemy in the szynegator swarm's space takes @Damage[1d8[sonic]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save).

Szynegator Swarm.publicNotes:
A single szynegator vibrating causes slight vertigo, but when these tiny beetle-like components harmonize, the atmospheric disruption they generate renders most forms of communication inoperable. Szynegators can usually be found in isolated or desolate areas of planets so their number can grow unimpeded. Their presence harbingers a full Swarm invasion.

Developing far out in the Vast from an insectile species known as kucharns, the Swarm is considered the greatest threat in existence for several cultures. Composed of countless subcolonies, each consisting of myriad component subspecies, the Swarm is a vast host that acts in psychic union for the purpose of consuming all matter across the cosmos. Most frightening of all is the Swarm's ability to assimilate the knowledge and DNA of those they consume. Thanks to its pre-Gap development of star travel, the Swarm had already consumed innumerable systems before history short-circuited, and the Swarm's pace only accelerated once it received access to the Signal and the hyperspace dimension known as the Drift.

Worlds touched by the Swarm are drained of their natural resources and rendered barren places known as huskworlds. What outsiders consider to be "Swarm space" is little more than a swathe of lifeless worlds that the Swarm have consumed in its reckless advance. The Swarm so thoroughly strips these worlds that surveyors struggle to find even trace amounts of inorganic resources, such as rare minerals and water. Few of these huskworlds have been thoroughly explored, as oftentimes exploration awakens inert drones, theorized to have been left behind in case the devoured system was ever colonized and repopulated. Pockets of Swarm activity exist well beyond this region of space, as the Swarm sent out countless subcolonies in the ages before the Gap, and even now, only some of them have reached their intended destinations.

The Swarm is perhaps most known in the current era for two important facts. First, its assault on the Pact Worlds and the Veskarium led to the cessation of hostilities and tenuous alliance between the two powers. Second, the Swarm is the direct ancestor of the shirren species. Having broken away sometime during the Gap, shirrens found succor with the deity Hylax, who was believed to have evolved from a pre-Swarm kucharn and who turned her back on the ravenous entity the Swarm has evolved into.

In recent years, the Swarm had been seen as little more than a distant boogeyman with no activities save for classified actions in the Suskillon system in the Vast. However, the recent discovery of the missing Szandite world of Krethiskar inadvertently awoke a dormant subcolony that had been responsible for the world's consumption. Since then, the newly dubbed Krethiskar subcolony has become a scourge of Near Space, engaged in an active invasion of Szandite Collective worlds and poised to spill over into other powers like the Veskarium and Pact Worlds. This rediscovered subcolony has begun rapid evolution of its components and remains cut off from the wider Swarm, but it still represents a clear danger to Near Space.

Akashic Dragon (Ancient).name: Akashic Dragon (Ancient)

Foresight.name: Foresight
Foresight.description:
你获得一种能知晓法术目标即将遇到的危险的第六感。如果对于法术目标你选择一个生物而不是自身，你会创造一个精神链接；通过该链接你能告知目标危险将至。该链接是一个心灵效果。由于该法术需要你处理大量的信息，你无法令超过一个预警术同时生效。再次施放预警术会结束之前的预警术。当预警术生效时，目标在其先攻骰上获得+2状态加值，并且对 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{无踪 Undetected} 生物或者被夹击时不视为@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VRSef5y1LmL2Hkjf]{Undetected}。除此之外，你获得下列反应动作： @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.VOEWhPQfN3lvHivK]{预警 Foresight}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.X9Na6IK8FSPcTuoc]{法术效果：预警术}

Telekinetic Tantrum.name: Telekinetic Tantrum
Telekinetic Tantrum.description:
You launch a barrage of powerful psychokinetic energy that overpowers creatures, hurls objects, and deals damage. When you Cast this Spell and the first time each round you Sustain the spell, you can choose one of the following effects. You can't choose the same effect twice in a row.

  • Back Off! You hurl up to 15 objects or creatures up to 40 feet in any direction (including up or down). You can hurl up to a total weight of 40 Bulk per creature or object. Creatures take bludgeoning damage based on the distance they moved and land @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}, as though they'd taken falling damage. Creatures that succeed on a Fortitude save against the spell avoid being moved.
  • Hard Rain You pummel all creatures in the area with debris and objects in the area, dealing 5d10 bludgeoning damage with a basic Reflex save.
  • Psychokinetic Slam Your psychokinetic storm slams into all creatures in the area. Make a ranged spell attack roll. All targets you hit take 7d6 force damage and are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Interplanar Teleport (at will; self only.name: Interplanar Teleport (at will; self only; to and from Akashic Record and the Universe only)
Interplanar Teleport (at will; self only.description:
需求 你具有目标位面的位面密钥用于给法术定位

你和你的盟友跨越位面之间的屏障进行旅行。目标移动至其他位面，比如火元素位面、幽界、或者外层裂隙。你必须知晓目标位面的存在，而且需要使用由该位面的物质制作的魔法位面密钥来给法术定位。就如同跨界传送这个法术本身一样，大多数著名位面的位面密钥具有罕见稀有度，而鲜为人知的位面及半位面通常具有稀有、甚至有可能时独特稀有度的位面密钥。

该法术极其不精准，你会出现在目标之一（由你选择）上一次造访该位面时的位置周边，误差为1d20 x 25英里。若所有目标都是初次造访某个特定位面，你们就会出现在该位面的随机位置。跨界传送不会提供返回的手段，不过除非发生了什么另外情况，否则再次施放跨界传送能让你返回自身之前身处的位面。

Truesight.name: 真景术 Truesight
Truesight.description: 你可以看见60尺内的事物的本来面貌。GM秘密骰反制检定，对抗区域中的所有幻术或者变形效果，但这只会决定你是否能看破它（例如，如果该检定成功对抗了一个变形法术，你可以看见该生物的本来形态，但你不会结束该变形法术）。

Truespeech (constant).name: Truespeech (constant)
Truespeech (constant).description:
无论什么语言，目标都可以理解其所有的词语，并且向其他生物用该语言交流。当处于一群各异的生物中时，每次目标将要说话，他可以选择一个生物，并使用一种该生物理解的语言来讲述，即使该目标不知道这语言具体是什么。

升阶（7环级） 持续时间为8小时。

Clairvoyance.name: 鹰眼术 Clairvoyance
Clairvoyance.description: 你在射程内创造出一个@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{隐形 Invisible}的悬浮眼睛（它甚至可以在你的视觉线或者效果线之外）。这只眼睛不能移动，但是你可以从该处看到所有方向，如同使用你通常的视觉感官。

Confusion.name: 困惑术 Confusion
Confusion.description:
你使用奇怪的能量波动扰乱了目标的思维，令其随机行动。法术效果由目标的意志豁免决定。你可以解消该法术。

大成功 目标不受影响。

成功 目标胡言乱语，并且获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{震慑 Stunned 1}。

失败 目标获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{困惑 Confused}1分钟。在其每个回合结束，它能尝试一次新豁免来结束困惑术。

大失败 目标困惑1分钟，并且没有提前结束的豁免。

升阶（8环级） 你能以至多10个生物为目标。

Mind Reading.name: 读心术 Mind Reading
Mind Reading.description:
你通过一次短暂的心灵接触来尝试阅读目标的想法。它必须尝试一次意志豁免。目标会暂时免疫你的读心术1小时。

大成功 目标得知当施放该法术时你模糊的表层想法。

成功 你得知目标的智力调整值是高于，等于，还是低于你。

失败 你得知当施放该法术时目标模糊的表层想法，并且你得知目标的智力是高于，等于，还是低于你。

大失败 如失败，并且在法术持续时间中，你可以维持该法术来再次侦测目标的表层想法。目标不会获得另外的豁免。

Akashic Download (at will).name: Akashic Download (at will)
Akashic Download (at will).description:
You execute a psychic search of the Akashic Record for information about a specific topic and magically transfer this data to your comm unit or computer. When you Cast this Spell, choose any skill in which you're trained that has the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} action.

Any time during the duration, you can consult your comm unit while attempting a Recall Knowledge check using your chosen skill. This check gains a +1 status bonus. Checking your comm unit is part of the action to Recall Knowledge. You must be holding the comm unit to do so. The comm unit instantly compiles and displays snippets and selections from various media relevant to the topic, which grants you the bonus. If you roll a critical failure on this check, you get a failure instead. If the check is successful and the subject is a creature, you gain additional information or context about the creature. Once you reference the comm unit, the spell ends. When the spell ends, your access to the Akashic Record ends, and all the information you downloaded returns to the Akashic Record.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.h1i8c47U0IOrMrHJ]{Spell Effect: Akashic Download}

Heightened (3rd) You can reference your comm unit three times before the spell ends.

Akashic Eye.name: Akashic Eye
Akashic Eye.description: Instead of using their own memories, an Akashic dragon can ascertain information from the Akashic Records. This gives it the Akashic Record Lore skill, which it can use to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} of any kind.

+2 Status to All Saves vs. Occult.name: +2 Status to All Saves vs. Occult

Akashic Interference.name: Akashic Interference
Akashic Interference.description:
90 feet. The swirl of constant uploads and downloads near the Akashic dragon make attempts to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} unpredictable. A creature other than an akashic dragon that enters or begins its turn in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:38|options:area-effect] save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to this dragon's Akashic interference for 1 minute.

Critical Success For 1 round, when the creature attempts to Recall Knowledge, it gains a +3 status bonus to its check.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure For 1 round, when the creature attempts to Recall Knowledge, it takes a –3 status penalty to its check.

Critical Failure As failure, but the effect lasts 1 minute.

Deflecting Lasers.name: Deflecting Lasers
Deflecting Lasers.description:
Trigger The dragon is critically hit by an attack

Effect The Akashic dragon raises a web of deflecting lasers using the eyes embedded all over their body, gaining resistance 20 to all damage against the triggering attack and absorbing some of the blow's energy, recharging their Akashic Barrage. If the Akashic dragon has succeeded at a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} check against the creature in the last minute, they gain resistance 30 instead.

Akashic Adaptability.name: Akashic Adaptability
Akashic Adaptability.description: The Akashic dragon can activate magic and tech items with the Cast a Spell activation of any tradition as if the spells they contain were occult spells.

Akashic Barrage.name: Akashic Barrage
Akashic Barrage.description: The dragon condenses cutting truths and fiery revelations from the Akashic Record into sizzling laser fire that they project as a cone from their primary eye to assail their foes. This deals @Damage[18d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30] (@Check[reflex|dc:38|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:stupefied] save). If a creature critically fails its Reflex save against Akashic Barrage and the Akashic dragon has succeeded at a check to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about the creature in the last minute, the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round. The dragon can't use Akashic Barrage again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Akashic Barrage]]{1d4 rounds}.

Draconic Frenzy.name: 龙之狂怒 Draconic Frenzy
Draconic Frenzy.description: The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.

Draconic Momentum.name: Draconic Momentum
Draconic Momentum.description: The dragon recharges their Akashic Barrage whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.

Records of the Enemy.name: Records of the Enemy
Records of the Enemy.description: The first time each encounter the Akashic dragon hits and deals damage to a creature with their jaws Strike, they receive information about the creature as if they'd succeeded at a check to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about that creature.

Shocking Revelation.name: Shocking Revelation
Shocking Revelation.description:
Requirements The dragon has successfully Recalled Knowledge about an enemy within 90 feet within the last minute

Effect The dragon utters a shocking truth about the creature that even it might not know, causing it and its allies to hesitate or act erratically. Every enemy within 90 feet that can hear the secret must attempt a @Check[will|dc:38|options:area-effect,inflicts:slowed,inflicts:confused] save. Each creature is then immune to Shocking Revelation for 24 hours.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round.

Akashic Dragon (Ancient).publicNotes: Though rarely encountered outside their crumbling, relic-filled lairs, Akashic dragons occasionally live in hubs of knowledge, such as bustling space stations or cities. These dragons, bestowed with a direct connection to the Akashic Record, are arrogant curators of knowledge, assuming (often correctly) that no other being could possibly have a deeper understanding of the universe's secrets. Akashic dragons spend extensive time in the Akashic Record, often emerging after what they perceive as many days while mere hours have passed for the rest of the galaxy.
Akashic Dragon (Ancient).allSaves: +2 status to all saves vs. occult

Bastorox.name: Bastorox

Shield Arm.name: Shield Arm
Shield Arm.description: A bastorox's battering arm can also be used as a shield (Hardness 5, 30 HP, BT 15). If the shield is destroyed, the bastorox takes a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls made with its battering arm Strikes. A destroyed shield arm regenerates over the course of 24 hours.

Motion Sense.name: Motion Sense
Motion Sense.description: A bastorox can sense nearby creatures through vibrations in air or water caused by movement as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

Colony Mind.name: Colony Mind
Colony Mind.description: 30 feet. All allies with colony mind within the listed range of each other are in constant telepathic communication and are aware of all threats any individual knows about. While within range of at least one ally with colony mind, when a creature rolls a success on a save against a mental effect, it gets a critical success instead.

Raise a Shield.name: 举起盾牌 Raise a Shield
Raise a Shield.description:
需求 你正持握盾牌。

你举起盾牌保护自己。当你举起盾牌时，你将盾牌所列的环境加值添加到自己的AC上。你的盾牌会一直保持举起直到你的下一回合开始。

Bioelectric Jolt.name: Bioelectric Jolt
Bioelectric Jolt.description: The bastorox unleashes a bolt of bioelectric energy from its arm blade. The bolt deals @Damage[6d8[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in a @Template[type:line|distance:30] with a @Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|options:area-effect] save. The bastorox can't use Bioelectric Jolt again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Bioelectric Jolt]]{1d4 rounds}.

Bastorox.publicNotes:
The dreaded bastoroxes are bio-organic bulwarks that use an immense chitinous shield to deflect damage while loosing bolts of bioelectricity at foes. They hold positions for advancing Krethiskar subcolony components, such as the multitudes of agile emganats. These biohulks are but one of the new innovations found within the ranks of the Krethiskar subcolony, and they were active during the siege of Rodnarth, where they implacably advanced through that world's treacherous swamps.

Developing far out in the Vast from an insectile species known as kucharns, the Swarm is considered the greatest threat in existence for several cultures. Composed of countless subcolonies, each consisting of myriad component subspecies, the Swarm is a vast host that acts in psychic union for the purpose of consuming all matter across the cosmos. Most frightening of all is the Swarm's ability to assimilate the knowledge and DNA of those they consume. The Swarm so thoroughly strips these worlds that surveyors struggle to find even trace amounts of inorganic resources, such as rare minerals and water. Few of these huskworlds have been thoroughly explored, as oftentimes exploration awakens inert drones, theorized to have been left behind in case the devoured system was ever colonized and repopulated. Pockets of Swarm activity exist well beyond this region of space, as the Swarm sent out countless subcolonies in the ages before the Gap, and even now, only some of them have reached their intended destinations.

The Swarm is perhaps most known in the current era for two important facts. First, its assault on the Pact Worlds and the Veskarium led to the cessation of hostilities and tenuous alliance between the two powers. Second, the Swarm is the direct ancestor of the shirren species. Having broken away sometime during the Gap, shirrens found succor with the deity Hylax, who was believed to have evolved from a pre-Swarm kucharn and who turned her back on the ravenous entity the Swarm has evolved into.

In recent years, the Swarm had been seen as little more than a distant boogeyman with no activities save for classified actions in the Suskillon system in the Vast. However, the recent discovery of the missing Szandite world of Krethiskar inadvertently awoke a dormant subcolony that had been responsible for the world's consumption. Since then, the newly dubbed Krethiskar subcolony has become a scourge of Near Space, engaged in an active invasion of Szandite Collective worlds and poised to spill over into other powers like the Veskarium and Pact Worlds. This rediscovered subcolony has begun rapid evolution of its components and remains cut off from the wider Swarm, but it still represents a clear danger to Near Space.

Psychic Fungus Harvester.name: Psychic Fungus Harvester

Mycelium Network.name: Mycelium Network
Mycelium Network.description: Psychic fungi are connected to a network of psychic filaments bonding all psychic fungi from the same colony. This network grants the harvester the ability to detect creatures capable of thought within 30 feet of a bonded psychic fungus up to 1,000 feet away as an imprecise sense.

Release Spores.name: 释放孢子 Release Spores
Release Spores.description:
Trigger The harvester is reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The harvester releases a cloud of spores in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] centered on a corner of its own space that lasts for 1 round. Each creature that's in the cloud or enters it is exposed to befuddling spores. If this reaction was triggered by damage from a melee attack, the triggering creature uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its roll for its Fortitude save (two degrees worse if it was a jaws Strike or other Strike with the creature's mouth).

Befuddling Spores.name: Befuddling Spores
Befuddling Spores.description:
Plants and fungi are immune

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:18]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 3} (1 round)

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 4} (1 round)

Sporocapture.name: Sporocapture
Sporocapture.description:
An intelligent creature hit by a harvester's lash must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save. The effect lasts until the end of the harvester's next turn, but the harvester can Sustain it, keeping the lash unusable on the target's forehead. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 24 hours.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} with the harvester.

Critical Failure The creature is fascinated with the harvester and must spend each of its actions on its turn to move closer to the harvester as expediently as possible while avoiding obvious dangers. If a fascinated creature is adjacent to the harvester, it stays still and doesn't act. If the creature is attacked, the fascination ends.

Psychic Fungus Harvester.publicNotes:
In order to grow and spread, a sufficiently stable cluster of psychic fungi dispatches harvesters to collect nutrients. While most harvesters return with decaying animal and plant matter, the fungi's preferred prey are thinking creatures that they lead back to the colony for the elders' consumption.

A menace to every sentient being, psychic fungi are an invasive species that propagate by using the minds of sapient creatures. Psychic fungi that have integrated a higher intelligence can become a regional or even planetary threat.

Arcade Leap.name: Arcade Leap
Arcade Leap.description: The hardlight mascot can make a special Leap up to 15 feet vertically and up to 20 feet horizontally without a check.

Stomp.name: Stomp
Stomp.description: The hardlight mascot Leaps adjacent to a Medium or smaller creature, then makes a kick Strike against it. On a successful attack, it deals kick damage plus an additional 1d8 force damage. On a critical hit, the target is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Taunt.name: 嘲讽 Taunt
Taunt.description: The hardlight mascot sings a jingle to a creature within 60 feet, dealing @Damage[4d6[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:19|basic] save).

Thermatrod Caldera.name: Thermatrod Caldera

Temperature Sense.name: Temperature Sense
Temperature Sense.description: A thermatrod caldera can detect nearby changes in temperature as a precise sense to detect living creatures and other heat sources.

Melting Aura.name: Melting Aura
Melting Aura.description: 15 feet. A creature that begins its turn within this aura takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:26|basic] save).

Slag Barrage.name: Slag Barrage
Slag Barrage.description: The thermatrod sprays part of its molten core in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] that hardens moments later. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[11d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic] save). A creature that fails the save is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} ([[/act escape dc=29]]). The thermatrod can't use Slag Barrage again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Slag Barrage]]{1d4 rounds}.

Thermatrod Caldera.publicNotes:
Under certain electromagnetic conditions, two or more thermatrods might eject their molten cores into a shared magma chamber. The conjoined material forms a caldera, a mighty beast with starmetal veins running through its plates.

Originally thought to be exiles from the Plane of Fire, thermatrods actually developed independently in multiple star systems in the Universe. A thermatrod resembles a molten bipedal creature, though they often walk on their front knuckles to support their heavy frames. Thermatrods sport distinctive raised dorsal plates that resemble hexagonal basalt columns, which researchers use to estimate an individual's age. A thermatrod's hardened exterior varies greatly based on local mineral deposits that make up a thermatrod's diet.

Flarysca.name: Flarysca

Sun Sense.name: Sun Sense
Sun Sense.description: A flarysca can sense the presence of sunlight within 60 feet as a precise sense.

Lazy Sunlight.name: Lazy Sunlight
Lazy Sunlight.description:
15 feet. Creatures other than the flarysca that enter or begin their turn within the aura must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:25] save. On a failure, a creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1}, or increases it clumsy value by 1 if it was already clumsy.

If it was already @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} or greater, it instead becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} until it begins its turn outside the lazy sunlight aura. Creatures can't reduce their clumsy values while within the lazy sunlight aura.

On a critical success, a creature becomes temporarily immune to lazy sunlight for 1 hour.

Reactive Burst.name: Reactive Burst
Reactive Burst.description:
Trigger A creature interacts with or moves through a sunbeam containing a sleeping flarysca

Effect The flarysca startles awake, letting out a burst of fire as their body reconstitutes within the sunlight. The triggering creature and any other creature within 30 feet of the flarysca must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save or take @Damage[8d6[fire]] damage.

Rejuvenate (Deactivated by Cold).name: Rejuvenate (Deactivated by Cold)
Rejuvenate (Deactivated by Cold).description:
The flarysca has @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.lR9R5Vld8Eu2Dha5]{Regeneration} 20 (deactivated by cold) while in direct sunlight. In addition to being deactivated by cold, this regeneration deactivates for 1 round whenever the flarysca takes an Attack action.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Regeneration]

Creeping Warmth.name: Creeping Warmth
Creeping Warmth.description:
Frequency once per minute

Effect The flarysca shines brightly, increasing the radius of their lazy sunlight aura by 5 feet for 1 minute.

Light Jump.name: Light Jump
Light Jump.description:
Frequency once per round

Requirements The flarysca is standing within direct sunlight

Effect The flarysca teleports themself to another sunbeam or area of direct sunlight they can sense within 60 feet, with the effects of the translocate spell.

Flarysca.publicNotes:
What seem to be glimmering motes of dust drifting in a sunbeam might be the scattered forms of flaryscas drifting across space, dormant but poised to ignite at any moment. Flaryscas materialize ethereally within a sun's rays, dancing to the farthest reaches of space where they flare up for a brief, intense period, then presumably fade away into repose.

While pleasant to look at, flaryscas are responsible for unexplained deaths occurring on many worlds, and even in space. Their soporific aura lulls creatures to curl up lazily in the sun, ignoring cravings for sustenance as flaryscas cavort around these slumberers. While perfect for an afternoon nap or a leshy cosmonaut in search of a snack, on rare occasions such a slumber might prove deadly.

Elementals are energetic intrusions from the primal planes of existence. They take many forms, from mindless eddies in the wind or water to complex entities capable of wielding primal magic. The advent of space travel and the progress of technology have allowed even more living embodiments of the elements to manifest in the Universe.

Natural events like wildfires, solar storms, and floods can unleash hordes of unruly elementals on a planet or into a region of space. Sometimes, a gathering of powerful elementals causes a natural disaster on an epic scale, like an off-the-charts solar flare that fries a planet's power grid or a ceaseless storm swirling in a gas giant's atmosphere.

Elindrian Queen.name: Elindrian Queen

Call Cosmos.name: Call Cosmos
Call Cosmos.description:
You reach into the void and call down a vertical column of dangerous cosmic material that rains destruction upon creatures in the area and shrouds the terrain around it in a swirl of stardust. This matter is made up of bits of burning stars and chunks of frozen comets, and it deals 5d12 cold damage and 5d12 fire damage to every creature in the area when you Cast the Spell. For the spell's duration, the cosmic material reduces visibility and mobility, making the area greater difficult terrain and making everything seen within or through the area @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}.

Heightened (10th) The cold and fire damage each increase by 2d12.

Massacre.name: 大屠杀 Massacre
Massacre.description:
你释放死亡的波动，吸尽其路径上的生命力量。每个在线形区域上的17级或者更低的生物必须尝试一次强韧豁免。如果来自大屠杀的伤害令一个生物的生命值降低到0，该生物立即死亡。如果大屠杀没有杀死任何一个生物，其奔涌的虚能会向你席卷回来，对线形区域上的每个生物造成额外@Damage[30[void]]伤害（包括17级以上的生物）并且对你造成30虚能伤害。

大成功 该生物不受影响。

成功 该生物承受9d6虚能伤害。

失败 该生物承受@Damage[(ternary(gte(@item.rank,10),115,100))[void]]伤害。

大失败 该生物死亡。

升阶（10环级） 该法术可以影响至多19级的生物。成功时的伤害增加到10d6，失败时增加到115。

Desiccate.name: 干涸凋死 Desiccate
Desiccate.description:
你将水分从目标的身体中抽出，造成10d10虚能伤害。由水组成的生物（例如水元素）以及植物生物使用其豁免骰更差一级的结果。身躯不包含明显水分的生物（例如土元素）免疫干涸凋死。

升阶（+1） 该伤害增加1d10。

Moment of Renewal.name: 再起时刻 Moment of Renewal
Moment of Renewal.description: 目标立即经历相当于一天的回复。所有24小时后便结束的不利效果会结束，但这不会缩短任何正在影响目标的法术持续时间。该目标如同休息了24小时一般回复生命值和恢复状态，但它们不会再次进行其日常准备，或者获得除了治疗以外的其他休息的好处。这些目标会暂时免疫1天。

Singularity Seed.name: Singularity Seed
Singularity Seed.description:
A tiny particle of incredibly dense mass magically blossoms into a gravitational singularity that pulls creatures toward it and spatially compresses any creatures that are moved into it. You create a singularity in a 5-foot square that is unoccupied by any creatures. All creatures and unsecured objects in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:50] around the singularity are moved toward the singularity when the spell is cast, depending on their Reflex save (see below). Creatures and objects are moved starting with those closest to the singularity, so they might block the path of other creatures and objects that are pulled in later.

Creatures' spaces can overlap in the square that contains the singularity. A creature or object that enters the singularity or ends its turn there takes 10d10 bludgeoning damage with no saving throw. While in the singularity, a creature can take no actions except to attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} against your Spell DC. When the spell ends, the singularity disappears, and overlapping creatures are moved to the closest unoccupied space, as determined by the GM.

Squares in the emanation are difficult terrain for creatures moving away from the singularity, and creatures moving toward the singularity can move 10 feet for each 5 feet of movement they spend.

A creature needs to attempt a Reflex save if it's in the area when the spell is cast and when it ends its turn in the area.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is pulled 10 feet toward the singularity.

Failure The creature is pulled 20 feet toward the singularity.

Critical Failure The creature is pulled 30 feet toward the singularity.

Execute (at will).name: Execute (at will)
Execute (at will).description:
你将你的手指指向目标生物，并祈求万物的终结。目标承受70点虚能伤害，这有一次基础强韧豁免。如果目标是不死生物，或者具有虚能治疗能力，该法术失去死亡和虚能特征，获得命能特征，并且目标改为承受70点命能伤害，这有一次基础强韧豁免。

升阶（+1） 该伤害增加10。

Resurrect (elindrians only; up to 10 eli.name: Resurrect (elindrians only; up to 10 elindrians with one casting and a cast time of only 10 minutes if they perished within the last hour)
Resurrect (elindrians only; up to 10 eli.description:
你尝试呼唤目标的灵魂，使其返回自己的身体。这需要目标的身体位于仪式现场并且遗体相对完整（relatively intact）。目标必须是在过去的一年内死亡的。若 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.deities.Item.QZD0u1jxwz0kj8uI]{法莱斯玛 Pharasma} 认为目标大限已至，或者目标不愿返回到现世的话，该仪式自动失败，但你会在成功的宗教检定后了解到这一点，而且在仪式结束后无需支付开销。

大成功 目标被复活了。她复活时的生命值全满，保留死亡时已经准备的法术以及各种能量池剩余的点数，但原有身体所承受的任何长期性的衰弱（long-term debilitations）仍然折磨着它。目标在复生的过程中会见到自身所信仰神祇的代理人，代理人会激励目标，使其在攻击检定、察觉、豁免检定、和技能检定中获得+1状态加值，持续1星期。目标在彼世所经历的这段时间以某种方式永久地改变了她，这可能是轻微的人格变化、一缕白发或者一个奇怪的新胎记。

成功 同大成功，不过目标复活时生命值为1点，已经准备的法术以及各种能量池均被清空，但原有身体所承受的任何长期性的衰弱（long-term debilitations）仍然折磨着它。取代受到代理人的激励，该角色在彼世的时光使其暂时变得虚弱不堪。目标承受 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{笨拙 Clumsy 1}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{流失 Drained 1}和 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{力竭 Enfeebled 1} ，持续1星期；直至这个星期经过为止，这些状态无法被任何手段移除或减弱。

失败 你的尝试未能成功。

大失败 某种可怖的错误发生了——邪恶的灵魂占据了尸身、尸体被转化为特殊种类的不死生物、或者某种糟糕的命运降临到了目标头上。

升阶（6环级） 你能复活至多为12级的目标，仪式基础开销变为125gp。

升阶（7环级） 即便尸体仅剩下一小部分也可以用于复活目标；成功或大成功的仪式会创造出新的身体。目标必须是在过去的十年内死亡的。仪式需要四名次要施法者，每名次要施法者的等级都必须至少达到目标等级的一半。你能复活至多为14级的目标，仪式基础开销变为200gp。

升阶（8环级） 同7环级仪式，但你能复活至多为16级的目标，仪式基础开销变为300gp。

升阶（9环级） 即便没有尸体你也能复活目标，但前提是你了解目标的名字，并且在此之前的任意时刻曾经碰触过她的身体。目标必须是在过去的百年内死亡的，而且仪式结果为成功时不会赋予目标负面状态。仪式需要八名次要施法者，每名次要施法者的等级都必须至少达到目标等级的一半。你能复活至多为18级的目标，仪式基础开销变为600gp。

升阶（10环级） 同9环级仪式，不过目标的死亡时长已经不再重要。仪式需要十六名次要施法者，每名次要施法者的等级都必须至少达到目标等级的一半。你能复活至多为20级的目标，仪式基础开销变为1000gp。

Husk Mind.name: Husk Mind
Husk Mind.description:
Elindrians can communicate nearly instantaneously via a primal bond similar to a formian hive mind. While within telepathic range of at least one other elindrian with husk mind, an elindrian acolyte gains a +2 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls, Perception checks, and saving throws against mental effects. If one elindrian is aware of a combatant, all members of the husk mind within range are aware of it.

The elindrian's husk mind is integrated with their scent; for purposes of detecting other elindrians, formians, undead, or corpses, their scent is a precise sense, rather than imprecise.

An elindrian always knows the precise distance and direction to the Terror of Ukulam, so long as they're on the same plane of existence.

Vision of Elindrae.name: Vision of Elindrae
Vision of Elindrae.description: The elindrian queen assails creatures with visions of environmental destruction on the moon Elindrae. She targets as many creatures as she wishes within range of her telepathy and to whom she has a line of effect. The creatures take @Damage[3d6[mental],1d6[persistent,mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:40|basic] save) and are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a failure (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 4} on a critical failure). On a critical success, creatures are temporarily immune to Vision of Elindrae for 24 hours.

Elindrian Queen.publicNotes:
While no one has confirmed the existence of elindrian queens, the elindrians' few similarities to formians have led scholars to believe they must exist. Indeed, these formidable matriarchs are the primary force driving elindrians to unite with the Terror of Ukulam.

The people known as elindrians vanished eons ago into desolate husk-hives beneath the surface of the planet Castrovel. Until recently, they were creatures of legend, existing only in the most obscure formian histories. Now, scholars on Castrovel desperately search for additional records about elindrians, their origins, and—most importantly—the truth about their connection to the terrifying ooze-like creature that recently descended from their moon, Elindrae. One theory is that elindrians share some primal bond with the ooze-thing called the Terror of Ukulam, and perhaps both originate from Elindrae—other theories claim that elindrians created the ooze in the first place or that they're distant cousins to formians, corrupted by the Terror long ago.

Cerebinite.name: Cerebinite

Blast of Cerebinium Shards.name: Blast of Cerebinium Shards
Blast of Cerebinium Shards.description: Creatures that take mental damage are also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for 1 round on a critical failure

Crystalline Ambush.name: Crystalline Ambush
Crystalline Ambush.description:
Trigger A non-mindless creature enters a space adjacent to a cerebinite

Requirements Initiative hasn't yet been rolled

Effect The cerebinite automatically notices the creature, makes a crystalline growth Strike against one creature adjacent to itself, then rolls initiative.

Mind-Shattering Demise.name: Mind-Shattering Demise
Mind-Shattering Demise.description: When the cerebinite dies, it explodes in a blast fueled by the memories stored in its crystalline body, affecting all creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:30]. Each creature in the area (including other cerebinites) must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:22|options:area-effect,inflicts:stunned] save or take @Damage[2d8[mental]|options:area-damage] damage and become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} (or double damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} on a critical failure).

Cerebinite.publicNotes:
The mineral cerebinium, also known as memory crystal, naturally appears in the broken region of space known as Kazmurg's Absurdity. Typically, these minerals develop in remote subterranean locations, where they grow based on the presence of thinking minds within a planetary range. The more active minds in a region, the faster these mineral formations grow. A major world of millions can easily create vast underground stores, and in these majestic caverns, cerebinites spontaneously emerge.

Cerebinites are essentially mindless creatures—save for the memories that power them—who have developed as an offshoot of cerebinium growth. Though possessing no intellect of their own, they act off the impulses provided by the memories stored within the crystals that constitute their forms. In many ways, a cerebinite is like the many oozes that lie in wait throughout the cosmos.

Encountering these active mineral deposits often requires a delicate approach, as cerebinites can engage up close or at a short range by hurling cerebinium spikes and blasts of cerebinium shards charged with memories drawn from their surroundings. Even in death, these masses of cerebinium explode in a destructive burst of psychic energy, affecting the minds of those nearby with jumbled visions of countless different lives. When multiple injured cerebinites gather near one another, this proximity can cause an especially destructive chain reaction due to cerebinites' own susceptibility to mental attacks.

Xenowarden Biohulk.name: Xenowarden Biohulk

进阶 Hammer.name: 进阶 Hammer
进阶 Hammer.description:
Upgrades: 1

Valued as both a crafting tool and an effective weapon, this hammer uses specially weighted heads and handles to deliver maximum force with each strike.

Grenade Launcher (Tactical).name: Grenade Launcher (Tactical)
Grenade Launcher (Tactical).description:
Upgrades 1

Activate 2 Area Fire

The rotating barrel of this automatic breechloading launcher holds up to six grenades of any variety with smart loading technology, allowing its wielder to choose a different grenade each time they fire. A grenade launcher is a martial ranged weapon, has a reload of 2, and a range 350 feet.

Frag Grenade (Advanced).name: Frag Grenade (Advanced)
Frag Grenade (Advanced).description:
Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @Damage[1d6[bleed]]

These fragmentary grenades explode in a cloud of shrapnel and deal 3d8 piercing damage in a @Template[burst|distance:10] with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save.

Lattice Armor.name: 格栅甲 Lattice Armor
Lattice Armor.description: 在惊世奇土的某些地方也被称为“瓦尔曼（varman）”，这种护甲由金属细索编织成格状图案，并配有板甲部分，用于保护头部、颈部和肩部。这种护甲能像链甲环一样分散打击，但结构更紧密，因此更安静。

Battering Charge.name: Battering Charge
Battering Charge.description: The Xenowarden biohulk Strides three times, then makes a melee Strike, dealing an additional @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage on a hit. If the target of this Strike is an object or construct, this Strike ignores 5 Hardness.

Destructive Power.name: Destructive Power
Destructive Power.description: Whenever a Xenowarden biohulk makes a melee Strike that would deal bludgeoning damage, that Strike gains the razing trait.

Nature's Fury.name: Nature's Fury
Nature's Fury.description: The Xenowarden biohulk bellows with rage, causing plants to sprout from the ground and hinder the biohulk's enemies. Creatures within 30 feet of the biohulk take @Damage[6d8[slashing]] damage from the thrashing plants (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save), and the area becomes difficult terrain for 10 minutes. The Xenowarden biohulk ignores difficult terrain created by Nature's Fury. Then, the Xenowarden biohulk can attempt an Intimidation check to [[/act demoralize]] each creature in the area.

Xenowarden Biohulk.publicNotes:
Biohulks are devoted Xenowardens who purposefully fused their body with plants and other flora to gain formidable strength. This transformation is usually accomplished using biotech, magic, or genetic experimentation, and can't be reversed. Regardless of their method, biohulks are incredibly strong and capable of battering down most artificial barriers with ease. Xenowarden biohulks are usually extremists, entirely and passionately devoted to the Xenowardens' cause.

The Xenowardens are a powerful and well-respected organization who aim to safeguard and study the natural world and ensure that technological advances are ecologically responsible. With an incredibly diverse membership, their ranks include, among others, hermits who practice nature magic and eschew technology and scientists who create renewable energy from algae. On the other end of the spectrum are elite combat units like the Wildknights and the Greenguards and feared ecoterrorists like the enigmatic Oleander. The Xenowardens are led by a Greenspeaker, who's elected by acclaim and advised by their peers.

Vanguard-Class Soldier Robot.name: Vanguard-Class Soldier Robot

Integrated Advanced Painglaive.name: Integrated Advanced Painglaive
Integrated Advanced Painglaive.description:
Upgrades: 1

This fierce but deceptively elegant polearm rips apart enemies with a motor-powered fangblade. An adjustable pulse trigger allows the wielder to channel extra pain through the weapon.

Integrated Advanced Seeker Rifle.name: Integrated Advanced Seeker Rifle
Integrated Advanced Seeker Rifle.description:
Upgrades: 1

This single-shot sniper rifle is one of the most popular weapons in the Vast thanks to its stability and reliability with minimal upkeep.

Assault Jump.name: Assault Jump
Assault Jump.description: The vanguard-class soldier robot Leaps to a space it can see within 60 feet. All creatures within 5 feet of where the robot lands must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:33] save or be knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. The robot then makes a painglaive Strike against one creature within reach. The robot can't use Assault Jump again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Assault Jump]]{1d4 rounds}.

Linked Senses (60 feet).name: Linked Senses (60 feet)
Linked Senses (60 feet).description: The vanguard-class soldier robot shares senses with other allied vanguard-class soldier robots within 60 feet.

Seeker Warhead.name: Seeker Warhead
Seeker Warhead.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect The vanguard-class soldier robot fires a plasma warhead at an intersection within 200 feet it can see. All creatures in a @Template[type:burst|distance:15] take @Damage[8d12[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:33|basic] save). On a failure, a creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened} for 1 minute. The area in which the warhead exploded becomes difficult terrain.

X-Ray Vision (60 feet).name: X-Ray Vision (60 feet)
X-Ray Vision (60 feet).description: The vanguard-class soldier robot can see through most materials and walls within 60 feet. Its sight is blocked by 2 feet of wood or plastic, 1 foot of stone, 2 inches of metal, or any amount of lead or starmetal, and it can see only in black and white.

Vanguard-Class Soldier Robot.publicNotes:
Vanguard-class soldier robots are hulking machines of battle. They often have four clawed hands, digitigrade legs, a long tail, and a featureless, diamond-shaped head. Their back-mounted plasma launcher and burst-duration jump jets give them a hunched, predatory appearance, augmented by top-of-the-line weapons. Each is 8 feet tall and weighs 700 pounds.

Though their striking image has captured media attention, the vanguard-class soldier robot is too expensive to mass-produce on the scale of an army, and so they are used as special forces, vanguards, bodyguards, and even field commanders for all-robotic forces. A lack of large-scale feasibility is, admittedly, poor comfort to any soul within seeker warhead range.

Living soldiers are a liability. They have families who don't want them to die, need salaries, have morale, require discipline, commit atrocities, and have opinions on the war. They're a necessary, but unfortunate, part of warfare for any society without an abiding love for necromancy.

Cutting-edge soldier robots promise to solve this problem. Soldier robots have no loved ones, opinions, self-preservation instinct, or tendency toward random atrocities. Many companies market their soldier robots as a humane solution to modern combat.

In practice, they're anything but.
