Without lives on the line, soldier robots encourage their owners to engage more often and more violently. Standoffs turn into skirmishes, skirmishes into battles, and inevitably the innocent are caught up in the mix. They're particularly popular with corporations and non-governmental organizations that appreciate being able to throw a dozen soldier robots at any naysayer, public critic, or explorer they dislike while being able to deny true responsibility for what happens.

Though there's constant public fear of soldier robots going rogue, it has yet to prove a serious concern. If a soldier robot wants you dead, it's because someone doesn't mind if it kills you.

Dust Worm.name: Dust Worm

Electrocommunication.name: Electrocommunication
Electrocommunication.description: Dust worms communicate by shaping and reading electromagnetic fields. This functions like telepathy with creatures that share this ability.

Electromagnetic Sight.name: Electromagnetic Sight
Electromagnetic Sight.description: Dust worms sense their surroundings by reading electromagnetic fields, including disruptions caused by creatures or objects. This is a precise sense within the listed range. Intense fields, such as areas emitting radiation or auras of electricity, make creatures within them @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} to the diaspora worm.

Electromagnetic Blindness.name: Electromagnetic Blindness
Electromagnetic Blindness.description:
Dust worms that take electricity damage must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save.

Critical Success The worm is unaffected.

Success The worm is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Failure The worm is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The worm is blinded for 1 round, then dazzled for 1 round.

Sandstorm Veil.name: Sandstorm Veil
Sandstorm Veil.description:
Requirements The dust worm is in a sandy or junk-ridden environment

Effect The dust worm uses its magnetic field to kick up a swirling shroud of sand and metallic bits in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] around itself. This shroud moves with the worm and lasts for [[/gmr 1d6 #rounds]]{1d6 rounds}. The shroud grants the worm resistance 6 to physical damage, and any creature that ends its turn within the area takes @Damage[2d6[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic] save).

Tongue Grab.name: Tongue Grab
Tongue Grab.description: A creature hit by the dust worm's tongue becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}. A creature can sever the tongue with a Strike against AC 21 that deals at least @Damage[14[slashing]] damage. This prevents the worm from using its tongue Strike until it regrows its tongue, which takes 1 day.

Tongue Reel.name: Tongue Reel
Tongue Reel.description:
Requirements The dust worm has @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} a creature with its tongue

Effect The diaspora worm attempts to [[/act reposition]] the grabbed creature toward the worm. If the grabbed creature is brought to within 10 feet or less of the worm, the worm can attempt to Swallow Whole the creature.

Dust Worm.publicNotes:
Diaspora worms mutated to navigate sandy or barren asteroid surfaces, and as a result, dust worms trade agility for bulk and can resist radiation. Their electromagnetic manipulation affects small bits of ferrous material, allowing them to telekinetically wield sand and small metallic shards as weapons or cloaks.

"Space worm" is a nonscientific term for a xenobiologically complex clade of invertebrate animals and beasts that make their home in the vastness of space. Some, like the diaspora and dust worms, show signs of intelligence and are directly related to each other. Others, like the surnoch, evolved separately, with their origins completely unknown to science.

Ferrofluid Ooze.name: Ferrofluid Ooze

Magnetic Sense.name: Magnetic Sense
Magnetic Sense.description: A ferrofluid ooze can feel magnetic fields, allowing it to detect large sources of magnetic metal (Bulk 1 or greater) and items with the tech trait within the listed range.

Magnetic Field.name: 磁场领域 Magnetic Field
Magnetic Field.description: 30 feet. A ferrofluid ooze is surrounded by a constant field that interferes with nearby equipment. Items with the tech trait inside the aura take a –1 circumstance penalty to all checks using them. Unattended metal objects in the aura of 1 Bulk or less are pulled adjacent to the ooze.

Attach.name: Attach
Attach.description:
A ferrofluid ooze can attach itself to any creature its size or larger that has the tech trait or is wearing or wielding items with the tech trait. This is similar to Grab, but the ooze moves with that creature rather than holding it in place.

As long as the ooze is attached, it's @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard}, and the creature it's attached to must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:11] to Interact with items with the tech trait. On a failure, the item becomes attached to the ooze (treat as if being held by the ooze).

Demagnetization.name: Demagnetization
Demagnetization.description: A ferrofluid ooze that takes fire damage has its magnetic field deactivated until the end of the ooze's next turn. It releases itself from any creature it was attached to, drops any held items, and can't use Magnetic Leap or Attach until its magnetic field returns.

Magnetic Leap.name: Magnetic Leap
Magnetic Leap.description: The ferrofluid ooze instantly Attaches to a creature within its magnetic field that meets the requirements of that ability. A ferrofluid ooze can't use this ability again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Magnetic Leap]]{1d4 rounds}.

Ferrofluid Ooze.publicNotes:
Since the earliest days of space travel, engineers have used ferromagnetic fluids to help with the construction, operation and maintenance of starships. It was only a matter of time before overeager spellcasters tinkered with this substance and inadvertently imbued a modicum of life into it, creating yet another strain of ooze to plague the cosmos. These strange oozes exert magnetic powers and have an instinctive desire to seek out sources of electromagnetism. Ferrofluid oozes are considered galactic nuisances, and many shipyards across the Pact Worlds have begun retaining the services of expert "ooze control" agents to ensure these fluids don't impede their day-to-day operations.

A common ferrofluid ooze appears as a spiked pool of dark metal liquid that shifts in erratic ways but always reacts to nearby magnetic sources. They're mostly found hiding in the darkest corners of starships and space stations.

Oozes of many types lurk in dark and abandoned places across the galaxy, emerging only to satiate their appetites and threaten adventurers, explorers, and hapless workers alike.

Host Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster).name: Host Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)

Energy Aegis.name: 辟能圣盾 Energy Aegis
Energy Aegis.description:
你使用长效的强力能量护盾保护目标。目标获得对酸蚀、寒冷、电击、火焰、力场、音波、命能、以及虚能伤害的抗力5。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.4Lo2qb5PmavSsLNk]{法术效果：辟能圣盾}

升阶（9环级） 该抗力提升到10。

Regenerate.name: 再生术 Regenerate
Regenerate.description:
灌注的命能赋予一个生物持续的治疗。目标临时获得再生15，这令其在每个它的回合开始时回复15生命值。当它具有再生时，目标不会由于对生命值造成的伤害死亡，并且其 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yZRUzMqrMmfLu0V1]{濒死 Dying} 值不会增长到会杀死它的程度（多数生物的濒死值会在濒死3停止），但如果其 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{受伤 Wounded} 值到达4或者更高，它会 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{失去意识 Unconscious} 直到受伤被治愈。如果目标承受酸蚀或者火焰伤害，其再生失效，直到其下个回合结束。

每当该生物从再生中获得生命值时，他也会再生一个受损或者被破坏的器官（如果有）。在法术持续时间中，该生物可以花费一个交互动作，将被切下的肢体接合到它被切下前的位置，将其重新接上。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.dXq7z633ve4E0nlX]{法术效果：再生术}

升阶（9环级） 该再生增加到20。

Root of all Pain.name: Root of all Pain
Root of all Pain.description:
Your finger pushes a tiny root into the target's body that begins to grow, burrowing through the nervous system as it feeds off the electric signals triggered by the agonizing pain of its intrusive spread. The target attempts a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes 2d8 mental damage the first time each round it performs any action other than an action that is purely mental, such as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} (or other actions as determined by the GM).

Failure As success, except the target takes 4d8 mental damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for the duration of the spell.

Critical Failure As success, except the target takes 6d8 mental damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for the duration of the spell.

Field of Life.name: 生命力场 Field of Life
Field of Life.description:
命能形成的场充满该区域，为其中的生物赋予温暖和活力。每个在该区域中开始回合的活物回复1d8生命值，并且任何在该区域中开始回合的不死生物承受1d8命能伤害。

升阶（8环级） 该治疗与伤害提升到1d10。

升阶（9环级） 该治疗与伤害提升到1d12。

Carcinization.name: Carcinization
Carcinization.description:
You manipulate the target's genetic code and accelerate the inevitable, pressuring the target to undergo carcinization and become a crab. A willing target fully transforms into a crab, as if it had cast animal form heightened to the same rank as this spell and using the following statistics.

  • Crab

    • Speed 25 feet, swim 15 feet
    • Melee 1 big claw, Damage 2d8 piercing
    • Melee 1 claw (agile), Damage 2d4 piercing.
    • Can breathe air and water.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.16Li37zRYKClxqsv]{Spell Effect: Carcinization}

Unwilling targets must instead attempt a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target's body gains minor features of a crab, its appendages turning into claws and causing it to become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1}.

Failure The target transforms into a partial crab-like being, becoming clumsy 1, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The target is transformed into a partial crab-like being and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

Toxic Cloud.name: 毒云术 Toxic Cloud
Toxic Cloud.description:
你创造一团有毒云雾。该效果如同@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.9XHmC2JgTUIQ1CCm]{迷雾术 Mist}一般，除了该区域会每轮远离你10尺。在该区域中开始回合的生物承受6d8毒素伤害，这有一次基础强韧豁免。你可以解消该法术。

升阶（+1） 该伤害增加1d8。

Wall of Stone.name: 石墙术 Wall of Stone
Wall of Stone.description:
你塑造一面岩石墙壁。你创造一个至多120尺长，20尺高1寸厚的石墙。你可以塑形石墙的路径，将墙壁的每5尺放在方格之间的边界上。该墙壁无需竖直，所以举例而言，你可以用其塑造一座桥，或者一组阶梯。你必须将该墙创造在连续的开放空间中，令其边界不会穿过任何生物或者物体，否则你会失去该法术。

每一段10尺乘10尺的墙壁具有AC10，硬度14，以及50生命值，并且它免疫重击，寒冷伤害，以及精准伤害。一段被摧毁的墙壁可以移动通过，但是因此留下的碎石会令其变成困难地形。

升阶（+2） 每一段墙的生命值增加15。

Unfettered Movement.name: 行动无碍 Unfettered Movement
Unfettered Movement.description:
你驱散可能影响一个生物的阻碍。当处于该法术效果中时，法术目标忽视会赋予其速度环境减值的效果。当法术目标尝试@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape}一个令其@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}、@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}或者@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}的效果时，它自动成功，除非该效果是魔法性的，并且其环级比行动无碍更高。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.CdAyAiMGESvgNQtz]{Spell Effect: Unfettered Movement}

Earthbind.name: 地缚术 Earthbind
Earthbind.description:
你使用大地的重量以干扰目标的飞行能力，其效果基于强韧豁免。如果该生物安全到达地面，它不会承受坠落伤害。

大成功 目标不受影响。

成功 目标安全坠落至多120尺。

失败 目标安全坠落至多120尺。如果它碰到地面，它1轮内不能飞行，浮空或者以其他方式离开地面。

大失败 目标安全坠落至多120尺。如果它碰到地面，它1分钟内不能飞行，浮空或者以其他方式离开地面。

Life Seal.name: Life Seal
Life Seal.description:
Trigger A creature within range enters an environment that would be harmful to it.

A shimmering bubble envelops the target's body, allowing it to breathe normally in a vacuum or other unsafe environment and protecting it from severe temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (7th) You can target up to 5 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts for 8 hours and also protects against extreme temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (10th) You can target up to 10 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts until your next daily preparations and also protects against incredible temperatures and radiation.

Wall of Wind.name: 风墙术 Wall of Wind
Wall of Wind.description:
你创造一层会干扰所有通过事物的暴风墙。这面由旋风组成的墙有5尺厚，60尺长，30尺高。该墙为竖直的，但你可以指定其形态。虽然风墙会搅动空气，但不会阻碍视觉。该墙壁具有下列效果。

  • 来自物理远程攻击的弹药（例如箭，矢，投石索弹丸，以及其他类似大小的物体）无法穿过该墙。更大的远程武器的攻击，例如标枪，如果其路线通过该墙壁，则其在攻击骰上承受-2环境减值。重型远程武器和不创造实体物体的法术效果可以不受减值地通过该墙。
  • 该墙对尝试从地面通过它的生物为困难地形。气体，以及处于@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.V8wXOsoejQhe6CyG]{Vapor Form}的生物，无法穿过墙壁。
  • 尝试使用移动动作飞过墙壁的生物必须尝试一次强韧豁免。

大成功 该生物本回合可以正常移动穿过该墙。

成功 该飞行生物本回合可以正常移动穿过该墙，但是该墙为困难地形。

失败 该墙阻挡飞行生物的移动，并且当前动作的剩余移动力被浪费。

大失败 如同失败，并且该生物会被推离该墙10尺。

Entangling Flora.name: 纠缠植被 Entangling Flora
Entangling Flora.description:
植物与菌类无法抑制地快速成长，缠绕生物。区域内的所有表面均为困难地形。对于每轮内在该区域内开始自身回合的生物，必须尝试1次反射豁免。失败时，其速度承受-10尺环境减值，直至离开该区域为止，大失败时，它还会@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}1轮。生物能够尝试@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{逃脱 Escape}来移除这些效果。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.rjM25qfw5BKj9h97]{法术效果：纠缠植被}

Environmental Endurance.name: 忍受环境 Environmental Endurance
Environmental Endurance.description:
你保护目标对抗危险气温。选择严寒或炎热。目标不受你选择的气温的影响，但这对极寒和极热无效。

升阶（3环级） 目标受到保护，免遭严寒或炎热影响。

升阶（5环级） 目标受到保护，免遭严寒、炎热、极寒和极热影响。

Speak with Animals.name: 动物交谈 Speak with Animals
Speak with Animals.description: 你能向动物提问、得到动物的回答、以及对动物使用交涉技能。该法术不会让动物们变得比平时更加友善。狡猾动物的沟通表现得简短且模棱两可，而缺乏智能的动物通常会发表无意义的意见。

Water Breathing.name: 水中呼吸 Water Breathing
Water Breathing.description:
目标可以在水中呼吸。

升阶（3环级） 该持续时间增加到8小时。

升阶（4环级） 该持续时间增加到直到你的下次日常准备。

Ant Haul.name: 重负术 Ant Haul
Ant Haul.description:
你增强了目标的骨骼和肌肉系统，以承受更大的重量。目标可以比通常达到@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.D5mg6Tc7Jzrj6ro7]{Encumbered}额外携带3负载，最大不超过6负载。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.5yCL7InrJDHpaQjz]{Spell Effect: Ant Haul}

Caustic Blast.name: 酸液喷溅 Caustic Blast
Caustic Blast.description:
你发射一团立即爆发的酸液，泼溅到周围生物上。区域中的生物承受1d8酸蚀伤害，这有一次基础反射豁免；在大失败时，该生物也会承受@Damage[(1+floor((@item.rank -1)/2))[persistent,acid]]伤害。

升阶（+2） 该初始伤害增加1d8，并且持续伤害增加1。

Detect Magic.name: 侦测魔法 Detect Magic
Detect Magic.description:
你发出一股标记魔法存在的脉冲。你不会获得魔法存在或不存在以外的任何信息。你可以选择忽略你完全知晓的魔法，例如你或者你盟友的魔法物品以及持续法术。仅当幻术魔法的环级低于你的侦测魔法环级时，你才能侦测幻术魔法。但是，具有幻术灵光但是没有在外表上做伪装的物品（例如一瓶隐形药水），通常能够正常侦测。

升阶（3环级） 你能知晓法术侦测出的最强魔法效果的环级或等级，具体由GM决定。

升阶（4环级） 如3环级，你也能指出最高环级魔法的来源。如同一种模糊感官，你不会了解具体的位置，但是可以将来源锁定到5尺立方（或者最近的那个五尺立方，如果来源体积比这个区域更大）。

Detect Poison.name: 侦测毒素 Detect Poison
Detect Poison.description:
你可以侦测一个生物是否分泌毒素或者具有毒素，或者一件物体是否是毒素或者已经被下毒。你无法判断目标是否有多种毒素，也无法了解毒素的种类。特定的物质，例如铅或者酒精，是毒素，并会遮盖其他毒素。

升阶（2环级） 你会得知毒素的数量和类型。

Fear.name: 恐惧术 Fear
Fear.description:
你将恐惧植入目标；它必须尝试一次意志豁免。

大成功 目标不受影响。

成功 目标获得 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{惊惧 Frightened 1}.

失败 目标获得 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{惊惧 Frightened 2}.

大失败 目标获得 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{惊惧 Frightened 3} 并且 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{逃跑 Fleeing} 1轮.

升阶（3环级） 你能以至多五个生物为目标。

Seek the Stars.name: Seek the Stars
Seek the Stars.description:
In your mind's eye, you chart a path through the cosmos. If you're in Pact Worlds space, you immediately know which direction to travel in order to reach the Starstone on Absalom Station. If you're outside the Pact System, you know the direction to the nearest Drift beacon or the Starstone.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.

Stumble.name: Stumble
Stumble.description:
A burst of microgravity causes a tremor around the target, pulling it toward the ground. The target must attempt a Reflex save.

Failure The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} for 1 round. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 10 feet downward.

Critical Failure The target falls @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 20 feet downward.

Heightened (+1) If the target falls, it falls an additional 5 feet on a failure and 10 feet on a critical failure.

Tangle Vine.name: 绊足藤 Tangle Vine
Tangle Vine.description:
一条藤蔓凭空出现，从你手上弹射而出并捆绑在目标身上。尝试一次法术攻击检定对抗目标。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.TwtUIEyenrtAbeiX]{法术效果：绊足藤}

大成功 目标获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}状态，并且其速度承受-10尺环境减值，持续1轮。它能尝试@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{逃脱 Escape}对抗你的法术DC，来以此移除减值和禁足状态。

成功 目标速度承受-10尺环境减值，持续1轮。它能尝试逃脱对抗你的法术DC来移除减值。

失败 目标不受影响。

升阶（2环级） 效果持续2轮。

升阶（4环级） 效果持续1分钟。

Covered in Bugs.name: Covered in Bugs
Covered in Bugs.description:
Trigger The dragon critically fails a saving throw

Effect Swarming insects interfere with the effect, sacrificing themselves to protect their host's body and mind. The dragon attempts a @Check[flat|dc:17]. On a success, the dragon fails the saving throw instead of critically failing.

+2 status to all saves vs. mental.name: +2 status to all saves vs. mental

Excruciating Bites.name: Excruciating Bites
Excruciating Bites.description: A creature that takes damage from a host dragon's jaws Strike or is swallowed by the dragon is also bitten by hundreds of tiny insects; the creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:34] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} for 1 round.

Fast Swallow.name: Fast Swallow
Fast Swallow.description:
Trigger The dragon Grabs a creature

Effect The dragon uses Swallow Whole.

One with Swarms.name: One with Swarms
One with Swarms.description: The host dragon is immune to any attack made by a swarm of insects or similar creature (at the GM's discretion).

Swarm Breath.name: Swarm Breath
Swarm Breath.description: The dragon disgorges a swarm of biting insects that deals @Damage[12d8[piercing],2d6[persistent,piercing]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:50] (@Check[reflex|dc:37|basic] save). They can't use Swarm Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Swarm Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

Swarm Shape.name: Swarm Shape
Swarm Shape.description: The dragon transforms into an insect swarm. They drop all held, worn, and carried items. While in this form, they can't use attack actions (except Swarming Bites and Swarm Breath) and can't cast spells, but they can move through areas small enough for their individual insects to fit without having to Squeeze. They can use the same action to coalesce back into their normal form.

Swarming Bites.name: Swarming Bites
Swarming Bites.description:
Requirements The dragon is a swarm

Effect Each enemy in the swarm's space takes @Damage[12d6[piercing]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:37|basic] save). A creature that fails its save is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 round.

Host Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster).publicNotes: Innumerable insects follow these lumbering, ravenous dragons wherever they roam, swarming around them and living in the countless orifices along the host dragon's body. The dragon is both a part of the swarm itself and a part of the swarm's ecosystem—the swarm feeds on their body, and the dragon feeds on the swarm in a grotesque, centuries-long cycle that somehow results in the host dragon growing larger and more powerful even if they feed on no other substance in their lifetime. Host dragons draw their power from the primal instincts of insects and, despite their intelligence, often behave like the insects they gather to them. Host dragons despise answering questions and violently defy all attempts others make to understand their nature.

Free Captain Gunner.name: Free Captain Gunner

Carbon Shield.name: Carbon Shield
Carbon Shield.description: This durable yet lightweight defensive plate comes in a variety of shapes and sizes, and it's often customized with LED displays that broadcast personal insignia or an organization's colors and symbols.

Freebooter Armor.name: Freebooter Armor
Freebooter Armor.description:
Upgrades: 1

Freebooter armor is named for the pirates and outlaws who often wear these outfits. Freebooter armor typically consists of a breastplate worn under a jacket or flight suit, heavy boots and gloves, a helmet, and hidden straps and holsters for concealing weapons.

Four-Armed.name: Four-Armed
Four-Armed.description: You have four arms, which allows you to wield and hold up to four hands' worth of weapons and equipment. At any time, one pair of hands is designated as your active hands. You can change this designation from one pair of hands to another by taking the Switch Hands action, which is a single action. You can wield items only with your active hands.

Raise a Shield.name: 举起盾牌 Raise a Shield
Raise a Shield.description:
需求 你正持握盾牌。

你举起盾牌保护自己。当你举起盾牌时，你将盾牌所列的环境加值添加到自己的AC上。你的盾牌会一直保持举起直到你的下一回合开始。

Shield Block.name: 盾牌格挡 Shield Block
Shield Block.description: @Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ShieldBlock] In addition to the normal use of Shield Block, a Free Captain gunner can use Shield Block to prevent damage from an area attack and also prevent damage that would be dealt to an adjacent ally instead of themself.

Fire in the Hole!.name: Fire in the Hole!
Fire in the Hole!.description: A special payload gives the breaching gun the area (@Template[type:cone|distance:15]) and unwieldy traits until the end of the gunner's turn. The gunner makes an attack using Area Fire (@Check[reflex|dc:19|basic] save) that uses 3 projectiles. Enemies in the affected area who fail their save against the attack become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.enA7BxAjBb7ns1iF]{Suppressed} until the start of the gunner's next turn.

Raid Ready.name: Raid Ready
Raid Ready.description: The Free Captain gunner Switches Hands, then Raises a Shield or Reloads, in any order.

Shiver Your Timbers.name: Shiver Your Timbers
Shiver Your Timbers.description: A creature damaged by the gunner's breaching gun can't reduce its frightened condition until the gunner's next turn.

Free Captain Gunner.publicNotes:
Leading the assault with an explosion of cannon fire, the gunner's role in a boarding party is to incite a rout while protecting their fellow crew by suppressing a ship's defenses.

The Free Captains have been a scourge of interstellar shipping across the Pact Worlds for generations, but since the adoption of Drift Lanes, the outlaws have expanded their reach and scale by targeting ships across the galaxy using the new standardized interstellar shipping routes.

Garaggakal.name: Garaggakal

Emotion Sense.name: Emotion Sense
Emotion Sense.description: The garaggakal senses all creatures that are under emotion effects as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

Sense Through.name: Sense Through
Sense Through.description: The garaggakal's imprecise senses function through most materials and walls. The senses are blocked by energy fields (such as force fields and force effects) and sufficiently dense material: 10 feet of wood or plastic, 5 feet of stone, 1 foot of common metal, or 6 inches of lead or any starmetal.

Draining Saliva.name: Draining Saliva
Draining Saliva.description: A creature that takes damage from a garaggakal's spit Strike must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:20] save. On a failure, it's @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} on a critical failure).

Leech Life.name: Leech Life
Leech Life.description:
Requirements A living creature within 30 feet of the garaggakal has the drained condition

Effect The garaggakal amplifies the drain on the required creature's essential life force, dealing @Damage[5d6[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic] save). The garaggakal gains temporary Hit Points equal to half the damage dealt that last 1 minute. Regardless of the result of the save, the garaggakal then Flies up to 15 feet closer to the required creature, and the required creature becomes temporarily immune to Leech Life for 24 hours.

Phase Through.name: Phase Through
Phase Through.description: A garaggakal can shift out of phase with reality and thus can't be held by walls or cages. The garaggakal moves through up to 15 feet of solid matter. They must end their turn outside of the solid matter or take @Damage[5d6[bludgeoning]] damage. A garaggakal can't phase through force effects.

Garaggakal.publicNotes:
Most garaggakals are around 8 feet tall, weighing 400 pounds, with a 20-foot wingspan. Always on the move, they might find a temporary lair on a broken starship or floating chunk of terrain but are soon off again, in search of a new creature to be their next meal. While one rarely encounters garaggakals in the Universe, it can happen, particularly if their temporary lair exits in the Drift.

The Drift is a strange place, and the creatures native to it are stranger still. Garaggakals prove no exception. Shaped vaguely like humanoids, they have arms and legs but also sallow, rubbery skin with long, scaly tails and gauzelike wings. Most horrifyingly, their faces are almost entirely taken up by their enormous, funnel-like mouth, lined with sharp teeth. While they use these mouths for the obvious purpose of biting, they can also use its powerful suction to draw actual life force from their prey.

Also known as "Drift wraiths," garaggakals hunt down any living creatures that linger too long in the Drift. For garaggakals, anything that isn't a fellow garaggakal is prey. However, they're no mere mindless hunters. Garaggakals are surprisingly intelligent and prefer to dissect victims, learning all about them before eating them. Often, garaggakals take their victims apart while they're still alive. Xenobiologists have determined that garaggakals don't even technically need to eat to survive, so their relentless hunger is seemingly psychological. They do require the energy of the Drift to survive in the absence of food, however, and further need the energy of the Drift to reproduce, seeking out areas of intense radiation to absorb and then build a new offspring from their own cells. Their children are fully grown at creation.

Garaggakals surprise their prey. They are quite stealthy and can even phase through solid matter, suddenly appearing on the inside of a starship—bad news for whatever living creatures happen to be inside. Con artists sell garaggakal repelling devices for travelers to use while in the Drift to prevent such visits.

Biotech Superior War Machine.name: Biotech Superior War Machine

Interstellar Comms.name: Interstellar Comms
Interstellar Comms.description: A remote war machine maintains a constant data link with its pilot, using its pilot's languages and skill modifiers for any unlisted skill. In addition, the pilot can speak through the data link using the remote war machine as a mouthpiece.

Corpse Flower.name: Corpse Flower
Corpse Flower.description:
Trigger The war machine would be reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The war machine self-destructs, dealing @Damage[12d6[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save) to all creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:60]{60-foot radius}. For 1 minute after this ability is used, any creature that touches the war machine's remains is exposed to incredible radiation sickness.

Thermal Shadow.name: Thermal Shadow
Thermal Shadow.description: 10 feet. The war machine collects and reflects thermal energy to mask its presence. Any creatures targeting the war machine from outside the aura treats it as if it were @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} unless they possess scent or a non-visual–based sense.

Tentacle Flurry.name: Tentacle Flurry
Tentacle Flurry.description: The war machine makes a tentacle Strike against up to four creatures in reach, rolling the attack and damage roll only once for all targets.

Thoughtlance.name: Thoughtlance
Thoughtlance.description: A creature touched by one of the war machine's tentacles must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30|options:inflicts:slowed] save, becoming @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} on a failure (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} on a critical failure). Each time an affected creature ends its turn, its slowed valued decreases by 1.

Biotech Superior War Machine.publicNotes:
Devised by engineers based on Kalo-Mahoi, the robotic war machine, code name "atlapak," was built for aquatic warfare. Just like its amphibious creators, the atlapak is equally proficient on land. Code name "ksarik" was first observed in a terrifying attack on a Diasporan mining outpost operated by Ulrikka Clanholdings. When unfurled for battle, the ksarik becomes a mass of spiked tentacles and glaring red sensors that can tear through platoons of troopers in minutes.

The incursion began with a series of probes detected by anacites on Aballon. Strange machines with blazing sensors and articulated tentacles appeared out of nowhere, ripping through infrastructure seemingly at random and collecting intelligence all around Pact Worlds space. Panic spread fast, but since the machines always vanished without a trace, or exploded when captured, the sightings couldn't be confirmed or identified. Rumors began to circulate that the machines were a form of biotech never encountered before. Soon the Stewards became formally involved, shuttering the incidents behind the adamantine partition of top security clearance.

In Sarenith of 325 ag, after dozens of military operations failed to engage the elusive probes, a wing of fighters dispatched from the Idari finally got lucky and disabled one of the machines before it could self-destruct. Coaxed into giving up some of its secrets by top Pact Worlds scientists, the biotech vessel became the prototype for the "home grown" remote war machines that are currently being tested for use in combat. The replicas aren't alive but are built using robotic construction templates since the core of the original biotech design has proven too complicated to reverse engineer with Pact technology.

Remote war machines are proxies that can be controlled from a great distance. The original biotech war machines connect to the pilot's mind through a psychic link or unknown radio frequency. Robotic remote war machines connect to a computer via data link, allowing the pilot to control their proxy using a keyboard, virtual reality rig, or even a vidgame controller.

Scavenger Slime.name: Scavenger Slime

Techsense.name: Techsense
Techsense.description: An assembly ooze can detect creatures and objects with the tech trait as a precise sense within the listed range.

Scavenger Shell.name: Scavenger Shell
Scavenger Shell.description: A scavenger slime builds a shell for itself from discarded pieces of technology and a secretion of natural resin. This grants the ooze Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage it takes by an amount equal to its Hardness but temporarily removes its immunity to critical hits. Once the scavenger slime is reduced to half its Hit Points or immediately after being damaged by a critical hit, the scavenger shell breaks, removing its Hardness and reducing its Armor Class to 22.

Kitbash.name: Kitbash
Kitbash.description: Scavenger slimes can repair any item with the tech trait without tools.

Salvaged Armaments.name: Salvaged Armaments
Salvaged Armaments.description: A scavenger slime can incorporate up to three weapons into its protoplasmic body, gaining proficiency with each. These weapons are treated as integrated weapons and are powered by the scavenger slime's own technomagical nature without needing to reload.

Scavenger Slime.publicNotes:
For longer than anyone can remember, scavenger slimes have been found all over the galaxy. They hitch rides on starships, drift among wrecks and meteors, and lay hidden in junkyards. Their only goal: to repair everything they can get their protoplasm on. Scavenger slimes are believed to be an accidental marriage of technology and magic, built by an extremely advanced civilization as an all-purpose repair gel, only to develop rudimentary sentience and outlive their creators.

These creatures have an instinctive drive to repair and rebuild nearly any technological item they encounter. Using their extremely malleable bodies, which excrete a highly specialized resin, scavenger slimes can repair even exceedingly complex pieces of technology. They intuitively understand how to shield themselves from harm and how to operate any weapon they manage to find, aggressively using such armaments to drive any and all creatures away from their never-ending repair work.

Oozes of many types lurk in dark and abandoned places across the galaxy, emerging only to satiate their appetites and threaten adventurers, explorers, and hapless workers alike.

Thasteron Khefak.name: Thasteron Khefak

Gnaw Scrap.name: Gnaw Scrap
Gnaw Scrap.description: A khefak chews on an unattended object to feed, dealing @Damage[4[piercing]] damage every 10 minutes to the object. This damage ignores 10 points of Hardness. If a khefak doesn't spend at least 1 hour Gnawing Scrap per day, it begins to starve until it spends 10 minutes Gnawing Scrap.

Thasteron Radiation.name: Thasteron Radiation
Thasteron Radiation.description: 10 feet. A thasteron khefak gives off an aura of mild radiation. A creature who starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:20] save or take @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage and become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Thasteron Khefak.publicNotes:
When khefaks consume thasteron, they take on a crystalline appearance and grow into territorial, ravenous threats.

Khefak Biogenetics bioengineered khefaks to consume scrap thasteron so the waste could be refined into starship fuel. These creatures eventually became a prime example of a solution becoming a problem of its own. After the discovery of Drift travel, Akiton's thasteron market plummeted, removing the need for mining infrastructure, including khefaks. Khefak Biogenetics destroyed the production facilities, thinking they'd completely eradicated their creations. Khefaks are hardy, however, and the few survivors left the facilities. Beyond thasteron, they subsisted on inorganic materials, and their rapid reproduction rate allowed them to quickly spread all over Akiton. Some stowed away on starships, allowing them to spread throughout the Pact Worlds.

Sapient Purple Orb.name: Sapient Purple Orb

Telekinetic Maneuver.name: 念动战技 Telekinetic Maneuver
Telekinetic Maneuver.description: 你通过一股猛烈的念动力移动一个敌人或者其携带的事物。你可以对目标尝试@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{卸武 Disarm}、@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.7blmbDrQFNfdT731]{推撞 Shove}、@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.lOE4yjUnETTdaf2T]{移位 Reposition}或@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.ge56Lu1xXVFYUnLP]{摔绊 Trip}，使用法术攻击骰，而不是运动检定。

Translate.name: 通译语言 Translate
Translate.description:
在你施放该法术时，目标会理解其正听到或者阅读的一种语言的含义。这不会令其能够理解密文，使用隐喻表达的含义，或者类似的内容（由GM所判定）。如果目标能够听见数种语言并且了解该情况，它能选择要理解的那种语言；否则，随机选择这种语言。

升阶（3环级） 该目标也能使用该语言交流。

升阶（4环级） 你能以至多10个生物作为目标，并且这些目标也能使用该语言交流。

Daze.name: 晕眩术 Daze
Daze.description:
你侵入目标的精神，利用精神冲击令目标晕眩。该冲击造成1d6心灵伤害，具有基础意志豁免。如果目标在豁免中大失败，它获得@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{震慑 Stunned 1}。

升阶（+2） 该伤害增加1d6。

Figment.name: 虚构幻术 Figment
Figment.description:
你创造出简单的声音或图像的幻术。声音会为法术添加听觉特征，且不能包含能被理解的单词或复杂的音乐。图像会为法术添加视觉特征，但是它不能大于5尺边长的立方体，且在15尺的距离内观察会发现它的构造明显很粗糙且缺乏细节。当你施放或维持该法术时，你能尝试用幻术让人@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.GkmbTGfg8KcgynOA]{分神 Create a Diversion}，这会为你的欺骗检定提供+2环境加值。若对某个生物进行的此尝试失败了，该生物就不会相信虚构幻术。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.2Hg3a06gZCSnUgSA]{法术效果：虚构幻术}

Mind Skewer.name: Mind Skewer
Mind Skewer.description:
You overload a creature's mind with a glut of psychic information or divine judgments. The mental onslaught deals 2d6 mental damage. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

Seek the Stars.name: Seek the Stars
Seek the Stars.description:
In your mind's eye, you chart a path through the cosmos. If you're in Pact Worlds space, you immediately know which direction to travel in order to reach the Starstone on Absalom Station. If you're outside the Pact System, you know the direction to the nearest Drift beacon or the Starstone.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.

Soothe.name: 安抚术 Soothe
Soothe.description:
你安抚目标的精神，增强其精神防御并治愈其伤口。目标在你施放该法术时，回复1d10+4生命值，并且在持续时间中，目标对抗心灵效果的豁免上获得+2状态加值。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.nkk4O5fyzrC0057i]{法术效果：安抚术}

升阶（+1） 该治疗量增加1d10+4。

Telekinetic Hand.name: 念动之手 Telekinetic Hand
Telekinetic Hand.description:
你创造出一只漂浮着的魔法手掌，它可以隐形或者宛如幽灵，它能抓住目标物体并向着任意方向缓慢漂浮至多20尺。当你维持法术时，你能够将物体移动额外20尺。若物体位于空中之时法术结束，那么该物体便会坠落。

升阶（3环级） 你能以一个1负载或更轻的无主物体为目标。

升阶（5环级） 法术射程增加至60尺，而且你能以一个1负载或更轻的无主物体为目标。

升阶（7环级） 法术射程增加至60尺，而且你能以一个2负载或更轻的无主物体为目标。

Telekinetic Projectile.name: 念动射弹 Telekinetic Projectile
Telekinetic Projectile.description:
你向目标射出区域中一个未固定的，无主的，1负载1以下的物体。对目标尝试一次法术攻击骰。如果你命中，你造成2d6的钝击，穿刺，或者挥砍伤害（对应于你所扔出的物体）。射出物体的特殊特征或者魔法特性不会影响该攻击或者伤害。

大成功 你造成双倍伤害。

成功 你造成完整伤害。

升阶（+1） 该伤害增加1d6。

Mistvision.name: Mistvision
Mistvision.description: A sapient purple orb always ignores @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealment} caused by fog, smoke, and mist.

Astral Mist.name: Astral Mist
Astral Mist.description:
Frequency once per minute

Effect The sapient purple orb emits a red mist (as the spell) centered on themself. Sapient orbs can choose to emit transparent mist, granting no concealment. Allies and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} creatures in the mist gain a +2 status bonus to saving throws. Additionally, any enemy that's in the mist when it appears, that enters the mist, or that ends its turn in the mist must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save unless it's fascinated.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature becomes fascinated by the mist.

Critical Failure The creature becomes fascinated by the mist and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Excandescent Flash.name: Excandescent Flash
Excandescent Flash.description:
Frequency once per minute

Effect The sapient purple orb momentarily increases their luminance to an aggressively bright spectrum. Each other creature within 20 feet that can see the sapient purple orb must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:21] saving throw or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}. On a critical failure, the target instead becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round, then dazzled for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

Sapient Purple Orb.publicNotes:
Purple orbs are curious, social beings who unfailingly attempt to make contact with any life-form they encounter, though these attempts are frequently inept. Few other creatures understand their ancient language, so purple orbs instead use magic, telepathy, minor illusions, and flashes of light and flowing mist from their 3-foot-diameter luminous bodies.

Purple orbs are eager for cooperation and solidarity with their chosen friends and family, whether those are other sapient orbs or completely different beings, making it relatively easy to befriend them—or deceive them. Purple orbs are devout supporters and defenders of their companions, flinging themselves bodily into the fray if a friend is in danger. They become a deep dark purple as they grow more emotional in the heat of conflict (violent or otherwise), though their emotions rarely grow so intense that they're willing to take another being's life. That purple orbs seem to value their own lives less than those of their friends might be among the reasons that the number of sapient purple orbs in the universe seems to be dwindling.

These enigmatic, orb-shaped beings seem to have no home world, organized society, traditions, or religion. Sightings of the orbs are sporadic—they briefly appear in large numbers, then vanish for a year or more, only to reappear in an entirely different region of space for a few months. Scholars and friends of the orbs have noticed that each time the orbs appear, their numbers have diminished.

Sapient orbs occasionally form permanent bonds, merging three to six orbs (always of the same type, as far as anyone knows) into a cluster called a luminant. Only sapient orbs at advanced stages of their life cycle can form such clusters, and the merge is permanent and irrevocable. Luminants are highly intelligent but far more reclusive than other sapient orbs, making it difficult to discern their motivations or purpose in sapient orb society.

Plasma Ooze.name: Plasma Ooze

Thoughtsense.name: 思维感知 Thoughtsense
Thoughtsense.description: The plasma ooze can sense all non-mindless creatures within the listed range.

Electromagnetic Field.name: Electromagnetic Field
Electromagnetic Field.description:
100 feet. When an item with the tech trait is activated inside the aura, it must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:36] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2} on a critical failure).

A tech creature that starts its turn within the aura must attempt a @Check[will|dc:35] save. If it fails, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2} on a critical failure).

An item or creature that succeeds at this save becomes immune to this aura for 1 hour.

Magnetic Draw.name: Magnetic Draw
Magnetic Draw.description: Large and smaller creatures and all unattended objects within 100 feet must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:36] save or be pulled 15 feet toward the plasma ooze (30 feet on a critical failure).

Plasma Drain.name: Plasma Drain
Plasma Drain.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect each creature and wielded object with the tech trait within 100 feet of the plasma ooze must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:36] save. For each target that fails this save, the ooze regains @Damage[15[healing]] Hit Points and the object loses half of its remaining charges (if battery-powered).

Plasma Slag.name: Plasma Slag
Plasma Slag.description: A creature damaged by the ooze's pseudopod must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:36] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} on a critical failure). A creature with the tech trait uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its roll.

Plasma Ooze.publicNotes:
Plasma oozes pose a significant menace to seasoned spacefarers within the Pact Worlds. These entities materialize as expansive networks of crackling electromagnetic energy, assuming a captivating spherical form pulsating with luminous hues of purple and blue, with searing white-hot bursts scattered throughout.

These oozes primarily lurk near the surface of stars but are also drawn to potent electromagnetic phenomena elsewhere in the Universe. The electromagnetic fields exuded by the ooze present dangers in space, potentially even incapacitating shielded starships. Armed scions connected to the Plane of Fire, known as the Sunkillers, claim to actively hunt these enigmatic beings, armed with specialized gear and sonic weaponry.

Oozes of many types lurk in dark and abandoned places across the galaxy, emerging only to satiate their appetites and threaten adventurers, explorers, and hapless workers alike.

Noquasar Spark.name: Noquasar Spark

Gravity Lurch.name: Gravity Lurch
Gravity Lurch.description: A creature critically hit by a noquasar spark's graviton flare is knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Ionized Plasma Blast.name: Ionized Plasma Blast
Ionized Plasma Blast.description: The noquasar spark unleashes plasma in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] or @Template[type:line|distance:30]. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[2d4[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:16|basic] save). A creature that critically fails its save also takes @Damage[1d4[persistent,electricity]|options:area-damage] damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. The noquasar spark can't use Ionized Plasma Blast again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Ionized Plasma Blast]]{1d4 rounds}.

Noquasar Spark.publicNotes:
A noquasar spark appears as a glimmer of intensely bright light with an agonized face.

When a solarian perishes while out of sync with the cycle of stars, their soul might become entangled with their solar mote. Extreme cosmic forces act on the soul as it attempts to reach the River of Souls, stretching it infinitely thin for all of eternity.

Hardlight Havoc.name: Hardlight Havoc

Error State.name: Error State
Error State.description:
Before the hardlight havoc begins its turn in combat, it gains one of the following error states at random, losing any previous error state.

Change Character The hardlight havoc regains @Damage[2d8[healing]|options:area-damage] Hit Points.

Pixelation The hardlight havoc is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} from all creatures.

Power Leak The hardlight havoc is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}. At the end of its turn, it deals @Damage[4d6[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage to creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save).

Surge The hardlight havoc is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} until the end of its turn and can use the extra action to Stride or Strike.

Tethered 120 feet.name: Tethered 120 feet
Tethered 120 feet.description: A hardlight life-form can't travel more than the indicated number of feet from its projector. A hardlight life-form reduced to 0 HP vanishes, though its projector reconstructs it in [[/gmr 1d4 #hours]]{1d4 hours}. The only way to permanently destroy a hardlight life-form is to find its projector and either repair or destroy it.

Flicker.name: 闪烁术 Flicker
Flicker.description: The hardlight havoc teleports up to 40 feet. If the destination is within melee reach of an enemy, it can make a melee Strike against that enemy. If it teleports to a square without a surface to stand on, it falls after attempting a Strike and takes no damage from falling.

Hardlight Havoc.publicNotes:
This hardlight life-form is unstable, displaying stuck frames and erratic movements.

Unlike normal holograms, hardlight life-forms are tangible, living entities.

Whiskered Renkroda.name: Whiskered Renkroda

Motion Sense.name: Motion Sense
Motion Sense.description: A whiskered renkroda can sense nearby creatures through vibration and air or water movement as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

Circling Reposition.name: Circling Reposition
Circling Reposition.description:
Trigger A creature ends its movement within the whiskered renkroda's reach

Effect The whiskered renkroda circles its prey, Striding up to its Speed. The renkroda must move to a location where the triggering creature is within its reach.

Forest Passage.name: 林间穿行 Forest Passage
Forest Passage.description: The whiskered renkroda ignores any difficult terrain caused by plants, such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth.

Terrifying Roar.name: Terrifying Roar
Terrifying Roar.description:
The whiskered renkroda bellows ferociously. Each non-renkroda creature within 60 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save. The creature is then temporarily immune for 10 minutes.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3}.

Upending Charge.name: Upending Charge
Upending Charge.description: The whiskered renkroda charges forward and slams into its prey. It Strides twice. If the whiskered renkroda ends its movement with an enemy within its reach, it makes a body Strike against that enemy. On a hit, the enemy is knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Whiskered Renkroda.publicNotes:
The smallest of all renkrodas, whiskered renkrodas have adapted to move easily through dense jungles and forests and blend in amid the vibrant green flora. Whiskered renkrodas have whisker-like antennae growing from their chins that detect vibrations, enabling them to detect prey hiding amid the dense foliage. When they spot their prey, renkrodas charge into the fray and slam into their prey with enough force to topple trees or vehicles. Occasionally, whiskered renkrodas live and hunt in packs, to better protect themselves from larger predators.

Renkrodas are a family of large dinosaur-like predators native to the planet Castrovel. They've spread beyond the jungle planet's boundaries thanks to smugglers, poachers, and ultra-wealthy big-game hunters. Bipedal and strong with long necks and decorative spines, renkrodas are best known for their terrifying roars, which they bellow frequently as a form of long-range communication and to scare away other predators and scavengers. Over a dozen species of renkrodas exist, each specially adapted to their environment or ecological niche.

Nihili.name: Nihili

Decompressing Glance.name: Decompressing Glance
Decompressing Glance.description:
Trigger A creature within 30 feet that the nihili can see starts its turn

Effect The target must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:20] save. If it fails, it's @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} for 1 minute as it struggles to catch its breath.

Decompressing Gaze.name: Decompressing Gaze
Decompressing Gaze.description:
The nihili stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet and imparts vivid visions of their prior death by decompression. A creature affected by this gaze must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save. If it fails and hasn't already been @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} by Decompressing Glance or this ability, it becomes fatigued. If the creature is already fatigued, a failed save causes the creature to begin suffocating until the end of the nihili's next turn. The creature remains conscious and can take actions on its turn, such as casting a spell or searching for a source of air. The creature can hold its breath as normal.

A creature can attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save at the end of its turn to break free from the experience being projected into its mind; on a success, the effect of Decompressing Gaze ends. The nihili can Sustain Decompressing Gaze on their turn or end this effect as a free action. The targeted creature's access to air is restored when the nihili targets a new creature with their Decompressing Gaze or the creature successfully saves against the effects. This otherwise follows the rules for suffocation.

Gravity Grab.name: Gravity Grab
Gravity Grab.description:
The nihili generates a field of gravity that pulls all creatures and unsecured objects toward it. Each creature within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is pulled 5 feet toward the nihili.

Failure The creature is pulled 15 feet toward the nihili.

Critical Failure The creature is pulled 30 feet toward the nihili.

Nihili.publicNotes:
Dying from exposure in space is horrific, and many starship crews obsessively monitor their hull and armor integrity to avoid such a fate. It can also lead to the creation of the foul undead known as nihilis. A nihili arises after an individual in vacuum dies of asphyxiation while all gases exit their body and their lungs collapse from attempts to hold their breath. Legends say the first person to die in such a terrible manner had a vision of the goddess of undeath, Urgathoa, who bestowed upon them a single kiss, transforming them into the very first nihili. The same legends go on to claim that this nihili still floats aimlessly through space, waiting to cling to an unsuspecting starship.

The physical appearance of a given nihili varies. Most appear as zombie-like versions of their respective ancestries, but with gruesome stretch marks and pockets of bloat caused by escaping gases during their death. A nihili maintains their prior intellect, subverted entirely to inflicting the same suffering on the living that they endured. By focusing their gaze on a target, a nihili can impart to that creature the memories of their own demise, potentially suffocating them.

Electrovore.name: Electrovore

Electricsense.name: Electricsense
Electricsense.description: An electrovore can sense electricity, including the electrical impulses found in most living creatures' nervous systems, as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

Electrical Discharge.name: Electrical Discharge
Electrical Discharge.description:
Effect The electrovore unleashes a shock wave that deals @Damage[2d6[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage to creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] (@Check[reflex|dc:15]). It can't use Electrical Discharge again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Electrical Discharge]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage. Items in its possession with the tech trait becomes glitching 1. If the target has the tech trait, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}.

Failure As success, but full damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}.

Critical Failure As success, but double damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 3}.

Siphon.name: Siphon
Siphon.description: When an electrovore scores a critical hit with a tail Strike against a living creature wielding or wearing tech gear or a creature with the tech trait, it siphons off a portion of the target's electrical energy. This restores @Damage[5[healing]] Hit Points to the electrovore immediately, plus 1 Hit Point per minute for the next 10 minutes; the additional healing doesn't increase if the electrovore siphons multiple times.

Electrovore.publicNotes:
Electrovores consume electricity as their sole source of sustenance. On their native planet, they fed on a species of three-headed electric eels, but volcanic activity wiped out their food source, sending electrovores to the brink of extinction. Electrovores found their salvation when explorers landed on their planet. The explorer's equipment and vessels had ample amounts of the electricity that the electrovores require. They stowed away on the vessels and soon spread throughout the Pact Worlds, causing damage and mayhem wherever they appeared.

Nothing terrifies a team of engineers more than finding an electrovore chewing on wires in a remote part of their starship. Where there's one electrovore, a dozen more likely await discovery. These 3-foot-tall reptilian creatures, sometimes called "conduit rats," breed rapidly and create such a disturbance in power sources that, over the course of days or weeks, a starship or factory might completely shut down from lack of power. There are many lifeless star vessels adrift in space because an electrovore infestation wasn't stopped in time.

Removing an infestation is no easy task, as electrovores are single-minded and cunning. The creatures are adept at finding places to hide, stalking their preferred energy sources and finding the correct moment to strike. Packs of electrovores even coordinate feedings in different areas at the same time to make it harder to stop them.

When confronted, electrovores rarely fight to the death, preferring to eat some electricity and retreat, hide, and heal, then return for another feast.

Snoop-Class Infiltrator Robot.name: Snoop-Class Infiltrator Robot

Integrated Commercial Crossbolter.name: Integrated Commercial Crossbolter
Integrated Commercial Crossbolter.description:
Upgrades: 1

This high-tech crossbow uses a series of pistons to propel bolts along its barrel. These quiet-firing semiautomatic weapons are popular in high-density settlements where gunfire might cause a panic.

Integrated Hacking Toolkit (Commercial).name: Integrated Hacking Toolkit (Commercial)
Integrated Hacking Toolkit (Commercial).description: A hacking toolkit is required for Computers checks to Hack computers. You can use a hacking toolkit to access a computer without using a user interface, but this requires physical contact with the computer or contact via an infosphere or a similar linked network.

Integrated Infiltrator's Toolkit (Commer.name: Integrated Infiltrator's Toolkit (Commercial)
Integrated Infiltrator's Toolkit (Commer.description: You need an infiltrator's toolkit to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.2EE4aF4SZpYf0R6H]{Pick Locks} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.cYdz2grcOcRt4jk6]{Disable Devices} (of some types) using the Thievery skill.

Reactive Camouflage.name: Reactive Camouflage
Reactive Camouflage.description: The robot comes packaged with photosensitive reflectors on its exterior panels. It can [[/act hide]] even without cover or concealment.

Sleeping Gas Bomb.name: Sleeping Gas Bomb
Sleeping Gas Bomb.description:
The infiltrator robot fires a bomb within 30 feet that disperses sleeping gas in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10]. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes a –1 status penalty to Perception checks for 1 round.

Failure The creature falls @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}. If it's still unconscious after 1 minute, it wakes up automatically.

Critical Failure The creature falls unconscious. If it's still unconscious after 1 hour, it wakes up automatically.

Snoop-Class Infiltrator Robot.publicNotes:
Not a front-line combatant, an infiltrator robot's primary tactic is to quietly enter a location, use its sleeping gas bombs to disable those guarding its target, and collect its payload. The leadership of the Pact Worlds found that the snoop-class infiltrator robot could have other, darker tasks during the Silent War: its reactive camouflage could help it transport deadly explosives to sabotage ships, shipyards, and other military installations.

Brought to the forefront during the war against the Veskarium, and further perfected by various corporate interests within the Pact Worlds, infiltrator robots are known for being able to get into places where no one else could. Generally small and agile, infiltrator robots sneak into enemy territory and either download precious data or record incriminating evidence.

Akata.name: Akata

Hibernation.name: Hibernation
Hibernation.description: An akata can secrete resin that encases it safely in a noqual cocoon. The cocoon has Hardness 9, 40 HP, and a BT of 18. It has resistance 5 to damage from magical sources. While ensconced within this cocoon, the akata can't be harmed, doesn't need to eat or drink, and gains @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.sebk9XseMCRkDqRg]{Lifesense} 30 feet. During its hibernation, the akata remains dormant until exposed to extreme heat or it senses a living creature. It can then break free of its cocoon within [[/gmr 1d4 #minutes]]{1d4 minutes}.

No Hearing.name: No Hearing
No Hearing.description: Akatas are immune to auditory effects, automatically critically fail Perception checks that require it to hear, and take a –2 status penalty to Perception checks (but not initiative rolls) that involve sound but also rely on its other senses.

Salt Water Vulnerability.name: Salt Water Vulnerability
Salt Water Vulnerability.description: Salt water attacks an akata's cells and acts as a potent acid. If the akata is fully immersed in salt water, it takes @Damage[4d6[acid]] damage from the water immediately and at the end of each of its turns as long as it remains immersed. Its weakness to salt water applies to this damage.

Void Death.name: Void Death
Void Death.description:
An akata implants microscopic larvae into any creature it bites, but only Small or Medium humanoids make suitable hosts; all other creatures are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} at Stage 2 and further instead.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:17]

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effect 1 (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (1 day)

Stage 3 as stage 2 (1 day)

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} (1 day)

Stage 5 as stage 4 (1 day)

Stage 6 dead and corpse rises as a void zombie in [[/gmr 2d4 #hours]]{2d4 hours}

Akata.publicNotes:
Few creatures inspire more dread in isolated explorers and lonely space stations than the breathless, leonine akatas, which infest creatures with parasites to transform them into ravenous void zombies.

An akata's toothy maw, glowing yellow eyes, and lashing twin tails mark it as a silent predator. Their slinking, hairless blue flesh spreads out into a mane of rubbery tentacles. Despite lacking ears, these quadrupeds stalk prey with an enhanced sight and sense of smell. Akatas typically stand at about 3-1/2 feet tall and weigh 400 pounds.

In times of scarcity, akatas ooze noqual from their pores and spin pale green crystalline cocoons capable of holding them in stasis for centuries. They can even use their cocoons to traverse space and find new hosts for their larvae. When a host dies from this infestation, known as void death, the strongest offspring animates the cadaver as a void zombie. Fiercely territorial, they hunt in colonies that are capable of bringing a settlement to its knees within weeks.

Diaspora Worm Sage.name: Diaspora Worm Sage

Electrocommunication.name: Electrocommunication
Electrocommunication.description: Diaspora worms communicate by shaping and reading electromagnetic fields. This functions like telepathy with creatures that share this ability.

Electromagnetic Sight.name: Electromagnetic Sight
Electromagnetic Sight.description: Diaspora worms sense their surroundings by reading electromagnetic fields, including disruptions caused by creatures or objects. This is a precise sense within the listed range. Intense fields, such as areas emitting radiation or auras of electricity, make creatures within them @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} to the diaspora worm.

Electromagnetic Blindness.name: Electromagnetic Blindness
Electromagnetic Blindness.description:
Diaspora worms that take electricity damage must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save.

Critical Success The worm is unaffected.

Success The worm is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Failure The worm is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The worm is blinded for 1 round, then dazzled for 1 round.

Darkwater Veil.name: Darkwater Veil
Darkwater Veil.description:
Requirements The diaspora worm is in water

Effect The worm alters the structure of water within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20], forming light-absorbing nanotubes. This field moves with the worm and lasts for 1 minute. The emanation deactivates non-magical light sources and automatically attempts to counteract magical light (counteract rank 9, counteract modifier [[/r 1d20+38 #Counteract]]{+38}). This field also provides cover for creatures inside of it from those outside, and vice versa.

Darkwater Vortex.name: Darkwater Vortex
Darkwater Vortex.description: The diaspora worm sage stirs up a 40-foot-tall cylinder of water with a 20-foot radius within 300 feet. If the area isn't already filled with water, this ability fails. The vortex buffets all creatures, dealing @Damage[5d10[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic] save). Creatures that fail the save are pulled 5 feet toward the center of the vortex (or 10 feet on a critical failure).

Electromagnetic Surge.name: Electromagnetic Surge
Electromagnetic Surge.description:
The diaspora worm sage emits a concentrated wave of electromagnetism in a @Template[type:cone|distance:50]. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:35] save. A creature with the tech trait uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its roll.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and becomes immune to Electromagnetic Surge for 1 hour.

Success The creature's items with the tech trait become glitching 1 for 1 round.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}, and its items with the tech trait become glitching 2 for 1 minute. A creature with the tech trait also gains this glitching condition.

Critical Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} for 1 round, and its items with the tech trait become glitching 3 for 10 minutes. A creature with the tech trait also gains this glitching condition.

Tongue Grab.name: Tongue Grab
Tongue Grab.description: A creature hit by the diaspora worm's tongue becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}. A creature can sever the tongue with a Strike against AC 37 that deals at least @Damage[32[slashing]] damage. This prevents the worm from using its tongue Strike until it regrows its tongue, which takes 1 day.

Tongue Reel.name: Tongue Reel
Tongue Reel.description:
Requirements The diaspora worm has @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} a creature with its tongue

Effect The diaspora worm attempts to [[/act reposition]] the grabbed creature toward the worm. If the grabbed creature is brought to within 10 feet or less of the worm, the worm can attempt to Swallow Whole the creature.

Diaspora Worm Sage.publicNotes:
Diaspora worms that survive for centuries grow significantly in size, power, and intelligence. They mostly protect their ancestral home, preserving bioelectric coral records and directing the actions of younger worms

"Space worm" is a nonscientific term for a xenobiologically complex clade of invertebrate animals and beasts that make their home in the vastness of space. Some, like the diaspora and dust worms, show signs of intelligence and are directly related to each other. Others, like the surnoch, evolved separately, with their origins completely unknown to science.

Biotech Ultimate War Machine.name: Biotech Ultimate War Machine

Constant Pulse Radar.name: Constant Pulse Radar
Constant Pulse Radar.description: The war machine can detect all creatures and objects within 100 feet of itself, treating hidden, invisible, and unobserved creatures as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}.

Interstellar Comms.name: Interstellar Comms
Interstellar Comms.description: A remote war machine maintains a constant data link with its pilot, using its pilot's languages and skill modifiers for any unlisted skill. In addition, the pilot can speak through the data link using the remote war machine as a mouthpiece.

Corpse Flower.name: Corpse Flower
Corpse Flower.description:
Trigger The war machine would be reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The war machine self-destructs, dealing @Damage[16d6[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:36|basic|options:area-effect] save) to all creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:60]{60-foot radius}. For 1 minute after this ability is used, any creature that touches the war machine's remains is exposed to incredible radiation sickness.

Psionic Flare.name: Psionic Flare
Psionic Flare.description:
Trigger A creature within 60 feet of the war machine Casts a Spell

Effect The war machine fires a focused lightning beam targeting only the triggering creature. If the creature fails its save, the spell becomes weakened. If the weakened spell requires a saving throw, the target uses the outcome one degree of success better than the result of its roll. If the weakened spell requires a spell attack, that attack roll takes a –4 circumstance penalty.

Radio Silence.name: Radio Silence
Radio Silence.description: 60 feet. Non-allied comm units and other computers don't function within the remote war machine's aura unless their operator succeeds at a @Check[computers|against:stealth|rollerRole:target] check against the war machine's Stealth DC as an Interact action.

Swirling Shield.name: Swirling Shield
Swirling Shield.description:
1 to 3

The remote war machine's tentacles surround it in defensive patterns. For 1 minute, it gains a number of reactions equal to the number of actions spent on Swirling Shield. These extra reactions can be used only for Psionic Flare or Reactive Strike.

Tentacle Flurry.name: Tentacle Flurry
Tentacle Flurry.description: The war machine makes a tentacle Strike against up to four creatures in reach, rolling the attack and damage roll only once for all targets.

Thermal Camouflage.name: Thermal Camouflage
Thermal Camouflage.description: The war machine becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} for 1 minute or until it attempts to make a Strike.

Biotech Ultimate War Machine.publicNotes:
Only one member of the Stewards has ever encountered the biotechnical horror known as code name "moonflower" and lived to tell the tale—and only after they emerged from a months-long coma. According to her story, a single moonflower war machine was responsible for the massacre of Liavaran moon S-117, which used to house an entire Stewards deep space telescope array and planetary defense battery.

The incursion began with a series of probes detected by anacites on Aballon. Strange machines with blazing sensors and articulated tentacles appeared out of nowhere, ripping through infrastructure seemingly at random and collecting intelligence all around Pact Worlds space. Panic spread fast, but since the machines always vanished without a trace, or exploded when captured, the sightings couldn't be confirmed or identified. Rumors began to circulate that the machines were a form of biotech never encountered before. Soon the Stewards became formally involved, shuttering the incidents behind the adamantine partition of top security clearance.

In Sarenith of 325 ag, after dozens of military operations failed to engage the elusive probes, a wing of fighters dispatched from the Idari finally got lucky and disabled one of the machines before it could self-destruct. Coaxed into giving up some of its secrets by top Pact Worlds scientists, the biotech vessel became the prototype for the "home grown" remote war machines that are currently being tested for use in combat. The replicas aren't alive but are built using robotic construction templates since the core of the original biotech design has proven too complicated to reverse engineer with Pact technology.

Remote war machines are proxies that can be controlled from a great distance. The original biotech war machines connect to the pilot's mind through a psychic link or unknown radio frequency. Robotic remote war machines connect to a computer via data link, allowing the pilot to control their proxy using a keyboard, virtual reality rig, or even a vidgame controller.

Skreesire.name: Skreesire

Camouflage.name: Camouflage
Camouflage.description:
Requirements The skreesire is in rocky terrain

Effect Until the next time they act, the skreesire resembles rocky terrain. They have an automatic result of 29 on @Check[deception] checks and DCs to pass as rocky terrain.

Enthrall.name: 注目术 Enthrall
Enthrall.description:
Effect A target within 30 feet hears a telepathic manifestation of the skreesire's will and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save.

Failure The creature must use the first action of its next turn to Stride toward the skreesire.

Critical Failure The creature must use all its actions on its next turn to Stride toward the skreesire; when it's adjacent to the skreesire, it drops @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} and ends its turn.

Skreesire.publicNotes:
Skreesires are reptilian monstrosities with a formidable will, razor-sharp teeth, and nonfunctional wings atop a mass of ambulatory tentacles.

Skreesires, and their offspring skreelings, are vicious predators who skulk and prowl in the crags and caverns of the cosmos.
